member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelBarrierWeight
member.summary: The weight of features when considered as barriers to labeling.

remarks.para: An or a , which has no of its own, can be added to a map's operational layers. Their features will be automatically considered by the labeling process, to block labels being placed overlapping the features. This property controls how much labels must avoid the layer's features.
Note: Other layer types, which have their own , can also have their features treated as barriers, but this is controlled by their and properties.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelBarrierWeight.None
member.summary: The feature will not act as an barrier to labels.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelBarrierWeight.Low
member.summary: The feature will be avoided by labels unless that's the only way they can be placed.
member.remarks: Labels will still overlap this feature if that is the only way they can be placed.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelBarrierWeight.Medium
member.summary: The feature will be avoided by labels unless that's the only way they can be placed after trying some additional positions.
member.remarks: Labels will still overlap this feature if that is the only way they can be placed. This is similar to , but the labeling process can try additional intermediate alternative positions. Not all styles of labeling will have additional positions to try.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelBarrierWeight.High
member.summary: The feature is an exclusion zone for labels.
member.remarks: Labels that overlap the feature will not be placed unless the label is using a deconfliction strategy of or .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelDeconflictionStrategy
member.summary: The strategy for moving labels to avoid overlapping point symbols or higher priority labels.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelDeconflictionStrategy.Automatic
member.summary: The label will use the default deconfliction strategy ().
member.remarks: This value corresponds to no value being specified for a property.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelDeconflictionStrategy.Dynamic
member.summary: Place the label in the preferred location, but move to an alternative location to not overlap higher priority labels or point feature/graphic symbols.
member.remarks: This label is placed only if it can be moved to a location that will not overlap a higher priority label.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelDeconflictionStrategy.None
member.summary: Place the label in the preferred location, regardless of overlaps with other features, graphics or labels.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelDeconflictionStrategy.Static
member.summary: Place the label in the preferred location, unless it would overlap a higher priority label or point feature/graphic symbol.
member.remarks: If it overlaps a lower priority label, then the lower priority label may disappear or move (depending on its ). If this label overlaps a higher priority label, then this label is not placed.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelDeconflictionStrategy.DynamicNeverRemove
member.summary: Place the label in the preferred location, but move to an alternative location to minimize overlapping higher priority labels or point feature/graphic symbols.
member.remarks: This label is always placed, even if it overlaps a higher priority label.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelExpression
member.summary: Abstract base class for the different types of expressions that can be used to create label text.
member.remarks: Users can read the expression script using the property. To specify an expression script, create an object of type , , or .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelExpression.Expression
member.summary: Gets or sets the label expression script string.

remarks.para: The expression is expected to be a complete, self-contained label expression in the language specified by the label expression subclass object.

Warning: If the expression attempts to use attributes which don't exist then no label will be produced. This is distinct from the attribute existing but having a null or empty value, which can form part of the produced text label.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelLineConnection
member.summary: The strategy for whether line features with the same label, and matching end vertices, should be joined before sharing a label.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelLineConnection.Automatic
member.summary: The default approach for connectable features is to connect them ().
member.remarks: This value corresponds to no value being specified for a property.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelLineConnection.MinimizeLabels
member.summary: Line geometries with the same label and coincident end vertices should be considered together as a single, continuous geometry when placing labels.
member.remarks: For example, if a single label is being placed on a line feature, then the single location will be chosen along the combined geometries. This combining will ignore junctions, so it may be ambiguous which line feature after a junction is the continuation of the earlier geometry.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelLineConnection.None
member.summary: Keep one label per line feature geometry.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelLineConnection.UnambiguousLabels
member.summary: Line geometries with the same label and coincident end vertices should be joined, until they hit a junction.
member.remarks: For example, if a single label is being placed on a line geometry (composed of multiple features' geometries, but all having the same label text), then a label will be placed on both sides of any junction, to make it unambiguous which geometries are the continuation of the initial geometry.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelLineOrientation
member.summary: Whether above or below a line geometry will be interpreted as above or below on the "screen", or with respect to the "direction" of line's geometry (that is, the digitization order in which the vertices are listed).

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelLineOrientation.Direction
member.summary: above will mean that the label will be offset perpendicularly left from its line segment. below will mean that the label will be offset perpendicularly right from its line segment.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelLineOrientation.Screen
member.summary: above will mean that the label will be offset perpendicularly from its line segment towards the top of the screen. below will mean that the label will be offset perpendicularly from its line segment towards the bottom of the screen.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelLineOrientation.Unconstrained
member.summary: The label will be offset perpendicularly to whichever side of the line geometry has space (defaulting to "above," in the "screen" sense).

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelMultipartStrategy
member.summary: The strategy for the number of labels to be placed on a multi-part feature.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelMultipartStrategy.Automatic
member.summary: Labeling will use the default distribution of labels across multi-part features.

remarks.para: This depends on Feature type:

list.item: Polygon labels will use .
Point and Line labels will use .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelMultipartStrategy.LabelLargest
member.summary: If a feature consists of multiple parts, only the largest one will be assigned a label.

remarks.para: If a line feature consists of multiple part geometries, or a polygon feature consists of multiple ring geometries, only the largest one will be assigned a label.
If the largest part of the feature is not currently visible on the GeoView, then the label will not be visible. It will not automatically move to the largest visible part.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelMultipartStrategy.LabelPerFeature
member.summary: One label per feature (not supported in this API).

remarks.para: The intended use is to place one label to represent each feature, even if the feature is made up of multiple parts. For example, a single label is placed at the center of a group of point parts, not necessarily at the position of any individual part.
This value can be set when labeling is authored in ArcGIS Pro, but is not supported yet in this API. If used, the placement will be as for .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelMultipartStrategy.LabelPerPart
member.summary: If a feature consists of multiple geometries, each geometry will be assigned a label.
member.remarks: If a line feature consists of multiple line geometries, or a polygon feature consists of multiple ring geometries, each geometry will be assigned a label.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelMultipartStrategy.LabelPerSegment
member.summary: One label per segment (not supported in Runtime yet).

remarks.para: The intended use is to place a label on each segment of each line geometry.
This value can be set when labeling is authored in ArcGIS Pro, but is not supported yet in this API. If used, the placement with be as for .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelOverlapStrategy
member.summary: The strategy for whether another label is allowed to be placed over this feature or label.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelOverlapStrategy.Automatic
member.summary: The other label will use the default overlap behavior which depends on the object being overlapped.
member.remarks: This value corresponds to no value being specified for a overlap properties.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelOverlapStrategy.Allow
member.summary: The other label will be allowed to place itself in its preferred location, ignoring this obstacle.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelOverlapStrategy.Avoid
member.summary: The other label will move to minimize overlapping this obstacle, but will overlap if necessary.
member.remarks: The other label can only move if its allows.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelOverlapStrategy.Exclude
member.summary: The other label will not be placed rather than overlap this obstacle.
member.remarks: The other label can only move if its allows. If it cannot move, or if there is no free position available, then the other label will not be placed.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelOverrunStrategy
member.summary: The strategy for whether a label should be visible at viewing scales where the feature is too small for the label to fit.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelOverrunStrategy.Automatic
member.summary: The label will use the default overrun behavior which depends on the object being overrun.
member.remarks: This value corresponds to no value being specified for the property.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelOverrunStrategy.None
member.summary: The label cannot run past the ends of its line geometry or edges of its polygon geometry.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelOverrunStrategy.Allow
member.summary: The label can run past the ends of its line geometry or edges or its polygon geometry.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelRemoveDuplicatesStrategy
member.summary: The strategy for whether labels should be removed if they have the same text as other labels, to reduce clutter and free up space.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelRemoveDuplicatesStrategy.Automatic
member.summary: The default approach to duplicate labels to is keep them ().
member.remarks: This value corresponds to no value being specified for a property.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelRemoveDuplicatesStrategy.All
member.summary: Remove nearby duplicate labels.
member.remarks: The distance within which to look for duplicates is controlled by .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelRemoveDuplicatesStrategy.FeatureType
member.summary: Remove nearby duplicate labels, if they belong to the same Feature Type.
member.remarks: Two features are considered the same type if they are both point features, both line features, or both polygon features. The distance within which to look for duplicates is controlled by .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelRemoveDuplicatesStrategy.LabelClass
member.summary: Remove nearby duplicate labels, if they below to the same Label Class.
member.remarks: Two labels are considered the same Label Class if they are both created from the same . The distance within which to look for duplicates is controlled by .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelRemoveDuplicatesStrategy.None
member.summary: No duplicates should be removed.
member.remarks: Place all labels on the map, regardless of duplicates.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelRepeatStrategy
member.summary: The strategy for whether a label should have multiple copies created and placed along or across the same feature.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelRepeatStrategy.Automatic
member.summary: Labeling will use the default repetition for the feature type.
member.remarks: Line feature labels will use . Point and Polygon feature labels will use .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelRepeatStrategy.None
member.summary: Only place one label per feature.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelRepeatStrategy.Repeat
member.summary: Repeat the label along or across the feature.
member.remarks: The frequency of repetition is controlled by the property.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackAlignment
member.summary: The strategy for how multi-row (stacked) text should be horizontally aligned.
member.remarks: Multi-row text labels can each be horizontally aligned on the left, right or at their center. By using the , the user can choose to use the of the for all labels, or can choose to have the most aesthetic alignment calculated case-by-case for each label depending on its placement position around its feature. This is particularly useful for labels of dense point features, where labels may move above, below, left or right of their point symbol, in order to fit on the display.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackAlignment.Automatic
member.summary: The default behavior is to use .
member.remarks: This value corresponds to no value being specified for a property.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackAlignment.Dynamic
member.summary: Set the alignment depending upon the label's position with respect to its feature.
member.remarks: Have the alignment decided case-by-case for each label depending on its placement position around its feature. This is particularly useful for labels of dense point features, where labels may move above, below, left, or right of their point symbol, in order to fit on the display. For example, a stack to the right of a point symbol will be left-aligned.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackAlignment.TextSymbol
member.summary: Follow the alignment specified by of the .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackBreakPosition
member.summary: The strategy for whether a row of text should be broken before or after it exceeds the ideal length.
member.remarks: If stacking is turned on, label placement can insert a line break before or after the word that overruns the maximum number of characters per row.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackBreakPosition.Automatic
member.summary: Use the default break position ().

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackBreakPosition.After
member.summary: Insert the line break after a word that has broken the limit.
member.remarks: This means rows will generally be longer than the .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackBreakPosition.Before
member.summary: Insert the line break before a word that will break the limit.
member.remarks: This means rows will generally be shorter than the (although they will overrun for individual words larger than this count).

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackSeparator
member.summary: A separator character in a label, where a line break may be inserted in long text.
member.remarks:
A single separator is defined by:
The separator character that should potentially be replaced by a line break.
Whether the separator should always be used for line break.
Whether the separator should remain visible.
Whether the separator should appear at the end of one row, or the beginning of the next.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackSeparator.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with default values.
member.remarks: The default separator has an empty and will be ignored.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackSeparator.BreakPosition
member.summary: Gets or sets the position where the separator will be written, if used and remaining visible.

remarks.para: Whether the separator code point should appear at the end of one row, or the beginning of the next, if chosen as a line break position. This does not matter if the separator is not to be visible when used.
The default value is which will give the same behavior as .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackSeparator.IsForced
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether a line break must be inserted when the is encountered.
member.remarks: The default value is false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackSeparator.Separator
member.summary: Gets or sets a string intended to describe one code point.

remarks.para: Each specifies a code point to be looked for in the text. A code point is often thought of as a single character in the text, but may need several chars in a string to describe it. For example:

list.item: To use a comma as a separator, the string would be just ",".
To use Hebrew Punctuation Paseq (U+05C0) as a separator, the would need to encode the code point as \u05C0 (UTF-16).
To use higher range Unicode code points such as Aegean Word Separator Dot (U+10101), the can be encoded as either \U00010101 (UTF-32) or \uD800\uDD01 (UTF-16).

remarks.para: The default value is an empty string, meaning that the separator will be ignored.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackSeparator.IsVisible
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the separator should still be visible, if chosen as a line break position.

remarks.para: For example, a user may wish to keep a hyphenation mark at the end of a row of text, but hide a comma.
The default value is false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackSeparator.UnknownJson
member.summary: Gets a collection of unknown data from the source JSON.
member.remarks: Unknown JSON is a dictionary of values not defined in the ArcGIS specification used to create this object but found in the source JSON. If the object is written back to JSON, any unknown JSON data is not persisted. The ArcGIS specification may be for a web map, web scene, REST API, and so on.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackSeparator.UnsupportedJson
member.summary: Gets a collection of unsupported data from the source JSON.

If the object is written back to JSON, any unsupported JSON data is persisted. The ArcGIS specification may be from a web map, web scene, REST API, and so on.

member.summary: Use the default position ().

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackStrategy
member.summary: The strategy for whether the text should be stacked or wrapped, rather than place long trailing labels across the map.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackStrategy.Automatic
member.summary: Stacking will be enabled for some feature types.

remarks.para: The default stacking behavior for labels will depend on their feature type:

list.item: Line feature labels will not stack.
Point and polygon feature labels may stack.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackStrategy.None
member.summary: The text should not be broken, no matter how long it is.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelStackStrategy.Allow
member.summary: The label placement engine can break the text into two or more rows, if the rows are longer than the limit.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelTextLayout
member.summary: The layout to use for label text, which can be either horizontal, straight, perpendicular, or follow the line feature.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelTextLayout.Automatic
member.summary: The text layout will be determined by the individual feature type.

remarks.para: For each label, the layout will be determined by the feature type:

list.item: Line feature labels will use .
Point and polygon feature labels will use .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelTextLayout.Horizontal
member.summary: Text will be written horizontally (with respect to the screen).
member.remarks: If the map is rotated within the screen, then the text will be redrawn (and possibly repositioned) to remain horizontal with respect to the screen.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelTextLayout.Perpendicular
member.summary: Text will be written perpendicular to the text layout.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelTextLayout.Straight
member.summary: Text will be written along a straight line, following the point positioning angle or at a tangent to the line feature.

remarks.para: An angle for placement and orientation will be calculated for each label if the labelAngleInfo JSON property for the has been specified.
Centered point labels will stay centered on the point feature, but will be rotated to the angle (or horizontal, if none has been defined).

Offset point labels will be positioned around the point feature, at the position specified by the angle. The text will lie along this angle, so that it stretches radially from the edge of the point feature.
Note that if a label has an angle of zero then its text will be drawn horizontally with respect to the map. If the map is rotated within the screen, the text will rotate with the map.
Line labels will be positioned as usual, but then rotated (around their midpoint) to lie on a straight line at a tangent to the line geometry at that point.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelTextLayout.FollowFeature
member.summary: Text will bend to follow a curved or multi-segment line feature.

remarks.para: Individual characters will be angled to follow the changes in angle of the line geometry.
If the text overruns the beginning or end of the line geometry, then the remaining characters will be laid out in a straight line extrapolated from the last geometry segment.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelTextOrientation
member.summary: The orientation of label text.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelTextOrientation.Automatic
member.summary: Use the default text orientation ().

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelTextOrientation.Direction
member.summary: Text follows the placement angle direction, even if it means being drawn upside-down.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.LabelTextOrientation.Screen
member.summary: Text follows the screen orientation, and flips if necessary.
member.remarks: Text should be flipped through 180 degrees to keep it readable on the screen. The text will flip whenever the feature determines that the text is angled upside-down, or if the map is rotated within the screen to make the text upside-down (with respect to the screen).

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.SimpleLabelExpression
member.summary: An expression script using the Simple REST language.

remarks.para: The label Simple expression expects to hold a legal, Simple script and to be read and evaluated by a Simple expression interpreter. An example expression that combines text with a field value is: "State: " CONCAT [State_Name].

Note that quotes are needed around the literal text. See https://developers.arcgis.com/documentation/common-data-types/labeling-objects.htm for full documentation on the labelExpression syntax and functions.

Warning: If the expression attempts to use attributes which don't exist then no label will be produced. This is distinct from the attribute existing but having a null or empty value, which can form part of the produced text label.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.SimpleLabelExpression.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with an empty expression script.
member.remarks: An empty script is a legal Simple expression that will evaluate to an empty string.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.SimpleLabelExpression.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a specified Simple expression script.
member.remarks: The expression is expected to be a complete, self-contained Simple expression.
member.param: The Simple expression script string.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.WebmapLabelExpression
member.summary: The label Webmap expression expects to hold a legal Webmap script, to be read and evaluated by a Webmap expression interpreter.

remarks.para: An example expression that combines text with a field value is: State {State_Name}. Note that quotes are not needed around the literal text.

Warning: If expression using attributes which don't exist then no label will be produced. This is distinct from the attribute existing but having a null or empty value, which can form part of the produced text label.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Labeling.WebmapLabelExpression.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with an empty expression script.
member.remarks: An empty script is a legal Webmap expression that will evaluate to an empty string.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a specified Webmap expression script.
member.remarks: The expression is expected to be a complete, self-contained Webmap expression.
member.param: The Webmap expression script string.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Map
member.summary: A map is a container for layers. Use a map together with a map view to display layers of geographic data in 2D.

remarks.para: The map contains layers of mapping data and information such as basemaps, popups, renderers, and labels which define the map's capabilities. You can access the map content directly or visualize the map in a map view.

In an MVC architecture, the map represents the model and map view represents the view. The map specifies how the geographic data is organized, and the map view renders the data on the screen and allows users to interact with it.

A map can contain a basemap and one or more operational layers:
A basemap helps orient the user of the map. Typically it sits behind operational layers and shows roads and parcel boundaries to give context to operational layers. Basemaps can also contain layers which are drawn over the top operational layers such as label layers.
An operational layer provides content that is of unique interest to the app and the task at hand, such as data about earthquakes, traffic, or weather. Its content might change frequently.
You can create a map from an existing web map using its URL or . Alternatively, you can get a map directly from a collection. The map properties will be hydrated when the map is loaded.
If you create a new map, the spatial reference of the first layer you add, which is typically the first layer in the , defines the spatial reference of the entire map.
See Maps 2D for more information about maps.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Map.#ctor(System.Uri)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a URL to a web map.

remarks.para: If the specified is a portal item URL, the underlying is created and can be accessed through . The URL can be a direct URL to a web map or the URL of a portal item.

Examples of supported URL formats:
The web map viewer page, for example: https://www.arcgis.com/apps/mapviewer/index.html?webmap=55ebf90799fa4a3fa57562700a68c405
The web map viewer page (classic), for example: https://www.arcgis.com/home/webmap/viewer.html?webmap=55ebf90799fa4a3fa57562700a68c405
The web map item details page, for example: https://www.arcgis.com/home/item.html?id=55ebf90799fa4a3fa57562700a68c405
The REST sharing API data page, for example: https://www.arcgis.com/sharing/rest/content/items/55ebf90799fa4a3fa57562700a68c405/data
The REST sharing API page (with or without the query parameter f=json), for example: https://www.arcgis.com/sharing/rest/content/items/55ebf90799fa4a3fa57562700a68c405?f=json

member.param: Uri of a web map on ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Enterprise portal. A will be created to reference the web map and accessible through .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Map.MaxScale

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Map.ReferenceScale
member.summary: Gets or sets the reference scale for the map.

remarks.para: The reference scale is the scale at which feature symbols and text will appear at their authored size. If the viewing scale is different from the reference scale, then the symbology and text graphics will be scaled to keep the same size on the map. Only those layers for which scaleSymbols is true will be scaled. For example, .
The default value is 0.0. Zero reference scale means that no reference scale is set and all symbology and text graphics will be drawn at their default screen size.

Updates to the reference scale will redraw other layers that use the reference scale, and may redraw other layers that depend on them. For example, dynamic labels may need to re-position to avoid overlaps.

member.value: The reference scale. May be 0 when layer is not loaded, or if layer has no reference scale set.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Map.OfflineSettings
member.summary: Gets offline settings configured by the author of an online web map (if appropriate).

remarks.para: Offline settings optimize the data that is delivered during download and synchronization. For example, they can control feature and attachment delivery or define whether the map's basemap is created using a tile package already on the device.
This property is null unless the map represents an online web map and the author has configured the offline settings.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Map.SaveAsync(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Saves any changes that have been made to the map to the associated .

remarks.para: Asynchronous method to save any changes that have been made to the map. The map must have been constructed using the Map(PortalItem) constructor to associate it with a PortalItem object, and must be loaded.
Updates the map content data on the portal and also updates the portal item properties stored on the portal to match those stored within the map's PortalItem object.

member.param: Flag to indicate whether the map should be saved to the supported web map version that the API supports (see system requirements). This may cause data loss as unknown data is not saved. If true, unknown data will be removed and the map will be saved. If false, the operation will fail if the map contains unknown data that will not be saved. This flag can be used to provide a warning to users that some map data may be lost when the map is saved.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous save operation.
member.exception: Map must be loaded before saving to a portal.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Map.SaveAsync(System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Save any changes that have been made to the map to the associated PortalItem.

remarks.para: Asynchronous method to save any changes that have been made to the map allowing cancellation. The map must have been constructed using the Map(PortalItem) constructor to associate it with a PortalItem object, and must be loaded.
Updates the map content data on the portal and also updates the portal item properties stored on the portal to match those stored within the map's PortalItem object.

member.param: Flag to indicate whether the map should be saved to the supported web map version that the API supports (see system requirements). This may cause data loss as unknown data is not saved. If true, unknown data will be removed and the map will be saved. If false, the operation will fail if the map contains unknown data that will not be saved. This flag can be used to provide a warning to users that some map data may be lost when the map is saved.
CancellationToken
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous save operation.
member.exception: Map must be loaded before saving to a portal.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Map.SaveAsAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.ArcGISPortal,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalFolder,System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.RuntimeImage,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Save the map to a new .

remarks.para: Asynchronous method to save a map as a new portal item. The map does not need to be associated with an existing PortalItem object. The map must be loaded. The result is a reference to the new PortalItem which the map is now associated with.

Note that if the map was already associated with a PortalItem object (either because the map was created using a PortalItem or was previously saved), that object will be replaced with a new PortalItem, effectively duplicating the map on the portal.

member.param: The portal to save the map to
The portal folder in which to save the map. If null is passed it will be saved to the user's root folder.
The title of the map. Cannot be empty
The description of the map
The tags to be associated with the newly created portal item
The thumbnail of the map

Flag to indicate whether the map should be saved to the supported web map version that the API supports (see system requirements). This may cause data loss as unknown data is not saved. If true, unknown data will be removed and the map will be saved. If false, the operation will fail if the map contains unknown data that will not be saved. This flag can be used to provide a warning to users that some map data may be lost when the map is saved.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous save as operation. The value of the task result is a object.
member.exception: Map must be loaded before saving to a portal.

member.summary: Clones this instance of the map and its members.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Map.UnknownJson
member.summary: Gets the unknown JSON from the web map source JSON in a dictionary of JSON strings. This data will be lost if the web map gets saved back to JSON.
member.value: The unknown JSON objects stored in the map.
member.remarks: The UnknownJson is the JSON that allows apps to access any custom JSON that is not part of the known web map specification and couldn't be validated against the JSON Schema. This JSON can be accessed by 3rd party applications that need to use this extra information. Code relying on data in UnknownJson should be resilient against data no longer available in future updates, where support gets added and this data gets parsed and moved into public properties. Any data in here will be lost if map is saved. JSON that ArcGIS Maps SDK doesn't support but is part of the web map specification will be available in the .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Map.UnsupportedJson
member.summary: Gets the unsupported data from the web map source JSON from the web map source JSON in a dictionary of JSON strings. This data will be persisted in the document.
member.value: The unsupported JSON objects stored in the map.
member.remarks: The unsupported is the JSON that is not yet supported by the ArcGIS Maps SDK for .NET but is part of the known web map specification. Any JSON in this collection will be retained when a map is saved. JSON that is not part of the web map specification will be available in the .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Map.MaxExtent
member.summary: Gets or sets the extent that constrains the display to a specific region.

remarks.para: The max extent is initialized when the map loads or when you set it explicitly. This value limits panning in the map view to a specific area of the map.

If the max extent is null when the map loads, or you set it to null, max extent is calculated from the union of the full extent of all basemap layers. If there is no basemap, it is set as the full extent of the map's first operational layer.

If the is not set, the max extent is used as the initial viewpoint. If the is set, but is outside of the max extent, the max extent takes precedence and the display will initially show the max extent.

member.value: The extent that constrains the display to a specific region.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.MobileMapPackage
member.summary: A mobile map package.
member.remarks:
Mobile map packages allow you to work offline with maps on a mobile device. Each mobile map package can encapsulate one or more map definitions, including their basemap layers, data layers, layer content, layer styles and pop-up definitions. Each mobile map package contains an with metadata about the package, such as the description and thumbnail.
A single archive file with a .mmpk extension.
A directory containing an unpacked mobile map package.
You can create a mobile map package using either:
ArcGIS Pro (from version 1.3). This API version supports any up to major version 5. Mobile map packages created with ArcGIS Pro are stored in a single file with an .mmpk extension. Data contained in this mobile map package file cannot be edited. For more information, see the Create Mobile Map Package tool in ArcGIS Pro.
Use to generate and download a mobile map package on-demand, or use to download a mobile map package prepared as an offline map area by the map author (known as the ahead-of time workflow). Mobile map packages downloaded using the are stored in a directory containing an unpacked mobile map package. You can edit feature data contained in this mobile map package and you can synchronize changes with their online feature services. For more information, explore the on-demand and ahead-of-time workflows offered by the .
Mobile map packages created with ArcGIS Pro can also include transportation networks, locators, and links to online services. You can choose whether to use:
A tile package (such as .tpkx or .vtpk) or an online basemap as the .
A mobile geodatabase (.geodatabase) or an online feature service as one of the . Online services (such as traffic or weather) can provide excellent contextual information for your users. If the mobile map package is going to be used in areas of poor connectivity, however, you must ensure that data critical to your workflow is stored locally on the device. implements the interface. Once it is loaded, you can access its content and perform the following:
Determine the version of this package using the property. This API currently supports mobile map packages up to and including major version 5. If the package is from an unsupported version, it will fail to load.
Access the individual maps and display them in a MapView.
Discover whether the mobile map package has expired using the property.
Access the individual maps and display them in a MapView.
Programmatically add, remove, or modify layers by setting a display filter, changing the renderer, or changing the visibility on layers. These changes will not be persisted because mobile map packages are read-only. Note: If the mobile map package was created with ArcGIS Pro, you cannot edit its underlying data from feature tables in a mobile map package. However, if you create a based on the of a in the mobile map package, you can edit the data in the .

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.remarks: The path can refer to a file with an .mmpk extension or a directory containing an unpacked mobile map package.
member.param: A path to a mobile map package.

member.exception: Package was authored to prevent expired access.
Package contains data that requires unpack before opening.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.MobileMapPackage.Expiration
member.summary: Gets the expiration details for this mobile map package, if provided.

remarks.para: Expiration details provide:

list.item: The package’s expiration date and time.
Whether the maps can be accessed after expiration.
Any messages relevant for the user.

remarks.para: These expiration details can be specified when the author creates a mobile map package using ArcGIS Pro (from version 2.4). This requires the ArcGIS Pro Publisher Extension. Mobile map packages created with the do not support expiration.

By publishing a package with expiration details, the author can control the experience an end-user has when they try to access information that is no longer valid. For example, for time limited data (such as major sporting events), the author can ensure that the data cannot be accessed after the expiry date.
During package loading, this API will determine whether the mobile map package was authored with expiration. If so, then this property will be populated.

If the package has expired and was authored as , loading will fail and you will not be able to access the maps.The expiration details will be accessible for you to examine and/or communicate to the user.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.MobileMapPackage.Item
member.summary: Gets the mobile map package's describing metadata about the package.

remarks.para:
The item includes the metadata about the mobile map package, such as:
Title
Snippet (summary)
Description
Tags
Thumbnail If the package was created with ArcGIS Pro, the metadata was provided by the package author. If the package was created using the , the metadata was provided by the originating web map.
A package's item will be an instance of a .
Value is null if the package is not loaded.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.MobileMapPackage.LocatorTask
member.summary: Gets the for the mobile map package.

remarks.para: Use this task to geocode and reverse-geocode addresses and places. There is only one in each mobile map package but it can be used by all maps.
Returns null if there is no in the package or the package is not loaded.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.MobileMapPackage.Maps
member.summary: Gets a collection of Maps from the .

remarks.para: To use the maps in a , you first need to load the package.
If the mobile map package was created with ArcGIS Pro, the maps are presented in the same order in which they were packaged. Mobile map packages created with the will only contain one map.

If you display the map by setting the map to a MapView, the map will automatically load. If you only need to access the map's content or metadata, you will need to load it by calling . If the package is not loaded, an empty collection is returned.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.MobileMapPackage.Path
member.summary: Gets the path to the mobile map package.
member.remarks: The path can refer to a file with an .mmpk extension or a directory containing an unpacked mobile map package.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.MobileMapPackage.Version
member.summary: Gets the mobile map package's version.

remarks.para: The mobile map package version is set when the package is authored in ArcGIS Pro or when it is generated by the .
This property will be populated when you attempt to load the package. The version property will always be populated for you to examine, even if the package is an unsupported version.
This API currently support mobile map packages up to and including major version 5.
You may wish to check the version property before trying to access specific functionality. For example:

list.item: Expiration details are only available from mobile map package version 3.1 onwards.
Links to online services are only available from mobile map package version 4.0 onwards.

remarks.para: If the package fails to load, check that the version number has not exceeded the supported major version.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.MobileMapPackage.UnpackAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Unpacks a mobile map package file (.mmpk) to an output directory.

remarks.para: If the last level of the is not present, it will be created as part of the unpack task. The unpack task writes the full content of the mobile map package to the output directory. Care should be taken on devices with limited storage space, especially if the original package is very large. After unpacking, you can remove the original .mmpk file from the device.
Note that unpacking will fail if the package is expired and was authored as .

member.param: The path to a mobile map package file (.mmpk)
The path to a directory to write the mobile map package contents. This path must be specified without a trailing slash.
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous unpack operation.
member.exception: Package was authored to prevent expired access.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.MobileMapPackage.UnpackAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Unpacks a mobile map package file (.mmpk) to an output directory.

remarks.para: If the last level of the is not present, it will be created as part of the unpack task. The unpack task writes the full content of the mobile map package to the output directory. Care should be taken on devices with limited storage space, especially if the original package is very large. After unpacking, you can remove the original .mmpk file from the device.
Note that unpacking will fail if the package is expired and was authored as .

member.param: the path to a mobile map package file (.mmpk)
a path to a directory to write the mobile map package contents.
A for canceling the unpack operation.
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous unpack operation.
member.exception: Package was authored to prevent expired access.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.MobileMapPackage.Close
member.summary: Closes a mobile map package.

remarks.para: Closes a mobile map package and frees file locks on the underlying .mmpk file or directory.

All references to mobile map package data (maps, layers, tables, networks, locators, etc.) should be released before closing the package. If active references to mobile map package data exist, this method will still close the package, but subsequent rendering and data access methods will fail. Results of accessing mobile map package data after are undefined.
After closing a mobile map package, the underlying .mmpk file or directory can be moved or deleted.
Closing a mobile map package is not necessary if the package has not been loaded.

member.exception: Package was authored to prevent expired access.
Package contains data that requires unpack before loading.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.MobileScenePackage
member.summary: A mobile scene package.

remarks.para: Mobile scene packages allow you to work with 3D scenes on a mobile device. A mobile scene package contains all of the files necessary to work with your scenes and is stored on a device as either:

list.item: A single archive file with a .mspk extension.
A directory.

remarks.para: A mobile scene package can encapsulate one or more scenes, their layers and data, transportation networks, and a locator. The mobile scene package also contains an with metadata about the package (description, thumbnail, etc.). You can use these packages to share scenes across the ArcGIS products and to allow your users to work with scenes in the field. Mobile scene packages also allow you to continue working when you have limited connectivity.
Starting with ArcGIS Pro 2.3 you can create mobile scene packages for use by this API. Mobile scene packages are supported up to major version 3.
Mobile scene packages can contain links to online services, but be aware that these services will be unavailable if your device is offline. You can choose whether to use:

list.item: A tile package (.tpkx) or an online basemap as the .
A mobile geodatabase (.geodatabase) or an online feature service as one of the .
Local raster data (such as a DTED, GeoTIFF) or online image services as the elevation sources in a .

remarks.para: Online services (such as traffic or weather) can provide excellent contextual information for your users. If the mobile scene package is going to be used in areas of poor connectivity, however, you must ensure that data critical to your workflow is stored locally on the device.
Mobile scene packages implement the interface; you need to load the before you can access its content. Once loaded you can:

list.item: Determine the version of this package using the property. This API supports mobile scene packages up to and including major version 3. If the package is from an unsupported version it will fail to load.
Discover whether the package has expired using the property.
Access the individual scenes and display them in a .
Programmatically add, modify, and remove layers in the scene. Mobile scene packages, however, are read-only and these changes to scenes or layers will not be persisted to the device.

Whether the scenes can be accessed after expiration.

remarks.para: By publishing a package with expiration details, the author can control the experience an end-user has when they try to access information that is no longer valid. For example, for time limited data (such as major sporting events), the author can ensure that the data cannot be accessed after the expiry date.
These expiration details can be specified when the author creates a mobile scene package using ArcGIS Pro (from version 2.4). This requires the ArcGIS Pro Publisher Extension.
During package loading, this API will determine whether the mobile scene package was authored with expiration. If so, then this property will be populated.

If the package has expired and was authored as , loading will fail and you will not be able to access the scenes. The expiration details will be accessible for you to examine and/or communicate to the user.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.NavigationConstraint
member.summary: Identifies type of navigation constraint based on the surface elevation.
member.remarks: This enum is used to constrain all forms of camera navigation to the elevation surface.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.NavigationConstraint.None
member.summary: Camera navigation is unconstrained. In this mode the camera may pass above and below the elevation surface.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.NavigationConstraint.StayAbove
member.summary: Camera navigation is constrained to remaining above the elevation surface. The altitude of the camera may not fall below the elevation of the surface. If the elevation is added or updates resulting in the camera altitude being below the elevation the camera will be pushed up to be at the surface.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.OfflineSettings
member.summary: Represents settings in an online web map which the author has configured for offline use.
member.remarks: Offline settings can be configured by a web map author to provide default values which are appropriate for taking a map offline. Applications which take the map offline can choose to ignore these offline settings when setting parameters for an . Note: the following methods will use the offline settings to set default parameter values:

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.OfflineSettings.AttachmentSyncDirection
member.summary: Gets a setting configured by the web map author that provides guidance for how attachments should be synced when working offline.

remarks.para: If this setting is , then attachments should never be uploaded or downloaded when performing a sync operation.
If this setting is , then attachments should be uploaded when performing sync.
If this setting is , then attachments should be uploaded and downloaded when performing sync. This value can be used to set a default for .
Alternatively, using will pre-populate the parameters using this property.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.OfflineSettings.OnlineLayers
member.summary: Gets a list of layers that the web map author has determined will remain online when the map is taken offline.
member.remarks: Layers may be selected to remain online because they do not support being taken offline or to provide live contextual information such as traffic or weather.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.OfflineSettings.OnlineTables
member.summary: Gets a list of non-spatial tables that the web map author has determined will remain online when the map is taken offline.
member.remarks: Tables may be selected to remain online because they do not support being taken offline or to provide live contextual information such as traffic or weather.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.OfflineSettings.ReferenceBasemapFilename
member.summary: Gets the name of a local basemap file which can be used instead of downloading a basemap when taking this map offline.

remarks.para: Supported basemap formats are: .tpk, .tpkx, and .vtpk
The webmap author sets this property to denote an appropriate basemap file. Client code is responsible for ensuring that the basemap has been correctly side-loaded to the device.
This property can be used to inform settings for:

member.value: The reference basemap filename.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.OfflineSettings.ReturnLayerAttachmentOption
member.summary: Gets a setting configured by the web map author that indicates which feature layers should include attachments when taken offline.
member.remarks:
Based on the value of this setting, attachments should be included with:
None of the layers when the value is .
All of the layers when the value is .
Read only layers when the value is .
Editable layers when the value is . This value can be used to set a default for . Alternatively, using will pre-populate the parameters using this property.
member.value: The layer attachment option configured by the web map author.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.OfflineSettings.ReturnSchemaOnlyForEditableLayers
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the web map author indicates whether or not feature layers taken offline should be schema only and contain no data.
member.remarks: If true, this setting means that feature layers should be taken offline with no existing data in them. If false, this setting means that existing features should be included when taking data offline for an editing workflow. This value can be used to set a default for . Alternatively, using will pre-populate the parameters using this property.
member.value: true if the web map author intends editable layers to be downloaded as schema only, false to download with data.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Popups.Popup.EvaluatedElements
member.summary: Gets a collection of objects evaluated asynchronously by .

remarks.para: During the evaluation, each is replaced by the returned by the Arcade expression and each placeholder is replaced by its value. If the Arcade expression returns a list of attributes to be used by the pop-up element, these attributes will be used by .

The collection is empty until has been executed, and is cleared if is executed more than once. After evaluation, the order of the pop-up elements in the collection matches and the order of elements shown in the pop-up window. If an fails to be evaluated, then it will be skipped in the collection. Use the collection of returned from to learn the cause of the error.

member.value: A collection of objects evaluated asynchronously by .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Popups.Popup.UserInput
member.summary: Gets or sets the geometry of the click or tap location that invoked the popup.
member.remarks: If expressions reference the variable $userInput, the variable will be replaced by the value. Typically, this represents the map location the user clicked or tapped to launch a popup. This value may be a , an , or perhaps null (if the popup wasn't opened from map interaction). Therefore, check the geometry type before using it in an Arcade expression. A value for this property should be set by the SDK that opens a popup to ensure that Arcade expressions are evaluated correctly. If the is created from a call to an identify method that returns popups, such as GeoView.IdentifyLayerAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Layer, Point, double, bool), the will be automatically initialized with the coordinate where the identify operation began.
member.value: The geometry of the click or tap location that invoked the popup.

member.summary: Creates a new popup from a GeoElement.
member.param: A or .
member.returns: A from the GeoElement.
member.remarks: This method creates a default with default properties and default popup elements based on the attributes of the given .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Popups.Popup.EvaluateExpressionsAsync
member.summary: Asynchronously evaluates all expressions available at or at . The expression can include Arcade FeatureSet functions, which need to be evaluated asynchronously.

remarks.para: Also populates the from . During the evaluation, each is replaced by the returned by the Arcade expression and each placeholder is replaced by its value.

This method must be called before displaying the popup information in a UI so you can use synchronous method to get the formatted representation. If there are no and no popup elements of type then the result array will be empty.
The may be used in the , , , and .

member.returns: A task object representing the asynchronous evaluation of expressions. The value of the task result is a read-only list of .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Popups.Popup.GetFormattedValue(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Popups.PopupField)
member.summary: Returns the formatted value of the popup field.
member.param: The popup field.
member.returns: A string that has been formatted.
member.remarks: Dates and times are returned in the local timezone. If is not empty then the may be using expressions with Arcade FeatureSet functions. Use to evaluate the Arcade expressions before trying to get the formatted representation of the popup field's value, otherwise it may return an empty string. If the field is part of a , the is returned.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Popups.PopupDateFormat
member.summary: Format that controls how a date field is displayed in a popup.

remarks.para: Most formats are based on from , so the component order, formatting, day-of-the-week names, and month names vary based on the current culture.
Formats that end with -24 always use 24-hour time. The rest of the formats use either 12-hour or 24-hour time based on the current culture (e.g. 5:59 PM or 17:59 or 오후 5:59).
Formats that end with -LE always use "day-month-year" order for dates. The rest of the formats order date components based on the current culture (e.g. 12/30/1997 or 1997-12-30 or 30.12.1997).

Some formats may produce different results on .NET 5+ compared to .NET Framework or UWP, because .NET 5+ uses a different internationalization database. For more information, see .NET Globalization and ICU.
Documentation examples below are localized to en-US.

member.summary: Example: Dec 30, 1997

member.summary: Example: Tuesday, December 30, 1997
member.remarks: Matches the current .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Popups.PopupManager.EvaluatedElements
member.summary: Gets a collection of objects evaluated asynchronously by .

remarks.para: During the evaluation, each is replaced by the returned by the Arcade expression and each placeholder is replaced by its value. If the Arcade expression returns a list of attributes to be used by the pop-up element, these attributes will be used by .

The collection is empty until has been executed, and is cleared if is executed more than once. After evaluation, the order of the pop-up elements in the collection matches and the order of elements shown in the pop-up window. If an fails to be evaluated, then it will be skipped in the collection. Use the collection of returned from to learn the cause of the error.

member.value: A collection of objects evaluated asynchronously by .

member.summary: Asynchronously evaluates all expressions, including Arcade FeatureSet functions available at . member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.RasterElevationSource
member.summary: An elevation source based on raster elevation files.

remarks.para: A raster elevation source helps visualize maps and layers in 3D by providing a surface that layers may be draped or offset from. You can create a raster elevation source from a collection of raster elevation files, such as Digital Terrain Elevation Data (DTED) format files. See Raster To DTED for more information about creating raster elevation files based on the DTED tiling scheme.
To utilize an elevation source in a scene's surface, add it to the scene's collection.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Scene
member.summary: The scene is a container for layers. You use a scene together with a to display layers of geographic data in 3D.

remarks.para: The scene contains layers of mapping data and information such as elevation data, renderers, and labels which define the scene's capabilities. You can access the scene content directly or, more commonly, visualize the scene in a scene view.

In an MVC architecture, the scene represents the model and the scene view represents the 3D view. The scene specifies how the geographic data is organized, and the scene view renders the data on the screen and allows users to interact with it.

A scene can contain a base surface, a basemap, and one or more operational layers:
A base surface is the default surface on which layers are draped or from which relative layers are offset. The ground height is provided by objects that are combined to make the .
A basemap helps orient the user of the scene. It is composed of a collection of base layers, such as imagery or hillshade, that are draped on the surface, and/or reference layers such as street labels that are displayed on top of operational layers. This content is relatively static.
An operational layer provides content that is of unique interest to the app and the task at hand, such as data about earthquakes, traffic, or weather. This content can change frequently.
You can create a scene from an existing web scene using its URL or . Alternatively, you can get a scene directly from a collection. properties are hydrated when the scene is loaded.
If you create a new scene, the spatial reference of the first layer you add, which is typically the first layer in the , defines the spatial reference of the entire scene.
See Scenes 3D) for more information about scenes.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Scene.#ctor(System.Uri)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a Uri to a web scene.

remarks.para: If the specified is a portal item URL, the underlying is created and can be accessed through . The URL may be a direct URL to a web scene or the URL of a portal item.

Examples of supported URL formats:
The web scene viewer page, for example: https://www.arcgis.com/home/webscene/viewer.html?webscene=579f97b2f3b94d4a8e48a5f140a6639b
The web scene item details page, for example: https://www.arcgis.com/home/item.html?id=579f97b2f3b94d4a8e48a5f140a6639b
The REST sharing API data, for example: https://www.arcgis.com/sharing/rest/content/items/579f97b2f3b94d4a8e48a5f140a6639b/data
The REST sharing API page (with or without the query parameter f=json), for example: https://www.arcgis.com/sharing/rest/content/items/579f97b2f3b94d4a8e48a5f140a6639b?f=json

member.param: Uri of the web scene on ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Enterprise portal.

member.summary: Gets the that defines how tile based data is rendered.

remarks.para: The tiling scheme property is used by the to determine how to render tiled data.
This property is determined during . The of the first tiled elevation source () or tiled layer in the basemap or operational layer (see ) determines whether the value is ref or .
If you create a new scene, you can set this value before adding the base surface, basemap or operational layers. The must correspond to the of the layers to ensure they render correctly.
The default value is .

member.remarks: Provides a surface on which layers may be draped or from which relative layers may be offset. The most common use of the Surface is to represent the ground in a scene. It is comprised of a collection of elevation sources () which are combined in the to generate the surface. If the data from the elevation sources is spatially coincident it will use the elevation source order to determine which source to use.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Surface.GetElevationAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Geometry.MapPoint)
member.summary: Gets the elevation (in meters) for the specified location on the surface.
member.remarks: The returned elevation value is based on the highest possible level of detail available into the surface from the provided elevation sources. This results in the highest possible resolution for the elevation value for the given location. Note that this may differ from the value of the elevation currently being rendered which may be at a lower resolution.
member.param: The point location to find the elevation for.
member.returns: An asynchronous task of type double for the elevation at the specified position.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Surface.ElevationExaggeration
member.summary: Gets or sets the elevation exaggeration for the surface.
member.remarks: The altitude values of the surface data are multiplied by the exaggeration value. The default value is 1.0, indicating no exaggeration.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Surface.ElevationSources
member.summary: Gets the collection of elevation sources in the surface.

remarks.para: Elevation sources are an ordered collection of data that are combined to generate elevation values for the surface. The order of the elevation sources in the collection indicate which elevation has priority when the sources are spatially coincident. The collection is specific to a surface so you cannot reuse an elevation source collection coming from another .

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether this surface is enabled and should be rendered.
member.remarks: This value indicates if the surface is enabled and is rendered. If true, the surface's elevation sources are only used in the calculation of the surface if their is also true.
member.value: true if the surface is enabled, false otherwise.

member.value: The value can be between 0 and 1.0, where 0 is transparent and 1.0 is solid or opaque.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Mapping.Viewpoint
member.summary: Defines the visible area of a that is displayed in a .

remarks.para: In a MapView, the bounding extent of a geometry or the map's center point and scale can define the map view's two-dimensional .
In a , a represents the observer's position and perspective within three dimensions and defines the scene view's three-dimensional . Each camera has the following properties:

list.item: Geographic location on the surface (longitude and latitude)
Altitude (height, in meters, above sea level)
Heading (angle about the z axis the camera is rotated, in degrees)
Pitch (angle the camera is rotated up or down, in degrees)
Roll (angle the camera is rotated side-to-side, in degrees)

remarks.para: When you publish or save a map or scene, as a web map, web scene, mobile map package, or mobile scene package, you define its initial . When you load a map or scene into your app, you can access and change the value of this . If you add the map or scene to a , any change you make to the is ignored. To change the of a displayed map or scene, use view methods such as:

list.item: to instantly change the visible area with no animation.
to animate the display of the map to a viewpoint using the provided animation curve.
to instantly move the perspective in 3D scenes.

remarks.para: To determine the current visible area, call . Make sure that any user-initiated or programmatic navigation is complete before getting the current by checking the value of .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.License
member.summary: Represents an ArcGIS Maps SDK for .NET license.

remarks.para: Until an app is licensed, it runs at the Developer license level. All functionality is available, but the map display is watermarked and a message is logged stating that the app is in developer mode.

A deployed app needs to be run at minimum with the Lite license level to disable the map watermark. The Lite, Basic, Standard, or Advanced license level is set by calling with a license string obtained from the ArcGIS for Developers website or Esri Customer Service. With the varying license levels, a subset of functionality is available. Any attempt to use licensed functionality that is not available at the current license level will log or emit error messages.
To get Lite, Basic, Standard, or Advanced license level functionality, a user app is licensed in one of two ways:

list.item: With license information retrieved after logging in to a portal (Lite and Basic levels).
With a license string acquired from the ArcGIS for Developers website or Esri Customer Service.

remarks.para: To license with a license string, call .
To validate with information from a portal, you need a instance. You can get one by calling . Pass the to .

Both of these methods return a that reports the status of the license. Note that there is a 30-day timeout when using . If the app is licensed using this method, the user must log in to the portal again before licensing the app. When this period has expired, calling returns a which contains license status of , and the app continues to run at the current license level (default is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.License.Expiry
member.summary: Gets the license expiry date and time in UTC.
member.remarks: This is either the expiry date of a license set with or the portal subscription expiry when using .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.License.Extensions
member.summary: Gets the collection of extension licenses for the app.

remarks.para: For valid extension license keys supplied when calling , this collection is populated with information about the extension licenses. Use this function to iterate extension licenses available for the app.
Note that, although this collection is empty when running an app in developer mode, all extension licensed functionality will be available.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.License.IsPermanent
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the license is perpetual.
member.value: true if a license is perpetual, false otherwise.
member.remarks: Typically this returns true for apps licensed with a license string with 'none' for the expiration date field.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.License.LicenseLevel
member.summary: Gets the current license level set in the app. See for available levels.
member.remarks: By default, unless a deployment license is provided, apps run with a license.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.License.LicenseStatus
member.summary: Gets the current license status of the provided license.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.License.LicenseType
member.summary: Gets the license type of the current license.
member.remarks: In developer mode, this function returns . Otherwise, it can return if the app was licensed by logging in to a portal and acquiring a , or if the app was licensed from a license string. The license type can change while an app runs. For instance, the app may start running licensed with a Lite license key () and later become licensed from ().

For additional information on licensing an ArcGIS Maps SDK for .NET application, see Licensing Your ArcGIS Maps SDK for .NET Application.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.BeaconScanner
member.summary: Scans for nearby beacons.
member.remarks: uses data stored in the 'Beacons' feature layer to perform a scan and report information related to iBeacon devices. Some of the data produced by is not a part of the official iBeacon standard.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.BeaconScanner.#ctor(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.IndoorPositioningDefinition)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class using the information contained in the provided .

remarks.para:
uses the 'Beacons' feature layer from the ArcGIS Indoors Information Model, which is stored in , to perform scans for a list of beacons. For a map to be IPS-aware, one of the following must be true:
A reference to an indoor positioning data service is defined in the web map properties.
It contains an "ips_positioning" feature table.
If a map is IPS-aware, an can be returned using .

member.param: The from an IPS-aware map.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.BeaconScanner.AllDiscoveredBeacons
member.summary: Gets a list of all beacons discovered in the last scanning session.
member.remarks: A scanning session starts when you call and ends by calling . This property resets when user starts a new scanning session (by calling ). A value of 127 means the iBeacon device was detected, but no iBeacon data was received. While is running, some entries can have a of 127 even if this iBeacon device transmits iBeacon data due to how iBeacon devices transmit multiple packets with different information. If a packet not related to iBeacon is received first, then may consider this device as a beacon that does not transmit iBeacon data until the first iBeacon data arrives. To avoid that scenario, check for a value of 127 after stopping a scan session.
member.value: Returns a list of all beacons discovered in the last scanning session.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.BeaconScanner.Error
member.summary: Gets an error that describes a problem encountered while starting the .
member.remarks: This property notifies the user about errors that occur in . If cannot be started, this property stores information related to what caused to fail to start.
member.value: An error that describes a problem encountered while starting the .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.BeaconScanner.Status
member.summary: Gets the current status of the .
member.remarks: The status indicates if the is starting, started, stopping, stopped, or has failed to start.
member.value: The current status of the .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.BeaconScanner.StartAsync
member.summary: Starts the .
member.remarks: If is not loaded when starts, performs loading during the starting phase, which can lead to a significant delay before is started. To prevent such a delay, manually load before starting .
member.returns: a indicating when the has started.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.BeaconScanner.StopAsync
member.summary: Stops the .
member.returns: a indicating when the has stopped.

member.name: E:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.BeaconScanner.BeaconsDiscovered
member.summary: Returns a list of iBeacons discovered in the last second.
member.remarks: This event is triggered every second to provide all iBeacons visible in the last second. A visible iBeacon is a beacon that transmits iBeacon data in the advertisement data. If an iBeacon device does not produce an iBeacon advertisement packet, it will not be included in the list of visible beacons.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.IndoorsLocationDataSourceConfiguration.PathSnappingUnit

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with timestamp and additional source properties.

remarks.para: A can be created from a variety of sources and using different technologies. By supplying you can allow users of this to find out how the data was captured.

Information is provided as a set of key-value pairs, where the key describes the type of data held in the value. Values must be basic data types such as string, numeric, boolean or date. The creator of the can use any string for the key - but this API recognizes a number of well known keys which should be used if available. See :
floor (an integer value). The floor number of the when in a building. Use to reference this key.
satelliteCount (an integer value). The number of satellites used to fix the . Use to reference this key.
transmitterCount (an integer value). The number of transmitters used to create an indoor positioning system (IPS) position. Use to reference this key.
positionSource (a string value). This key can be used to indicate the position source: GNSS, AppleIPS, BLE, WIFI, CELL, IP. Where GNSS indicates global navigation satellite system and AppleIPS is Apple’s indoor positioning technology. It is possible to have multiple position sources. In that case positionSource is a list with comma-separated values. Use to reference this key.
floorLevelId (a string value). Unique ID of the feature stored in the levels in accordance with the ArcGIS Indoors Information Model. Use to reference this key.
Keys are case-sensitive.

member.param: A timestamp when location was received.
The horizontal and vertical map position of the location.
The horizontal accuracy in meters. Positive values or NaN are supported.
The vertical accuracy in meters. Positive values or NaN are supported.
The location's velocity in meters per second.
The location's course in degrees (clockwise), 0 being true North.

If true indicates this is an outdated device position retrieved and cached earlier, and not guaranteed to represent be the current location latest. Setting this to true will render with the , typically a grayed out location symbol.
A set of key-value pairs providing additional meta-data and properties about the source of this location.
