member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.Location.AdditionalSourceProperties
member.summary: Gets a set of key-value pairs providing additional meta-data and properties about the source of this .

remarks.para: A object can be created manually or by a variety of types for example from the default platform data source or external GPS. When user code receives a new , it can be important to determine how the information was obtained.

In addition, mobile devices may provide different details about their in-built location services. For example, Android systems can provide data on the number of satellites used to find a position, whereas on iOS this information is not available. When a given property is not available to the data source the entry should be omitted from the dictionary.

The property provides a means for the creator of a object to record flexible information such as:
The confidence level associated with the location.
The method used to obtain the location.
The provenance of the location.

Information is provided as a set of key-value pairs, where the key describes the type of data held in the value. Values must be basic data types such as string, numeric, boolean or date. The creator of the can use any string for the key - but this API recognizes a number of well known keys which should be used if available. See :

list.item: floor (an integer value). The floor number of the when in a building. Use to reference this key.
satelliteCount (an integer value). The number of satellites used to fix the . Use to reference this key.

positionSource (a string value). This key can be used to indicate the position source: GNSS, AppleIPS, BLE, WIFI, CELL, IP. GNSS indicates global navigation satellite system and AppleIPS is Apple’s indoor positioning technology. It is possible to have multiple position sources. In that case positionSource is a list with comma-separated values. Use to reference this key.

remarks.para: Keys are case-sensitive.
This collection is read-only and can only be assigned via the constructor.

member.value: A set of key-value pairs providing additional meta-data and properties about the source of this Location.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaAccuracyType
member.summary: The list of NMEA accuracy types.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaAccuracyType.Gst
member.summary: An accuracy calculated from GST message.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaAccuracyType.Gsa
member.summary: An accuracy calculated from GSA message.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaAccuracyType.Gga
member.summary: An accuracy calculated from GGA message.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaFixType
member.summary: The list of NMEA fix types.
member.remarks: Indicates the type of signal or technique being used by the GPS receiver to determine its location. For example, it indicates the quality of the signal, or the accuracy and reliability of the location being reported. The fix type is determined by the receiver based on number of satellites visible, the type of GPS receiver and the GPS technology being used.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaFixType.Invalid
member.summary: An invalid GPS fix type, which indicates that there is no satellite signal being received or there are not enough satellites available for proper location determination.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaFixType.Standard
member.summary: A standard GPS fix type, which indicates a standard GPS signal, or that Standard Positioning Service (SPS) is being used. SPS is the standard specified level of positioning and timing accuracy that is available, without qualification or restrictions, to any user on a continuous worldwide basis.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaFixType.Dgps
member.summary: A differential GPS fix type, which indicates that a Differential GPS is being used to provide increased accuracy over SPS. This technique uses a network of fixed ground based reference stations to broadcast the difference between the positions indicated by the satellite systems and the known fixed positions.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaFixType.Pps
member.summary: A PPS GPS fix type, which indicates that Precise Positioning System, encrypted for government use is being used by the receiver. PPS is the most accurate positioning, velocity, and timing information continuously available, worldwide, from the basic GPS.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaFixType.Rtk
member.summary: A real time kinematic GPS fix type, which is a technique used in land survey based on the use of carrier phase measurements of the GPS signals where a single reference station provides the real-time corrections of even to a centimeter level of accuracy.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaFixType.Frtk
member.summary: A float real time kinematic GPS fix type, which is very similar to the fixed RTK () method of calculating location, but is not as precise, typically around 20 cm to 1 meter accuracy range.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaFixType.Estimated
member.summary: An estimated GPS fix type, which is the determination of a location based on computations of position given an accurately known point of origin and measurements of speed, heading and elapsed time.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaFixType.Manual
member.summary: A manual input mode GPS fix type, which indicates that the location has been manually entered into the GPS receiver, and is not based on the satellite system.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaFixType.Simulation
member.summary: A simulation mode GPS fix type.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaGnssSystem
member.summary: Enumeration of supported GNSS (Global Navigation Satellite System) systems.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaGnssSystem.Unknown
member.summary: An unknown GNSS type.
member.remarks: Used if GNSS System ID is unknown or NMEA version before v4.11 is used.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaGnssSystem.Gps
member.summary: The Global Positioning System.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaGnssSystem.Glonass
member.summary: The Russian Global Navigation Satellite System.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaGnssSystem.Galileo
member.summary: The European Union Global Navigation Satellite System.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaGnssSystem.Bds
member.summary: The BeiDou Navigation Satellite System.
member.remarks: BeiDou is a Chinese satellite navigation system.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaGnssSystem.Qzss
member.summary: The Quasi-Zenith Satellite System.
member.remarks: Is a Japanese satellite navigation system.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaGnssSystem.NavIC
member.summary: The Navigation Indian Constellation.
member.remarks: Was renamed from IRNSS (Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System).

member.summary: An NMEA location object.
member.remarks: Contains information about parsed NMEA location.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaLocation.DgpsAge
member.summary: Gets the age of differential GPS data record in TimeSpan, Type 1 or 9, TimeSpan.Zero when DGPS is not used.
member.remarks: The DpgsAge shows the age of the differential data (in TimeSpan) and the time elapsed since the last valid differential correction. Because the validity of the differential correction depends on the difference between the time the differential signal is calculated the time it is used by the receiver, smaller values are better.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaLocation.MagneticVariation
member.summary: Gets the magnetic variation in degrees, or NaN if not available.
member.remarks: Positive values (easterly variation) subtract from the true course and negative values (westerly variation) add to the true course. For example, when an easterly/positive variation occurs, the compass needle points east of true north due to the difference between magnetic north and true north.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaLocationDataSource
member.summary: An NMEA data source object.

remarks.para: This object will parse specific NMEA messages pushed into it. Valid GGA, GSA, GST, GSV, RMC, VTG messages are parsed and, depending upon the message type and contents, a or may be raised. The will return a object. The will return an array of objects. This data source is based on NMEA version 4.11 (November 2018).

The Apple AppStore has guidelines and restrictions for apps that communicate with MFi accessories (for example: an external GPS receiver). Apps that use NMEA data need to satisfy the Apple requirements to be accepted in the store. Apps need to declare support via PPID in their submission and the MFi provider needs to list the bundle ID so Apple can cross reference it. For more information see the site, MFi Program.
Make sure the property is assigned to an NMEA stream prior to starting the datasource.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.remarks: Locations created from an NMEA data source object instantiated with this constructor will be projected from the defined by the parameter to the defined by the parameter using the . If the parameter is null the locations will not be projected and the locations will be in the , if the parameter is set it will be ignored. This constructor can be used when a receiver is outputting locations in a other than WGS84, such as when using a reference base station, and/or the application requires the output locations to be projected to a different using a .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaLocationDataSource.FromAccessory(ExternalAccessory.EAAccessory,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a NMEA LocationDataSource based on an iOS .

remarks.para: The created datasource will automatically handle opening the specified accessory and reading data from it when the datasource is started. Once the datasource has been started, the will be available.
To use the device in an iOS app, ensure the device is supported and enabled in Info.plist. For example this is required to enable support for a Bad Elf GPS receiver:

To obtain a supported accessory use the EAAccessoryManager.SharedAccessoryManager.ConnectedAccessories property and search by name: foreach (var accessory in ExternalAccessory.EAAccessoryManager.SharedAccessoryManager.ConnectedAccessories) { if (accessory.Name == "Bad Elf GNSS Surveyor") { return NmeaLocationDataSource.FromAccessory(accessory, "com.bad-elf.gps"); } }

member.param: The accessory to read NMEA from.
The accessory protocol used to read data. See for a list of supported strings on the accessory.
member.returns: A NMEA Location Datasource that reads from the specified accessory

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaLocationDataSource.FromStreamCreator(System.Func{Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaLocationDataSource,System.Threading.Tasks.Task{System.IO.Stream}},System.Func{Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.NmeaLocationDataSource,System.Threading.Tasks.Task})
member.summary: Returns a that will create the NMEA stream on , and destroy on using the provided start/stop methods.

remarks.para: This helper method is useful for only creating a datasource stream on start and stop, rather than always have the stream running regardless of the datasource state.
remarks.code: var port = new System.IO.Ports.SerialPort("COM1", 9600); NmeaLocationDataSource serialPortDatasource = NmeaLocationDataSource.FromStreamCreator( onStart: (ds) => { port.Open(); return Task.FromResult(port.BaseStream); }, onStop: (ds) => { port.Close(); return Task.CompletedTask; } ); MyMapView.LocationDisplay.DataSource = serialPortDatasource; MyMapView.LocationDisplay.IsEnabled = true; // This will trigger onStart to get called
remarks.para: As an alternative to this approach, you can also subclass and override the , and methods.

member.param: This method is called when the datasource starts, and should return a that points to the NMEA datasource.
This method is called when the datasource stops, and can be used to clean up resources.
member.returns: An NmeaLocationDataSource.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.RouteTrackerLocationDataSource
member.summary: Represents a location data source that uses a and a generic to output locations snapped to a route.

remarks.para: Setting the route tracker location data source is optional when desiring to have the location display symbol navigate along a route. Using this object provides the benefit of having the current location snapped to the route polyline during navigation. If the route tracker location data source is not used, you may notice that the actual GPS location tends to wander off the route polyline due to the accuracy provided by the GPS unit.

No distance tolerance needs to be specified to enable the snapping effect of having the location display symbol follow along the route polyline. An internal algorithm is used by the route tracker location data source to determine if location is 'on route' or 'off route'. If the location is seen as 'on route' then snapping will occur, otherwise the output location will be the original input GPS location. If it is determined that the tracking along the route is no longer needed the route tracker location data source's stop method can be called or the can be reset to a different data source.
To provide some insight on how the RouteTrackerLocationDataSource works, the data source will pass the locations from the event to the . The will then be returned in this data source's event.
To control the orientation of the location display symbol as it navigates along a route refer to the class.

The location collection can be derived from vertices in a provided , generated evenly along a polyline based on a travel velocity, or assigned to the collection directly.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Location.SimulatedLocationDataSource.IterationRate
member.summary: Gets or sets the rate over which the collection is iterated.
member.remarks: This controls the frequency of location updates for the simulated location data source. The default value is 1.0, resulting in calls to every second. To increase the frequency, provide a higher value. A value of 2.0, for example, doubles the frequency of location updates. To slow down updates, provide a lower value. A value of 0.5, for example, decreases location updates by a factor of two. The iteration rate does not affect the property of the items in the (they are always 1 second apart).
member.value: Rate at which the is iterated over (default is 1.0)

member.summary: Not reached destination status.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Navigation.DestinationStatus.Approaching
member.summary: Approaching destination status.
member.remarks: Value returned when provided GPS location gets within 500 meters of the next destination. This status can be used to manually call if a vehicle can not get closer to a destination to trigger a status of .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Navigation.DestinationStatus.Reached
member.summary: Reached destination status.
member.remarks: Value returned when provided GPS location gets within 7-40 meters, along the route, of the next destination. Once within 40 meters of the next destination the vehicle is tracked to determine if it is getting closer or moving away from the next destination. If the vehicle continues to get closer this value will be returned when within 7 meter of the next destination, if the vehicle starts to move away this value is returned. This status can be used to automatically call .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Navigation.ReroutingParameters
member.summary: Represents the parameters that are used to perform automatic re-routing if off-route while navigating along a route.

remarks.para: This class defines how automatic re-routing calculations will be performed if off-route is detected while navigating along a route. To enable re-routing, call the method passing in this object as the argument.

In order to be considered off-route, the location must be on the transportation network as well as off the current route. If a tracked location is in a parking lot, for example, it is not considered off-route and rerouting will not occur. If the next location is on the network but not on the route, automatic rerouting will begin.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Navigation.ReroutingParameters.#ctor(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.RouteTask,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.RouteParameters)

remarks.para: The following properties are ignored: , , , , , , and .
An exception is thrown if the or are null.

member.param: A capable of solving routes on the same network used by the original route, usually the same that was used to create the route being tracked. Used for rerouting in background thread.
The route parameters.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Navigation.ReroutingParameters.NetworkEdgeGeometries
member.summary: Gets or sets an containing the geometries that the 's network is based on. During the re-routing the geometries are used to determine if the current location is on or near the navigation network. The re-routing calculation will be skipped if the current location is off the network. The geometry type of the feature table must . Default value is null.

remarks.para: Setting this property is only needed when the for the navigation is based on a service. When the is based on local data on the device, access to the geometries is available to the re-routing algorithm automatically. The can be the original network source data or one created specifically for checking if located on an edge. If there are multiple sources, the geometries should be merged into a single table.
Only the geometries are needed in the , not the attributes.
Online feature service should support pagination, otherwise will throw an exception. To improve performance online feature service should have a >= 2000 (this decreases the count of online queries).

member.value: An or null.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Navigation.RouteTracker
member.summary: Uses a location to provide status and progress updates as a route is traversed (by a moving vehicle, for example).

remarks.para: RouteTracker can give the time or distance to the next maneuver, notify if the location is off-route, and regenerate a new route if necessary.
Basic workflow:

list.item: Create a new instance.
Enable rerouting via (if supported by the underlying route service).
Use to track the location of the device.
Subscribe to event to get the as the location changes. For example, this can be used to show in the GeoView.
Subscribe to to get the whenever new instructions are available.
If there are multiple stops, call each time status is returned. Before calling make sure that > 1 (a value of 1 means the last destination is being approached).

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Navigation.RouteTracker.#ctor(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.RouteResult,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class using a specific route from a route result with an option to specify treating coincident stops as a single destination.
member.remarks: An exception is thrown if the is not found or if it lacks or .
member.param: A generated from route task solve. The must contain at least one (with and .
The zero-based index into the list of , selecting a to track. The selected must contain and .

true if coincident stops (multiple stops in a row at the same location) should be treated as a single destination, false otherwise. When true, if there are multiple stops in a row at the same network location, the new voice guidance event will fire a single time and calling will skip over the other coincident stops, setting the state to the next relevant direction maneuver. If false, each stop will be treated as a unique destination. If your route does not have coincident stops, this parameter will have no effect.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Navigation.RouteTracker.IsReroutingEnabled

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Navigation.RouteTracker.SkipCoincidentStops
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether multiple stops in a row at the same location should be treated as one stop.
member.remarks: When true, if there are multiple stops in a row at the same network location, the new voice guidance event will fire a single time and calling will skip over the other coincident stops, setting the state to the next relevant direction maneuver. If the value is false each stop will be treated as a unique destination. The default value for this property is true. If your route does not have coincident stops the value of this property will have no effect.
member.value: Indicates if multiple stops in a row at the same location should treated as one stop

member.summary: Enables automatic rerouting when the tracker detects an off-route status.
member.remarks: Re-routing only occurs when the current location is off-route, but on or near the transportation network edges. For example if an off-route state is detected but the tracked location is in a parking lot, away from any transportation network edges, re-routing will not occur, and will occur only once a subsequent tracked location is on or near the transportation network edges.
member.param: A object that allows customizing background rerouting.
Optional token that may be used to cancel the async enabling operation.
member.returns: The representing the asynchronous operation to enable re-routing.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Navigation.TrackingDistance
member.summary: Defines tracking distance.
member.remarks: Tracking distance contains two types of distances - raw and display. Raw distance is always in meters (fixed units). Display distance is converted to units of .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Navigation.TrackingDistance.DisplayText
member.summary: Gets the formatted and rounded distance, ready for displaying.

remarks.para: Use this property to have consistent distances/units with voice guidance. The distance is converted to units of . The following rules are used for rounding and formatting:

Imperial Units:
term description
0 -> 50' Round to increments of 10' (e.g. "10", "20", "30", "40")
50' -> 0.1 mi (528') Round to increments of 50' (e.g. "50", "100", "150", etc.)
0.1 -> 1.0 mi Round to decimal mile (e.g "0.1", "0.2", "0.3", etc.)
1.0 mi -> 10 mi Round to half mile (e.g. "1.0", "1.5", "2.0", etc.)
10+ mi Round to mile (e.g. "10", "11", "12", etc.)

Metric Units:
term description
0 -> 50 m Round to increments of 10 m (e.g. "10", "20", "30", "40")
50 m -> 500 m Round to increments of 50 m (e.g. "50", "100", "150", etc.)
500 m -> 10 km Round to half km (e.g. "1.0", "1.5", "2.0", etc.)
10+ km Round to whole km (e.g. "10", "11", "12", etc.)

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.ArcGISPortal
member.summary: The entry point into the ArcGIS portal services hosted by ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise.

remarks.para:
ArcGIS portals are geospatial content management systems from Esri that customers can use to securely store, manage, and access different types of content. There are two types of portals:
ArcGIS Online portal - a service hosted by Esri that you can access with a ArcGIS Developer account or ArcGIS Online account.
ArcGIS Enterprise portal - a service hosted by ArcGIS Enterprise that you can access with an ArcGIS Enterprise account.

The class is the main entry point into the ArcGIS Portal Directory REST API that allows you to work with users, groups and content hosted within ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Enterprise portals. Once instantiated, you can use the to search for items and groups and see portal content, such as services, layers, and so on.
For more information about authenticating your app or its users with a portal, see the Introduction to security and authentication topic in the developer guide.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.ArcGISPortal.FindGroupsAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalQueryParameters)
member.summary: Finds portal groups from a query string.

remarks.para: This method obtains a object. Use the property to get the collection of objects.

objects are collections (aka. groups) of items, usually related to a specific area of interest such as maps, applications, and tools. Groups allow members to contribute items as a useful way to collaborate. This method allows searching for specific groups within ArcGIS Online (AGOL) or ArcGIS Portal and requires providing specific to limit the scope of which groups are returned.

You do not have to be a logged in user to get information from this method. You can gain this information from anonymous access. However, to obtain information about groups in your organization that have a level of Private you need to use the by supplying the appropriate username/password credentials.

By default the property that is used by this method uses a default set of high performance index fields when providing simple keyword string(s) for the search. As of AGOL/ArcGIS Portal v2.1, these fields are: id, title, description, snippet, tags, and owner. For more advanced searches, developers can preface their simple keyword strings with a more exhaustive set of REST named fields followed by a colon (:). These advanced searches can also take advantage of Boolean operators (AND, NOT, OR, +), wildcard operators (* and ?), as well as other techniques to more fully utilize the AGOL/ArcGIS Portal search capabilities. Comprehensive details on using the advanced search strings that follow the REST specification can be found in the Search Reference document in the ArcGIS Portal API.
The following are a few advanced REST search scenarios with example strings that you might consider using for the property:

To find one specific PortalGroup by it's Id: "id:1db70a32f5f84ea9a88f5f460f22557b"
To find all Private PortalGroups with the word 'federal' in the Title field: "title:federal AND access:private"
To find all PortalGroups with the word 'federal' in the Title field and the word 'restricted' in the Description field: "title:federal AND description:restricted"
To find all PortalGroups with the word 'state' in the Title field and not have the word 'restricted' in the Description field: "title:state NOT description:restricted"

NOTE: It is not required to use the advanced REST search syntax to find PortalGroups with this Method. Simple string syntax for the Portal.SearchParameters.QueryString Property such as: "federal" will find all occurrences of that string in the default high performance index fields.

member.param: The search parameters.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous find groups operation. The value of the task result is a .

member.summary: Finds portal users that match the query parameters specified.

remarks.para: This method obtains a object that contains objects. Use the property to get the collection of objects.

objects are user accounts within ArcGIS Online (AGOL) or ArcGIS Portal. Users add content to AGOL/ArcGIS Portal in the form of maps, tools, and applications. This method allows searching for specific user accounts within ArcGIS Online (AGOL) or ArcGIS Portal and requires providing specific to limit the scope of which users are returned.

You do not have to be a logged in user to get information from this method. You can gain this information from anonymous access. However, to obtain information about users in your organization that have a level of Private you need to use the AuthenticationManager by supplying the appropriate username/password credentials.

NOTE: While the method does return an collection of objects as result of the search operation, not all of the properties of the objects will contain data. This is by design to protect certain confidential information about a user.

member.param: The search parameters.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous search users operation.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.ArcGISPortal.FindItemsAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalQueryParameters)
member.summary: Finds portal items that match the query parameters specified.

remarks.para: This method obtains a object that contains objects. Use the property to get the collection of objects.

Portal.PortalItem objects are user items, usually related to a specific area of interest such as maps, applications, and tools. This method allows searching for specific items within ArcGIS Online (AGOL) or ArcGIS Portal by providing to limit the scope of which items are returned.

You do not have to be a logged in user to get information from this method. You can gain this information from anonymous access. However, to obtain information about items that have a level of Private you need to use the AuthenticationManager by supplying the appropriate username/password credentials.

By default the property that is used in this method uses a default set of high performance index fields when providing simple keyword string(s) for the search. As of AGOL/ArcGIS Portal v2.1, these fields are: title, tags, snippet, description, accessinformation, spatialreference, type and typekeywords. For more advanced searches, developers can preface their simple keyword strings with a more exhaustive set of REST named fields followed by a colon (:). These advanced searches can also take advantage of Boolean operators (AND, NOT, OR, +), wildcard operators (* and ?), as well as other techniques to more fully utilize the AGOL/ArcGIS Portal search capabilities. Comprehensive details on using the advanced search strings that follow the REST specification can be found in the Search Reference document in the ArcGIS Portal API.
The following are a few advanced REST search scenarios with example strings that you might consider using for the property:

To find one specific PortalItem by it's Id: "id:00e5e70929e14055ab686df16c842ec1"
To find all WebMap PortalItems with the word 'florida' in the Title field: "title:florida AND type:web map"
To find all Layer Package PortalItems with the word 'florida' in the Title field: "title:florida AND type:layer package"
To find all PortalItems with the word 'florida' in the Title field and the word 'esri' in the LicenseInfo field: "title:florida AND licenseinfo:esri"
To find all PortalItems with the word 'florida' in the Title field and not have the word 'esri' in the LicenseInfo field: "title:florida NOT licenseinfo:esri"
To find all PortalItems with located within a specific spatial extent and the word 'esri' in the LicenseInfo: "extent:[-114.3458, 21.7518] - [-73.125, 44.0658] AND licenseinfo:esri"
To find all PortalItems with a SpatialReference.WKID of 4267: "spatialreference:4267"
To find all PortalItems with a SpatialReference.WKID of 4267 or have the word 'cancer' in the Title field: "spatialreference:4267 OR title:cancer"

NOTE: It is not required to use the advanced REST search syntax to find PortalItems with this method. Simple string syntax for the property such as: "florida" will find all occurrences of that string in the default high performance index fields.

member.param: The search parameters.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous search items operation. The value of the task result is a object.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.ArcGISPortal.FindItemsAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalQueryParameters,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Finds portal items that match the query parameters specified

remarks.para: This version of the Method obtains a object that contains objects. Use the property to get the collection of objects.

Portal.PortalItem objects are user items, usually related to a specific area of interest such as maps, applications, and tools. This version of the Portal.ArcGISPortal.SearchItemsAsync Method allows searching for specific items within ArcGIS Online (AGOL) or ArcGIS Portal by providing to limit the scope of which items are returned.

You can call this version of the Method before calling the one of the ArcGISPortal.CreateAsync Methods and obtain information but the property will not be populated with valid information (i.e. its value will be Nothing/null). It is a recommended best practice to call one of the ArcGISPortal.CreateAsync Methods before calling this method so that all of the properties in the will contain valid data.

You do not have to be a logged in user to get information from this version of the Portal.ArcGISPortal.SearchItemsAsync Method. You can gain this information from anonymous access. However, to obtain information about items that have a level of Private you need to use the AuthenticationManager by supplying the appropriate username/password credentials.

By default the property that is used in this version of the Portal.ArcGISPortal.SearchItemsAsync Method uses a default set of high performance index fields when providing simple keyword string(s) for the search. As of AGOL/ArcGIS Portal v2.1, these fields are: title, tags, snippet, description, accessinformation, spatialreference, type and typekeywords. For more advanced searches, developers can preface their simple keyword strings with a more exhaustive set of REST named fields followed by a colon (:). These advanced searches can also take advantage of Boolean operators (AND, NOT, OR, +), wildcard operators (* and ?), as well as other techniques to more fully utilize the AGOL/ArcGIS Portal search capabilities. Comprehensive details on using the advanced search strings that follow the REST specification can be found in the Search Reference document in the ArcGIS Portal API.
The following are a few advanced REST search scenarios with example strings that you might consider using for the Portal.SearchParameters.QueryString Property:

To find one specific PortalItem by it's Id: "id:00e5e70929e14055ab686df16c842ec1"
To find all WebMap PortalItems with the word 'florida' in the Title field: "title:florida AND type:web map"
To find all Layer Package PortalItems with the word 'florida' in the Title field: "title:florida AND type:layer package"
To find all PortalItems with the word 'florida' in the Title field and the word 'esri' in the LicenseInfo field: "title:florida AND licenseinfo:esri"
To find all PortalItems with the word 'florida' in the Title field and not have the word 'esri' in the LicenseInfo field: "title:florida NOT licenseinfo:esri"
To find all PortalItems with located within a specific spatial extent and the word 'esri' in the LicenseInfo: "extent:[-114.3458, 21.7518] - [-73.125, 44.0658] AND licenseinfo:esri"
To find all PortalItems with a SpatialReference.WKID of 4267: "spatialreference:4267"
To find all PortalItems with a SpatialReference.WKID of 4267 or have the word 'cancer' in the Title field: "spatialreference:4267 OR title:cancer"

NOTE: It is not required to use the advanced REST search syntax to find PortalItems with this version of the Method. Simple string syntax for the Portal.SearchParameters.QueryString Property such as: "florida" will find all occurrences of that string in the default high performance index fields.

member.param: The search parameters.
A with which to cancel the operation if required.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous search items operation. The value of the task result contains a object that contains objects. Use the Property to get collection.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.ArcGISPortal.GetLoginTypeForUriAsync(System.Uri)
member.summary: Retrieves the for the specified portal Uri.
member.param: The portal Uri

remarks.para: For portals protected by multiple layers of authentication, this method returns the login type for the outermost layer that still requires credentials. Once credentials are provided for a layer, the method will then return the requirements of the next inner layer.
For example: When accessing an Enterprise portal that sits behind a certificate-protected proxy, initially this method will return . After a valid was added to the , it will return instead.

member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous operation. The value of the task result is a object.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemRelationship.MapToService
member.summary: Relates a map with a service.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemRelationship.WmaToCode
member.summary: Relates a web mapping application (WMA) with a code attachment.
member.remarks: All related items are treated as one item when shared or deleted, and the destination item inherits the sharing permissions of the origin item.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemRelationship.MapToFeatureCollection
member.summary: Relates a map with a feature collection.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemRelationship.MobileAppToCode
member.summary: Relates a mobile application with a code attachment.
member.remarks: All related items are treated as one item when shared or deleted, and the destination item inherits the sharing permissions of the origin item.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemRelationship.ServiceToData
member.summary: Relates a derived service and its source data.

remarks.para: The derived service is the origin of the relationship, and the source data is the destination of the relationship. The source data can be a service. This relationship can be queried in both directions.
The creator of this relationship must own all related items.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemRelationship.ServiceToService
member.summary: Relates a source service and a derived service.

remarks.para: The source service is the origin of the relationship, and the derived service is the destination of the relationship. This relationship can be queried in both directions.
The creator of the relationship must own all related items.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemRelationship.MapToAppConfig
member.summary: Relates a web map to an application configuration.
member.remarks: The creator of the relationship must own all related items, all related items are treated as one item when shared or deleted, and the destination item inherits all sharing permissions of the origin item.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemRelationship.ItemToAttachment
member.summary: Relates an item to an attachment.
member.remarks: The creator of the relationship must own all related items, and all related items are treated as one item when shared or deleted.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemRelationship.ItemToReport
member.summary: Relates an item to a report.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemRelationship.ListedToProvisioned
member.summary: Relates a listed item to a provisioned item.

remarks.para: Both the source (parent) and destination (child) items must be listed: the parent publicly and the child privately.
Additionally, the creator of the relationship must own all related items, and all related items are treated as one item when deleted.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemRelationship.StyleToStyle
member.summary: Relates a vector tile service to a style or vector tile service.
member.remarks: The creator of the relationship must own the destination item.

member.remarks: The creator of the relationship must own the destination item.

member.remarks: The creator of the relationship must own the origin item.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.InsightsScript
member.summary: An Insights Script is JSON that allows parameterized code created in the Insights console to be shared as an item and consumed in Insights in order for the user to run the script using different Insights data sets and fields as input parameters.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.KernelGatewayConnection
member.summary: A Kernel Gateway Connection is JSON that creates a connection string from Insights in order for the Insights console to connect and execute code against a Jupyter Kernel Gateway.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.KnowledgeGraph
member.summary: A Knowledge Graph enables you to connect, open, and access graph data so that it can be used in Link Charts and Maps.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.LinkChart
member.summary: A Link Chart enables you to visualize and perform link analysis using entities and relationships from a Knowledge Graph so that you may better understand their connectivity.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.Style
member.summary: A web style hosted on an ArcGIS portal.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.AdministrativeReport
member.summary: An administrative report in CSV format that outlines various organization usage activities. This cannot be shared to the public.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.ExportPackage
member.summary: A package that allows administrators or group owners to export a group, and its content, into a downloadable package (.epk file).

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.GeoJSON
member.summary: An open standard geospatial data interchange format that represents simple geographic features and their nonspatial attributes. Based on JavaScript Object Notation (JSON), GeoJSON is a format for encoding a variety of geographic data structures.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.GML
member.summary: Geography Markup Language (GML) file (.zip). An XML grammar defined by the OGC for expressing geographical features, serving as both a modeling language and an interchange format.

member.summary: A central premium capability within ArcGIS Hub Premium, empowering customers and their trusted community members to create, edit, and manage in-person/online events.

member.summary: A package of multiple solutions related to a specific project or goal.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.StoryMapTheme
member.summary: A set of fonts, colors, block styles, and other design elements that define the appearance of a StoryMap.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.WebAppBuilderWidget
member.summary: A custom widget (available via marketplace) is a configurable and shareable functional unit for use in ArcGIS Web AppBuilder. It will allow administrators to enable the use of a custom widget in Web AppBuilder UI so an organization can leverage the ArcGIS Online platform to host their applications.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItemType.EarthConfiguration
member.summary: An ArcGIS Earth configuration file is an exportable file containing all major settings in Earth. Earth users who have access to this file can customize the Earth settings by modifying this file. An Earth configuration file is saved as an .xml file. Users need to compress and zip the file before sharing. This zip file can be distributed and imported to Earth deployments.

member.summary: An unknown portal privilege type.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.FullEdit
member.summary: Grants the ability to add, delete, and update features in a hosted feature layer regardless of the editing options enabled on the layer.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.Manage
member.summary: Grants the ability to create listings, list items and manage subscriptions in ArcGIS Marketplace.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.Purchase
member.summary: Grants the ability to request purchase information about apps and data in ArcGIS Marketplace.

member.summary: Grants the ability to start trial subscriptions in ArcGIS Marketplace.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.OpenDataAdmin

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.ChangeUserRoles

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.DisableUsers

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.InviteUsers
member.summary: Grants the ability to invite members to the organization.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.ManageLicenses
member.summary: Grants the ability to assign licenses to members of the organization.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.UpdateGroups
member.summary: Grants the ability to update groups within the organization.

member.summary: Grants the ability to update and categorize content within the organization.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.UpdateUsers
member.summary: Grants the ability to update member account information within the organization.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.ViewGroups
member.summary: Grants the ability to view all groups within the organization.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.ViewItems
member.summary: Grants the ability to view all content within the organization.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.ViewUsers

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.CreateGroup
member.summary: Grants the ability for a member to create, edit, and delete their own groups.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.CreateItem
member.summary: Grants the ability for a member to create, edit, and delete their own content.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.JoinGroup
member.summary: Grants the ability to join groups within the organization.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.JoinNonOrgGroup

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.ShareToOrg

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalPrivilegeType.ShareToPublic
member.summary: For a user, grants the ability to share content to all users of the portal. For an administrator, grants the ability to share other member's content to all users of the portal.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.HelperServices
member.summary: Various services needed by clients.

remarks.para: This class contains properties to obtain information (most importantly the Url) for various special asynchronous web services available on ArcGIS Online (AGOL) or ArcGIS Portal. The HelperServices.GeocodeServices Property provides information for geocoding (aka. locator) tasks. The HelperServices.GeometryService Property provides information for various geometry tasks (for example: buffer, project, intersect, etc.). The HelperServices.PrintTaskService Property provides information for printing tasks. The HelperServices.RouteService Property provides information for routing tasks. To obtain a Portal.HelperServices object, use the Portal.ArcGISPortalInfo.HelperServices Property.

You do not have to be a logged in user using the AuthenticationManager to get information for the object. You can gain this information from anonymous access. However, to obtain information about the Portal.ArcGISPortalInfo object that has a level of Private or Shared you need to use the AuthenticationManager by supplying the appropriate username/password credentials.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.Item.UpdateItemPropertiesAsync
member.summary: Executes an asynchronous operation to update any item properties that have been changed.

remarks.para: This method causes the values that have been changed to be updated on the portal (for ) or on disk (for ).

For , this method updates the item's properties on the portal asynchronously. This update item operation allows users to update item information without changing the item content. Users can use this operation to update item information such as the title, description, tags, thumbnail, etc. This call is available to the item owner and the administrator of the organization.

For , this method updates the item's properties in a file on disk where applicable. Updating properties can be only done for local items that are unpacked. For example a mobile map package file (.mmpk) can be unpacked using . Once unpacked, local items within the package can be updated see and for any maps use . As well as packages, the local item available from an can be updated. This is accessed with . If the package is not unpacked an exception is thrown by the async task.

member.returns: A representing the asynchronous update operation.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.Item.UpdateItemPropertiesAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Executes an asynchronous operation to update any item properties that have been changed.

remarks.para: This method causes the values that have been changed to be updated on the portal (for ) or on disk (for ).

For , this method updates the item's properties on the portal asynchronously. This update item operation allows users to update item information without changing the item content. Users can use this operation to update item information such as the title, description, tags, thumbnail, etc. This call is available to the item owner and the administrator of the organization.

For , this method updates the item's properties in a file on disk where applicable. Updating properties can be only done for local items that are unpacked. For example a mobile map package file (.mmpk) can be unpacked using . Once unpacked, local items within the package can be updated see and for any maps use . As well as packages, the local item available from an can be updated. This is accessed with . If the package is not unpacked an exception is thrown by the async task.

member.param: A with which to cancel the operation if required.
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous update operation.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroup
member.summary: Represents a group within the portal (e.g., "San Bernardino Fires" ).

remarks.para: Collections of fully populated PortalGroup objects can be obtained by calling the , , and methods, and from the property. The load status of such objects is .
Alternatively a PortalGroup object can be constructed using if the group ID is known. In that case the load status is initially and the object needs to be loaded to populate its properties.

The portal group owner is automatically an administrator and is returned in the list of admins. The access property determines the visibility of the group to other users. If the group is private no one except the administrators and the members of the group can see it. If the group is shared with an organization, then all members of the organization see the group.

Administrators can invite, add to, and remove members from a group, and also update or delete the group. The administrator for an organization may also reassign the group to another member of the organization. Group members may leave the group. Authenticated users may apply to join a group.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroup.GetGroupUsersAsync
member.summary: Gets the users, owner, and administrators of the group.

remarks.para: This Method obtains a object which provides user information for a single such as: the usernames of the administrators of the group, and the usernames of the members who can access information in the group. objects are collections (aka. groups) of items, usually related to a specific area of interest such as maps, applications, and tools. Groups allow members to contribute items as a useful way to collaborate.

You do not have to be a logged in user to get information from this method. You can gain this information from anonymous access. However, to obtain information about a group that has a level of Private or Shared you need to use the AuthenticationManager by supplying the appropriate username/password credentials. As AGOL and ArcGIS Portal require the use of long term tokens to access secured information (i.e. information that is Private/Shared).

member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous get group users operation. The value of the task result is a object.

remarks.para: This Method obtains a object which provides user information for a single such as: the usernames of the administrators of the group, and the usernames of the members who can access information in the group. objects are collections (aka. groups) of items, usually related to a specific area of interest such as maps, applications, and tools. Groups allow members to contribute items as a useful way to collaborate.

You do not have to be a logged in user to get information from this method. You can gain this information from anonymous access. However, to obtain information about a group that has a level of Private or Shared you need to use the AuthenticationManager by supplying the appropriate username/password credentials. As AGOL and ArcGIS Portal require the use of long term tokens to access secured information (i.e. information that is Private/Shared).

member.param: Token that may be used to cancel the async operation.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous get group users operation. The value of the task result is a object.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroup.FindItemsAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroupContentSearchParameters)
member.summary: Finds portal items that belong to this group and match the given search parameters.

remarks.para: This method uses a Group Content Search operation that's designed specifically for finding items that belong to a group. It differs from the method which uses a general-purpose Search operation that's suitable for searching all items stored on a portal. The behavior of this method also differs in the following ways:

list.item: If the property is used, the categories specified there will be matched with group categories that have been specified by the group owner/administrator, as opposed to the organization-level categories that the general-purpose Search operation matches against.
An extra option is available, "added", to sort results by the time the items were added to this group.

member.param: The search parameters
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous search operation. The value of the task result is a that contains the objects that were found.

list.item: If the property is used, the categories specified there will be matched with group categories that have been specified by the group owner/administrator, as opposed to the organization-level categories that the general-purpose Search operation matches against.
An extra option is available, "added", to sort results by the time the items were added to this group.

member.param: The search parameters
A with which to cancel the operation, if required.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous search operation. The value of the task result is a that contains the objects that were found.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroupContentSearchParameters
member.summary: Contains search parameters suitable for finding portal items that belong to a .
member.remarks:
You can create an instance using one of the factory methods that are designed for particular types of searches, or you can use one of the constructors and specify a custom query string. A number of properties are also available to customize various aspects of the search. A typical usage pattern is:
(1) Create a new object using a factory method or a constructor.
(2) Optionally set one or more of the properties.
(3) Pass the parameters to and await the results.
(4) Optionally use to retrieve the next page of search results.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroupContentSearchParameters.Query
member.summary: Gets the query string used to search for items.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroupContentSearchParameters.SortField
member.summary: Gets or sets a comma-separated string containing one or more field names to sort the results by.
member.remarks: Field names are case-insensitive. Supported field names are: "title", "created", "type", "owner", "modified", "added", "avgRating", "numRatings", "numComments", and "numViews".

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroupContentSearchParameters.SortOrder
member.summary: Gets or sets the sort order, indicating whether the results are returned in ascending or descending order.
member.remarks: Default behavior is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroupContentSearchParameters.StartIndex
member.summary: Gets or sets the index (within the entire set of results) of the first in the current result set.
member.remarks: The index number is 1-based. The default value is 1.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroupContentSearchParameters.Limit
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of results to be included in the result set response.

remarks.para: The limit, along with the , can be used to paginate the search results.
The default value is 10, and the maximum allowed value is 100.
Note that the actual number of returned results may be less than limit. This happens when the number of results remaining after the is less than .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroupContentSearchParameters.Categories
member.summary: Gets a list of content category specifications to use when searching for items.

remarks.para: Each entry in the list is a string containing a comma-separated list of up to 8 group content categories. The exact full path of each category is required, and an OR relationship is applied between the categories within a particular string.
There can be up to 8 strings with an AND relationship being applied between the different strings.
For example, to search for items belonging to either the Water or Forest categories, both within the US, specify 2 strings as follows: "/Categories/Water,/Categories/Forest" and "/Region/US".

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroupContentSearchParameters.CanSearchPublic
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether public items outside the organization may be included in the search results.
member.remarks: Public items are included by default.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroupContentSearchParameters.BoundingBox
member.summary: Gets or sets an specifying the bounding box for a spatial search.

remarks.para: Spatial search is an overlaps/intersects function of the bounding box and the extent of the document. Documents that have no extent (e.g., mxds, 3dds, lyr) will not be found when doing a bounding box search.
Only bounding boxes with geographic coordinate system are supported. If property is not set, coordinates are assumed to be in WGS84.

If the user is logged in or if the Url is an organization custom Url, the information will be specific to the organization that the user belongs to. The default view of the portal is dependent on the culture of the user which is obtained from the users profile.
You may get the current portal info by the property.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItem
member.summary: An item (a unit of content) stored in an ArcGIS portal, such as a layer, web map, package file, or map service.

remarks.para:
ArcGIS portals support various types of portal items, including:
Web maps and scenes.
Services such as feature, map, scene, image, and OGC services that can be added as layers to a map.
Data files that can be uploaded, stored, and downloaded, such as .mmpk or .vtpk files.
Applications and tools.

Each portal item contains information about the item such as its unique ID, the owning , and the type of item it is (). Portal items can be kept private to the users that created them, shared with other users in specific groups, or made public and accessible to everyone.

You can use the portal item ID along with a portal URL to quickly instantiate a new portal item object. The item's details page has the following URL format: https://www.arcgis.com/home/item.html?id=[item_id]. If your portal item is a web map, you can also obtain a URL with the format: https://www.arcgis.com/home/webmap/viewer.html?webmap=[item_id] from the ArcGIS Online Map Viewer. In both cases, you can use the [item_id] as the ID to instantiate a portal item.
Alternatively, you can obtain a portal item directly from a portal using a method such as .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItem.Access
member.summary: Gets or sets the access level on the item. Private is the default and only the item owner can access. Shared allows the item to be shared with a specific group. Setting to Organization restricts item access to members of your organization. If Public, all users can access the item.

remarks.para: A can be accessed by various types of users. There are four levels of access that can be associated with a . Some of the access levels can be concurrent, others cannot. The Property however only returns a single PortalAccess level enumeration even though multiple levels of access may have been specified via the and/or Methods. If multiple access levels have been specified on a , the returned access level from the Property will be the least restrictive access level. The following diagram illustrates the hierarchy of access levels:

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItem.FolderId
member.summary: Gets or sets the ID of the folder in which the owner has stored the item. The ID is only returned to the item owner or an administrator for the organization.
member.remarks: A null folderID means either that the folderID is unknown to the user or the item is stored at the root folder of the user directory.
member.value: The owner folder ID.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItem.AverageRating

member.remarks: Uses a weighted average called "Bayesian average".

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItem.UnknownJson
member.summary: Gets unknown data from the source JSON.
member.remarks: Unknown JSON is a dictionary of values not defined in the ArcGIS specification used to create this object but found in the source JSON. If the object is written back to JSON, any unknown JSON data is not persisted. The ArcGIS specification may be for a web map, web scene, REST API, and so on.

If the object is written back to JSON, any unsupported JSON data is persisted. The ArcGIS specification may be from a web map, web scene, REST API, and so on.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItem.ShareWithAsync(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Shares the portal item with everyone or just with the user's organization. If both 'everyone' and 'organization' arguments are true, the item will be shared with everyone (). If both 'everyone' and 'organization' arguments are false, the item will be made private, unless the item has been shared with one or more groups. In that case, the items property will be set to . The groups an item has been shared with are not affected by this method. This Method is available only to authenticated users.

remarks.para: In order to use a user must be logged into the portal site with proper credentials.
The individual who is authenticated and logged into the portal site that desires to use to establish which Groups (via their values) the PortalItem is part of MUST meet one of the following criteria:

list.item: The authenticated user is the owner of the PortalItem.
The authenticated user is the Administrator of the Organization to which the PortalItem belongs.

The PortalItem has its value of Public and the authenticated user is the owner of the Group. Note: the PortalItem may not have been created/owned by the authenticated user; it is only important that the PortalItem be Public, no matter who created/owns the PortalItem.

The PortalItem has its PortalAccess value of Public and the authenticated user is the Administrator of the Organization to which Group belongs. Note: the PortalItem may not have been created/owned by the authenticated user; it is only important that the PortalItem be Public, no matter who created/owns the PortalItem.

member.param: If true, this item will be shared with everyone, for example, it will be publicly accessible. If false, the item will not be shared with the public.
If true, this item will be shared with the organization. If false, the item will not be shared with the organization.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous share operation.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItem.ShareWithAsync(System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)

The PortalItem has its value of Public and the authenticated user is the owner of the Group. Note: the PortalItem may not have been created/owned by the authenticated user; it is only important that the PortalItem be Public, no matter who created/owns the PortalItem.

The PortalItem has its PortalAccess value of Public and the authenticated user is the Administrator of the Organization to which Group belongs. member.param: If true, this item will be shared with everyone, for example, it will be publicly accessible. If false, the item will not be shared with the public.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItem.ShareWithGroupsAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalGroup})
member.summary: Shares this PortalItem with the specified list of . This Method is available only to authenticated users.

remarks.para: In order to use the PortalItem.ShareAsync Method, a user must be logged into the ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Portal site with proper credentials.

The individual who is authenticated and logged into the portal site that desires to use the PortalItem.ShareAsync Method to establish which Groups (via their values) the PortalItem is part of MUST meet one of the following criteria:

list.item: The authenticated user is the owner of the PortalItem.
The authenticated user is the Administrator of the Organization to which the PortalItem belongs.

The PortalItem has its value of Public and the authenticated user is the owner of the Group. Note: the PortalItem may not have been created/owned by the authenticated user; it is only important that the PortalItem be Public, no matter who created/owns the PortalItem.

The PortalItem has its PortalAccess value of Public and the authenticated user is the Administrator of the Organization to which Group belongs. Note: the PortalItem may not have been created/owned by the authenticated user; it is only important that the PortalItem be Public, no matter who created/owns the PortalItem.

remarks.para: NOTE: Setting the first parameter (groupIds) of the PortalItem.ShareAsync to nothing does not set any existing Group values. In other words, it does not empty the list of Groups associated with the PortalItem. The way to remove existing Group values from the PortalItem is to use the method.

Using this version of the PortalItem.ShareAsync only modifies Groups for the PortalAccess level of Shared. If it is desired to add or change the other PortalAccess levels for the PortalItem, consider using the other version: .

member.param: The collection of groups to share the item with.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous share operation. The value of the task result is a collection of objects with which the item was NOT shared.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalQueryParameters.Query
member.summary: Gets or sets the query string to search against.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalQueryParameters.SortField
member.summary: Gets or sets the field to sort by. (e.g for groups the allowed field names are title, owner, and created).
member.remarks: Sort field names are case-insensitive. Supported field names are "title", "created", "type", "owner", "modified", "avgrating", "numratings", "numcomments", and "numviews"

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalQueryParameters.SortOrder
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether order returns in ascending or descending order. Default is ascending.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalQueryParameters.StartIndex
member.summary: Gets or sets the index of the first entry in the result set response. The index number is 1-based. The default value of is 1 (e.g.,. the first search result). The start index, along with the parameter can be used to paginate the search results.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalQueryParameters.Limit
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of results to be included in the result set response. The default value is 10 and the maximum allowed value is 100. The start index, along with the limit parameter can be used to paginate the search results. The actual number of returned results may be less than . This happens when the number of results remaining after is less than .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalQueryParameters.CanSearchPublic
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether public items outside the organization may be included in the search results. Default behavior is they are included.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalQueryParameters.BoundingBox
member.summary: Gets or sets the search extent for a spatial search. Search requires a bounding box, a , or both. Spatial search is an overlaps/intersects function of the search extent and the extent of the document. Sorting in a spatial search is not supported. Documents that have no extent (e.g., mxds, 3dds, lyr) will not be found when doing a spatial search. Document extent is assumed to be in geographic coordinates.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalQueryParameters.Categories
member.summary: Gets a list of content category specifications to use when searching for items.
member.remarks: Each entry in the list is a string containing a comma-separated list of up to 8 content categories. The exact full path of each category is required, and an OR relationship is applied between the categories within a particular string. There can be up to 8 strings with an AND relationship being applied between the different strings. For example, to search for items belonging to either the Water or Forest categories, both within the US, specify 2 Strings as follows: "/Categories/Water,/Categories/Forest" and "/Region/US". The categories property is ignored if the PortalQueryParameters is used to search for groups using .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalUser.AddPortalItemAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalItem)
member.summary: Adds an item without content asynchronously. This operation is available only to authenticated users.

remarks.para: The user automatically becomes the owner of the item. The item by default is flagged as private (e.g., not shared with any group). This can be changed with the share item operation.
The url used for an item must be unique within the particular user's folder.

member.param: The portal item to add.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous add item operation. The value of the task result is a object.
member.exception: Operation is available only to authenticated users.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Portal.PortalUser.GetContentAsync
member.summary: Gets the folders and items that are stored at the user's root folder.

remarks.para: The purpose of this Method is to obtain content (items and folders) that belong to a particular authenticated user in a portal. This method performs an asynchronous request to obtain a object for the logged in user. You cannot use anonymous access to obtain a Portal.PortalUser object and use this Method to obtain valid results. As AGOL/ArcGIS Portal require the use of long term tokens to access secured user information.
To retrieve portal items from folders other than the root use .

member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous get items operation. The value of the task result contains a object.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.AddRastersParameters
member.summary: Adds raster datasets to a by setting various parameters.

remarks.para: Mosaic datasets (via the class) are used to manage, display, serve, and share raster data. When you create a new mosaic dataset, it is created as an empty container in a geodatabase to which you can add raster data. The AddRastersParameters object provides the mechanism to set various properties, so that a raster can be added to a mosaic dataset via the method.

Some of the properties on AddRastersParameters class that enable adding a raster into a mosaic dataset include specifying the file filter type (like .tif or .jpg) of the rasters that are to be included ( property), the directory location ( property) of the raster files, the minimum pixel cell size of the raster to be included ( and more.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.AddRastersParameters.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.AddRastersParameters.Filter
member.summary: Gets or sets the raster filename filter.

remarks.para: This function sets the optional raster filename filter, which is a regular expression with ECMA-262 grammar (http://ecma-international.org/ecma-262/5.1/#sec-15.10)

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.BlendRenderer
member.summary: A blend renderer can apply a color ramp to a hillshade to emphasize areas of high or low elevation of raster data in a .
member.remarks: The blend renderer extends with options to provide minimum and maximum stretch values, gamma adjustments, elevation raster, and color ramp. It provides a look similar to the original raster, but with some terrain shading, giving it a textured look and making changes in elevation more visible.

member.summary: Inclination of slope is calculated as percentage values. The values range from 0 to essentially infinity. A flat surface is 0 percent and a 45-degree surface is 100 percent, and as the surface becomes more vertical, the percent rise becomes increasingly larger.

member.summary: Same as Degree, but the z-factor is adjusted for scale using Pixel Size Power and Pixel Size Factor.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.ColormapRenderer
member.summary: A colormap renderer provides a discrete mapping of raster cell values to colors to help visualize the raster data in a .
member.remarks: All cells matching the specified value will be rendered using the mapped color. This is useful for thematic mapping tasks such as land classification when you want the values in the raster layer to be represented by a specified color. This concept of this class is similar to a , except that is specifically for rendering raster cell values.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.FrameCameraAddRastersParameters
member.summary: Adds raster datasets to a by setting various parameters including the use of frame and camera text files.

remarks.para: Mosaic datasets (via the class) are used to manage, display, serve, and share raster data. When you create a new mosaic dataset, it is created as an empty container in a geodatabase to which you can add raster data. The object provides the mechanism to set various properties including the use of camera and frame text files so that a raster can be added to a mosaic dataset (via the method).

Some of the properties on class that enable adding a raster into a mosaic dataset include specifying, the file filter type (like .tif or .jpg) of the rasters that are to be included ( property), the directory location ( property) of the raster files, the minimum pixel cell size of the raster to be included ( property) and more.

Unique attributes of using the class rather than the parent class have to do with defining 'frame' and 'camera' text files which specify how a raster image was captured from a satellite or aircraft. The frame and camera table files refers to a single perspective imaging camera with an optical lens that can be modeled as a pinhole. To define the frame table use the property, see the ArcGIS Pro document entitled 'Frames table schema' (https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/help/data/imagery/frames-table-schema.htm) for more information. To define the camera table use the property, see the ArcGIS Pro document entitled 'Cameras table schema' (https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/help/data/imagery/cameras-table-schema.htm) for more information.
