member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.HillshadeRenderer
member.summary: A hillshade renderer uses a grayscale representation of a 3D surface to help visualize the raster data in a .
member.remarks: A hillshade (also known as shaded relief) is a grayscale representation of a 3D surface, with the sun's relative position taken into account for shading the image. The and properties specify the light's position.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.HillshadeRenderer.#ctor(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.SlopeType,System.Double,System.Double,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Altitude angle of the light source.
Azimuth angle of the light source, measured clockwise from north.
Factor to convert z unit to x,y units, default is 1.
The manner used to express slope in an elevation surface.
Pixel size factor, default is 1.
Pixel size power value, default is 1.
Output bit depth, default is 8-bit.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.HillshadeRenderer.Altitude
member.summary: Gets the altitude angle in degrees.
member.remarks: Altitude is the sun's angle of elevation above the horizon and ranges from 0 to 90 degrees. A value of 0 degrees indicates that the sun is on the horizon, that is, on the same horizontal plane as the frame of reference. A value of 90 degrees indicates that the sun is directly overhead.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.HillshadeRenderer.Azimuth
member.summary: Gets the azimuth angle in degrees.
member.remarks: Azimuth is the sun's relative position along the horizon (in degrees). This position is indicated by the angle of the sun measured clockwise from due north. An azimuth of 0 degrees indicates north, east is 90 degrees, south is 180 degrees, and west is 270 degrees.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.HillshadeRenderer.OutputBitDepth
member.summary: Gets the output bit depth (pixel depth) of a raster cell.
member.remarks: The bit depth determines the range of values that a particular raster can store, which is based on the formula 2n (where n is the bit depth). For example, an 8-bit raster can have 256 unique values that range from 0 to 255.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.HillshadeRenderer.PixelSizeFactor
member.summary: Gets the pixel size factor.
member.remarks: PixelSizeFactor and values account for altitude changes (scale) as the viewer zooms in and out. This is recommended when using worldwide datasets. Only applicable if is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.HillshadeRenderer.PixelSizePower
member.summary: Gets the pixel size power.
member.remarks: and PixelSizePower values account for altitude changes (scale) as the viewer zooms in and out. This is recommended when using worldwide datasets. Only applicable if is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.HillshadeRenderer.SlopeType
member.summary: Gets the manner in which slope is calculated.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.HillshadeRenderer.Zfactor
member.summary: Gets the Z factor.
member.remarks:
A scaling factor used to convert the elevation values for two purposes:
To convert the elevation units (such as meters or feet) to the horizontal coordinate units (x, y) of the dataset, which may be feet, meters, or degrees.
To add vertical exaggeration for visual effect. If the x, y units and z units are in the same units of measure the z-factor is 1, unless a vertical exaggeration needs to be applied. If the x, y units and z units are in different units of measure the z-factor must be set to the appropriate factor, or the results will be incorrect. For example, if your z units are feet and your x, y units are meters, you would use a z-factor of 0.3048 to convert your z units from feet to meters (1 foot = 0.3048 meter).

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.ImageServiceRaster
member.summary: Raster data that is retrieved from an ArcGIS Image Service.
member.remarks: An ArcGIS image service provides access to raster data through a web service. A single raster dataset or a mosaic dataset which contains a collection of raster datasets can be served as one image service. The mosaic dataset can dynamically process and mosaic the images on the fly. An image service supports accessing both the mosaicked image and its catalog, as well as individual rasters in the catalog. An image service raster allows you to display raster data from image services using a . It also allows you to apply service-defined or client-defined rendering rules by setting on it. You can pass an image service raster as a raster input to a to work with local raster functions. The image service raster is derived from the raster class.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicMethod
member.summary: Enumerates mosaic method types.

remarks.para: A mosaic method specifies how multiple rasters in a are sorted.

An may not support every mosaic method. Check the service's metadata for 'Allowed Mosaic Methods' to see which methods are supported. Mosaic methods that are 'view-dependent' order rasters based on where the camera is located while 'view-independent' order rasters the same regardless of where the camera is located. See the document: Understanding the mosaicking rules for a mosaic dataset.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicMethod.None
member.summary: Orders rasters based on the order (ObjectID) in the mosaic dataset attribute table. Mosaic results are view-independent.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicMethod.Center
member.summary: Orders rasters based on the distance between each raster's center and the view center. Mosaic results are view-dependent.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicMethod.Northwest
member.summary: Orders rasters based on the distance between each raster's center and the northwest point of the service. Mosaic results are view-independent.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicMethod.Nadir
member.summary: Orders rasters based on the distance between each raster's nadir position and view center. Mosaic results are view-dependent. Nadir is defined as the point on the ground vertically beneath the perspective center of the camera lens that captured the image.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicMethod.Viewpoint
member.summary: Orders rasters based on the distance between each raster's center and a user defined view point. Mosaic results are view-independent.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicMethod.Attribute
member.summary: Orders rasters based on the absolute distance between their values of an attribute and a base value. Only numeric or date fields are applicable. The attribute is specified by and the value is specified by . Mosaic results are view-independent.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicMethod.LockRaster
member.summary: Displays only the selected rasters specified in . Mosaic results are view-independent.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicMethod.Seamline
member.summary: Orders rasters based on the predefined seamline. The property is defined by the seamline so is not applicable anymore. The MosaicOperator should be set to 'Blend' when using the Seamline option. Mosaic results are view-independent. See the document: Mosaic dataset seamlines.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicOperation
member.summary: Enumerates mosaic method types.
member.remarks: The mosaic operator is used to define how overlapping cells from various rasters in a mosaic dataset will be resolved in the mosaicked image. Look at the ArcGIS Pro document: Mosaic dataset properties in the Default Mosaic Operator section for more information.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicOperation.First
member.summary: The overlapping areas will contain the cells from the first raster dataset listed in the mosaic dataset.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicOperation.Last
member.summary: The overlapping areas will contain the cells from the last raster dataset listed in the mosaic dataset.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicOperation.Min
member.summary: The overlapping areas will contain the minimum cell values from all the overlapping cells.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicOperation.Max
member.summary: The overlapping areas will contain the maximum cell values from all the overlapping cells.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicOperation.Mean
member.summary: The overlapping areas will contain the mean cell values from all the overlapping cells.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicOperation.Blend
member.summary: The overlapping areas will be a blend of the cell values that overlap; this blend value relies on an algorithm that is weight based and dependent on the distance from the cells to the edge within the overlapping area. You will want to set Blend when using the .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicOperation.Sum
member.summary: The overlapping areas will contain the total sum of the cell values from all the overlapping cells.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.MosaicRule
member.summary: A rule determining how a requested image should participate in the mosaic.
member.remarks: An image service uses a mosaic rule to mosaic multiple rasters on-the-fly. A mosaic rule parameter is used by many image service operations, such as export image and identify operations. See the document: Understanding the mosaicking rules for a mosaic dataset.

member.summary: The rule that specifies how images are rendered or processed on an ArcGIS image service.

remarks.para: Raster images whose individual pixels represent elevation values can be rendered in a number of different ways, including representation of slope, aspect, hillshade, and shaded relief. A rendering rule defines how the ArcGIS image service renders its images.

You can create a from a or a JSON string that specifies a service-defined or client-defined rendering rule. For a list of functions and the syntax for these rendering rules, see Raster function objects.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Rasters.StretchRenderer
member.summary: A stretch renderer enhances the visual contrast of raster data in a .

remarks.para: A stretch renderer uses continuous raster cell values across a gradual ramp of colors to visualize raster data. It works well when you have a large range of values to display, such as in imagery, aerial photographs, or elevation models.

Stretching improves the appearance of the raster data by spreading its cell values along a histogram whose minimum and maximum values are defined by the raster's bit depth. For example, an 8-bit raster dataset will be stretched along a histogram from 0 to 255.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.ArcGISStreamService
member.summary: A that receives a stream of observations from an ArcGIS stream service.

remarks.para: Currently, this is the only concrete class deriving from .

Members of this class allow an application to:
connect to and receive observations from an ArcGIS stream service
access metadata about the ArcGIS stream service
set a server-side filter on the service to limit the number and type of observations sent
In the current release, only stream services that send point geometries are supported.
To learn about ArcGIS stream services see: ArcGIS Server - Stream Services in the ArcGIS Enterprise documentation.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.ArcGISStreamService.#ctor(System.Uri)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The Uri of the stream service.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class from a portal item.
member.param: An ArcGIS stream service .

member.summary: Gets the Uri of the stream service.
member.value: The Uri of the stream service.
member.remarks: For stream service objects created from a portal item, this property may be null until the service has loaded successfully.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.ArcGISStreamService.PortalItem
member.summary: Gets the the stream service was created from.
member.remarks: The supported item types are and .
member.value: The the stream service was created from.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.ArcGISStreamService.Filter
member.summary: Gets or sets a filter that defines which observations are returned from the service.

remarks.para: Use this property to define a filter that informs the server which observations to send on the stream. The filter may include spatial or attribute criteria (or both). Using this server-side filter is useful for limiting how much data must be handled in the client application, which can help with performance and memory usage in the application.
This property is used when the data source connection is initiated. The filter cannot be changed once the data source is loaded.
The default value is null, meaning no filter is applied.

member.value: Defines which observations are returned from the service.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.ArcGISStreamService.ServiceInfo
member.summary: Gets the metadata that describes the ArcGIS stream service.

remarks.para: Use this property to access metadata that describes the ArcGIS stream service. This property is null by default and is populated when the data source loads.

Once the data source is loaded, you can use this metadata to find things like:
Fields: field definitions for data in the stream
Track ID field: the field that contains a unique ID for each track in a track-aware service
Archive service URL: a service that contains the latest observations for dynamic entities provided by this stream

member.value: Metadata that describes the ArcGIS stream service.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.ArcGISStreamServiceFilter
member.summary: Limits which dynamic entities are returned from an ArcGIS stream service.

remarks.para: Set to an object of this type to apply a server-side filter on observations streamed from an ArcGIS stream service. Spatial or attribute filters (or both) are supported.
Using this server-side filter is useful for limiting how much data is streamed from an and handled in the client application which can help with performance and memory usage in the application.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.ArcGISStreamServiceFilter.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.ArcGISStreamServiceFilter.Geometry
member.summary: Gets or sets the geometry that is used to filter results.

remarks.para: Dynamic entity observations that are contained within this geometry are included in the stream. Those that are outside this geometry are excluded.
The default value is null, meaning no spatial filter will be applied to the stream.
Currently, only geometries are supported.

member.value: The geometry that is used to filter results.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.ArcGISStreamServiceFilter.OutFields
member.summary: Gets a collection of field names that identify the observation values the server should return.

remarks.para: This property instructs the service to return a subset of values when pushing observations to the client. Restricting the values returned will limit the amount of memory required to store individual observations and therefore reduce the memory footprint of the application.
The default is an empty collection, which returns all observation fields from the server.
Note that the geometry, track ID (if available), and timestamp fields are always included with each observation.
remarks.note: The output fields take effect when connecting to an empty . The following three steps are required to modify the output fields for an existing connection: , , and .

member.value: A collection of field names that identify the observation values the server should return.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.ArcGISStreamServiceFilter.OutSpatialReference
member.summary: Gets or sets the spatial reference to use when returning dynamic entities.

remarks.para: Use this property to instruct the service to provide observation geometries in the given spatial reference.

This property is useful if the client application needs dynamic entities in a specific spatial reference. Setting this property forces the server to reproject geometries before adding observations to the stream. This relieves the client application from the performance burden of reprojecting dynamic entities.
The default value is null which indicates that dynamic entities are returned in the spatial reference of the service.

member.value: The spatial reference to use when returning dynamic entities.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.ArcGISStreamServiceFilter.WhereClause
member.summary: Gets or sets a SQL expression used to filter dynamic entities.

remarks.para: Use this property to filter dynamic entities by attribute value. Setting this property causes the server to filter observations using the given expression before adding them to the stream.
The expression used as the should follow standard SQL syntax as discussed in the document SQL reference for query expressions used in ArcGIS.
The default value is an empty string, meaning observation geometries should be returned in the spatial reference of the service.

member.value: A SQL expression used to filter dynamic entities.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntity
member.summary: A that represents a real-world object received from a .

remarks.para: Dynamic entities are updated with data coming from a stream. These updates are referred to as observations and are represented by the class. An observation is a static snapshot of the state of a dynamic entity at a given time. This snapshot includes the associated dynamic entity's location (geometry) as well as all its attributes. For example, an observation for a moving aircraft may include updates to attributes like speed, altitude, and heading in addition to its updated location. While observations remain static, a dynamic entity changes because it's essentially a pointer to the most recent observation.

The properties of a , such as its attributes and geometry, are updated when new observations are received from the server. You should not cache the or collection properties as they are completely replaced when a new observation is received for the associated .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntity.EntityId
member.summary: Gets a unique identifier for the .
member.remarks: The EntityId property uniquely identifies a .
member.value: A unique identifier for the .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntity.Geometry
member.summary: Gets or sets the of the dynamic entity.
member.remarks: Dynamic entity geometry cannot be set. Attempting to set will throw .
member.value: The of the dynamic entity.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntity.GetLatestObservation
member.summary: Gets the latest associated with this dynamic entity.
member.remarks: The observation returned by this method represents a snapshot of the state of this dynamic entity at the time the observation was received. This method may return null if the dynamic entity has been purged from the local data cache.
member.returns: The latest observation associated with this dynamic entity.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntity.GetObservations(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the collection of objects associated with this dynamic entity.

remarks.para: Use this method to get a collection of objects associated with this dynamic entity. Observations are associated with a dynamic entity through a shared track ID. For data sources, the track ID field is defined by the property.
The returned observations are sorted from the most recent to the oldest.
This method may return an empty collection if the dynamic entity has been purged from the local data cache.

member.param: The maximum number of observations to return, including the latest. The default value is -1, which returns all available observations.
member.returns: A read-only list of associated with this .

member.name: E:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntity.DynamicEntityChanged
member.summary: Notification that the has changed.
member.remarks: This event is raised any time an associated observation is received from a or when an associated observation is purged from the local data cache.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityChangedEventArgs
member.summary: Describes changes to a sent by the event.

remarks.para: An object of this type is sent as an argument to the event. This event is raised whenever observations associated with an existing dynamic entity are received from the data source or purged from the local data cache.

If the last observation has been purged from the local data cache, the property is set to true. This indicates that the dynamic entity has been purged and will receive no more associated observations from the data source.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityChangedEventArgs.DynamicEntityPurged
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the itself has been purged.

remarks.para: This flag is set when the last observation associated with this dynamic entity has been purged from the local data cache. In this case, the dynamic entity is also purged and will not be associated with any new observations received from the data source.
Purge of a dynamic entity can be caused by exceeding the limits defined by or by an explicit call to .
The default is false.

member.value: Indicates whether the itself has been purged.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityChangedEventArgs.PurgedObservation
member.summary: Gets the purged .

remarks.para: If an observation associated with this dynamic entity was purged, this property contains the purged .
The default is null, meaning no observation associated with this dynamic entity was purged.
When a call to causes multiple observations for a dynamic entity to be purged, this property is set to the last purged observation of this dynamic entity and is set to true.

member.value: The purged .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityChangedEventArgs.ReceivedObservation
member.summary: Gets the newly received .

remarks.para: When an observation associated with this dynamic entity is received from the data source, this property contains the received observation.
The default is null, meaning no observation associated with this dynamic entity was received.

member.value: The newly received .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource
member.summary: A dynamic entity data source that streams observations to a client.

remarks.para:
The class is the base class for classes that stream dynamic entity data. The class provides methods and properties that allow:
maintenance of a persistent connection for streaming
control over the storage of observations in its local data cache
notification of received or purged observations
This class is used as the data source for a .

Users may extend this class by deriving from it. Derived classes must override the following methods:
: populate and return a object.
: start receiving or processing observation data.
: stop receiving or processing observation data.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.ConnectionError
member.summary: Gets the current network connection exception.

remarks.para: This property is null until an attempt to connect the data source is made. If a connection attempt succeeds, this property is set to null. If a connection fails, this property contains the platform-specific error.
If is greater than 0, this property will contain the exception that caused the data source to lose the connection.

member.value: The current network connection exception.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.ConnectionStatus
member.summary: Gets the current network connection status of the .

remarks.para: This property contains the state of the connection throughout the lifetime of the data source. The initial value of this property is .
Notification of a change to the property is signaled through the event.

member.value: The current network connection status of the .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.LoadError
member.summary: Gets the load error.
member.value: The load error.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.LoadStatus
member.summary: Gets the load status.
member.value: The load status.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.MaximumReconnectionAttempts
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of attempts to reconnect after a connection fails.

remarks.para: This property informs the data source to attempt reconnection if a connection is lost. When a connection fails, the data source attempts to reconnect the specified number of times waiting between attempts.
If reconnection fails on the final attempt, is set to and the is set to the platform specific connection error.
The default value is 12. The first 2 connection attempts are made without waiting the specified between attempts.

member.value: The maximum number of attempts to reconnect after a connection fails.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.ReconnectionInterval
member.summary: Gets or sets the time to wait between attempts to reconnect.
member.remarks: This property sets the amount of time delay between reconnection attempts after a connection fails. The default value is 10 seconds.
member.value: The time to wait between attempts to reconnect.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.PurgeOptions
member.summary: Gets a that defines when observations are purged from the local data cache.

remarks.para: Data received from the is stored in memory on the client and can grow quickly depending on the number of dynamic entities and frequency of updates. If purge options are not set appropriately, a data source may store too many observations in its local data cache and cause performance and memory issues for the application. Use the property to control app data storage for the local cache.
This property must be set before loading the data source and cannot be changed once the data source is loaded.
By default, purge options are set to remove observations from the data cache when a maximum limit of 100,000 observations is reached.

Note that you can clear all observations from the cache using . This clears all data from the local data cache, but does not disconnect from the stream. Dynamic entities and observations will continue to be added to the local data cache as they come from the stream.

member.value: A that defines when observations are purged from the local data cache.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.CancelLoad
member.summary: Cancels loading metadata for the object.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.LoadAsync
member.summary: Loads the metadata for the object asynchronously.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.RetryLoadAsync
member.summary: Loads or retries loading metadata for the object asynchronously.
member.remarks: Will retry loading the metadata if the object is failed to load. Will load the object if it is not loaded. Will not retry to load the object if the object is loaded. Will always call the done loading if this is called.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.ConnectAsync
member.summary: Connects to a service that provides dynamic entity observations.

remarks.para: Data does not start streaming from the dynamic entity data source until it's loaded and connected. Once the connection is successfully established, data begins to flow from the stream.

Use this method to explicitly connect to the data source. This call loads the data source if it is not already loaded and then initiates a persistent connection to the service that provides the observation stream. When this method completes, the is set to or .
Note that a data source connection is initiated implicitly if the data source is contained by a and added to a map or scene.

member.returns: A that represents the asynchronous connect operation.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.DisconnectAsync
member.summary: Closes the connection to a service.

remarks.para: Use this method to explicitly disconnect from a data source. When this method completes, the is set to or .
If this method is called when a data source has a of , the current connection attempt is cancelled.

member.returns: A that represents the asynchronous disconnect operation.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.OnLoadAsync
member.summary: Handles the loading process of a data source and returns .

remarks.para: Override this method to populate and return a object. This object defines the schema and metadata used by the internal data cache of the . If the returned object does not contain all required values, the data source will fail to load and will not be usable.

This method is called by the ArcGIS Maps SDK when loading is required. This can be due to an explicit call to load the data source using or called implicitly when a layer that contains the data source needs to be rendered.
Code in a derived class should throw an exception if the load operation fails. The exception is passed back to the data source and is available in the property.

member.returns: A representing the result of the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.OnConnectAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles the connection process of a data source.

remarks.para: Override this method to perform operations that initiate a connection and start the flow of data from an underlying data source. For instance, operations like connecting a websocket, opening a source file, or starting a polling timer are all operations that may be performed in this method.

This method is called by the ArcGIS Maps SDK when the connection is required. This can be due to an explicit call to or called implicitly when a layer that contains the data source needs to be rendered.
Code in a derived class should throw an exception if the connect operation fails. The exception is passed back to the data source and is available in the property.

member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous connect operation.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.OnDisconnectAsync
member.summary: Handles the disconnection process of a data source.
member.remarks: Override this method to perform operations that stop the flow of data from an underlying data source.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous disconnect operation.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.PurgeAllAsync
member.summary: Removes all dynamic entity observations from the in-memory data cache as well as from the map or scene.

remarks.para: Sometimes, a large number of dynamic entity observations can crowd the display and make information hard to interpret. Use this method to clear (purge) all dynamic entity observations to remove stale information and limit the amount of memory consumed. This does not disconnect you from the service, so new dynamic entity observations continue to display as they become available.
The following events will not be raised due to a call to this method:

member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous purge operation.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSource.QueryDynamicEntitiesAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityQueryParameters,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all dynamic entities currently matching the specified query parameters.

remarks.para: Use this method to query the dynamic entities currently stored in the data source. The query parameters can be used to filter based on track ID, geometry, spatial relationship, and attribute query string.
The query is performed on the most recent observation of each dynamic entity in the data source at the time the query is executed.
The returned object can be used to iterate over the query results.

member.param: Parameters for performing the dynamic entity query.
An optional cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.
member.returns: A that returns a type.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSourcePurgeOptions
member.summary: Purge options for a dynamic entity data source.

remarks.para: Data received from a is cached in memory on the client and can grow quickly depending on the number of dynamic entities and frequency of updates. Use this class and the property to control app storage in the local data cache.
Properties on this object affect cached observations and should not be confused with similar options in which are applied on the and affect visibility of observations on a map or scene.
By default, the maximum total number of observations stored is 100,000 with no limit to the number of observations per track and no observation age limit.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSourcePurgeOptions.MaximumDuration
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum time an observation remains in the application.

remarks.para: Observations older than the specified maximum duration are removed from the local data cache.

The default value is , which means the maximum duration is disabled and duration is not used to purge observations from the local data cache. Setting maximum duration to anything below 1 second defaults to 1 second.

member.value: The maximum time an observation remains in the application.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSourcePurgeOptions.MaximumObservations
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of observations cached by the application.

remarks.para: Use this property to limit the total number of observations cached in the local data cache. Use to limit the number of observations per unique track.
The default value is 100,000. A value of 0 disables the maximum observations purge option.

member.value: The maximum number of observations cached by the application.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityDataSourcePurgeOptions.MaximumObservationsPerTrack
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of observations per track.

remarks.para: Use this property to limit the number of observations cached in the local data cache per unique track ID.
The default value is 0 which means the maximum observations per track purge option is disabled.

member.value: The maximum number of observations per track.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityObservation
member.summary: A that represents a static observation of a received from a .

remarks.para: Dynamic entity observations are objects streamed by a . Each observation is a snapshot of the state of a at a given point in time.
Observations are stored in a local data cache as they are received from the data source. Observations may be purged from the local data cache according to the.
Multiple observations may be associated with a single (via a shared track ID). These observations may be retrieved using . The most current observation of a may be retrieved using .
Observations may also be retrieved by identifying observations on a map or scene, or by handling events directly from the .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.RealTime.DynamicEntityQueryResult
member.summary: The result of a query performed on a .

remarks.para: A query performed on a will return an object of this type. The query result allows you to iterate over the dynamic entities that satisfy the query.

Due to the real-time nature of dynamic entities in the result, individual entities may receive new observations, have existing observations purged, or be entirely purged before the application can use them. Use to verify the current state of an entity before using it.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregateField
member.summary: Defines a field or statistical for summarizing aggregated data from an .
member.remarks: Feature reduction uses statistical methods to aggregate geoelements into logical groups based on their location. Attribute values for each group () can be summarized for any numeric field using a specified , such as the average, maximum, or sum. A common workflow is to summarize aggregated groups with the number of geoelements they represent.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregateGeoElement
member.summary: A that represents aggregated geoelement on the map, for example a cluster of features or graphics.

remarks.para: Based on aggregation properties defined in , geoelements are aggregated dynamically as the scale of the map changes. Attributes of an are the summary statistics defined at the time of configuring aggregation on a . is a representing the centroid of the aggregated geoelement. By default, has a cluster_count attribute. Aggregated geoelements only have access to the defined. The fields from the feature layer are not accessible for use in popups, labels, or renderers for aggregated geoelements.
You can select or unselect aggregate geoelements using the property. Call to get the collection of geoelements represented by the aggregate geoelement.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregateGeoElement.Geometry
member.summary: Gets or sets the of the aggregate geoelement.
member.value: Geometry is a representing the centroid of the aggregate geoelement.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregateGeoElement.IsSelected
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the aggregate geoelement is selected, false otherwise.

remarks.para: Selecting an adds a temporary visual selection halo around the aggregate geoelement. Selected aggregate geoelements are rendered according to the .

As a user navigates the map, aggregate geoelements update dynamically depending on the map's scale and extent. When aggregate geoelements are re-created, new aggregate geoelements are drawn. This also clears selection halos of any previously referenced . However, properties of the previous referenced aggregate geoelements, such as their geometry, attributes, and associated underlying geoelements can still be accessed.

Note that the selection halo that highlights an aggregate geoelement is transient. In addition to the map's scale and extent changes, changes to underlying properties of also result in aggregate geoelements being re-created and re-drawn. When this happens, the existing selection halos on aggregate geoelements are cleared.

member.value: true if the aggregate geoelement is selected, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregateGeoElement.GetGeoElementsAsync
member.summary: Gets the collection of geoelements represented by this aggregate geoelement.

remarks.para: For an aggregate geoelement on a feature layer, this method returns a collection of features. For a graphics overlay, it returns a collection of graphics.

As a user navigates the map, aggregate geoelements update dynamically depending on the map's scale and extent. When aggregate geoelements are re-created, new aggregate geoelements are drawn. References to instances that are no longer drawn on the map retain their association to the geoelements they aggregated.

member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous get geoelements operation. The value of the task result contains a read-only collection of objects.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregationFeatureReduction
member.summary: A base class to define visual aggregation of geoelements.
member.remarks: Feature reduction dynamically aggregates groups of points that are within proximity of each other in order to represent each group with a single symbol. Such grouping allows you to see patterns in the data that are difficult to visualize when a layer contains hundreds or thousands of points that overlap and cover each other. The count of geoelements in each group can be displayed, and numeric attribute values can also be summarized within each group. Note, properties defined for are independent of properties of and . is not supported in 3D.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregationFeatureReduction.IsPopupEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the is enabled.
member.value: true to show the , or false to hide. The default is true.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregationFeatureReduction.PopupDefinition
member.summary: Gets or sets the pop-up definition on the aggregate geoelement.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregationFeatureReduction.AggregateFields
member.summary: Gets a list of that summarizes one or more attributes for a group of geoelements visualized with .
member.remarks: define the attributes of an . Each stores a value that is generated by aggregating values from a field referenced in the feature layer. These aggregate fields may be used in popups, labels, and renderers. Note that aggregated geoelements only have access to the defined. The fields from the feature layer are not accessible for use in popups, labels, or renderers for aggregated geoelements. For example, to display the sum of the population values in a cluster of features, you can create an aggregate field called 'sum_population' using the 'population' attribute of the . You can then use the 'sum_population' attribute as a label for the , but not the original 'population' attribute from the feature layer. See for more info. Note: Updates to this collection cause data to be re-aggregated.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregationFeatureReduction.LabelDefinitions
member.summary: Gets the collection of objects that define label display for the summarized attribute values of aggregated geoelements.
member.remarks: Label expressions can only refer to the fields defined in . Feature layer fields are not accessible to aggregated geoelements. AggregationFeatureReduction.LabelDefinitions is independent of FeatureLayer.LabelDefinitions and GraphicsOverlay.LabelDefinitions. Labels on aggregated geoelements will only appear if there is at least one defined.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregationFeatureReduction.MaxScale
member.summary: Gets or sets the map scale at which aggregation is enabled. Geoelements are not aggregated when the map scale goes beyond the maximum scale.
member.remarks: The default value is 0, which means aggregation is applied at all map scales. Note: changing this property causes data to be re-aggregated.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregationFeatureReduction.Renderer
member.summary: Gets or sets the symbology for displaying aggregated geoelements.

remarks.para: Only , , and are currently supported for . with multiple fields is not supported. If the renderer passed is not one of the supported renderer types listed above then original features from the or original graphics from the are drawn using or respectively. In the case of , a will contain an error indicating the use of an unsupported renderer. Also note, AggregationFeatureReduction.Renderer is independent of FeatureLayer.Renderer and GraphicsOverlay.Renderer.

If you want to symbolize aggregate geoelements using the same symbology used by the or , you may clone FeatureLayer.Renderer or GraphicsOverlay.Renderer and set it here. You can also create a new renderer of a supported type. If you create a new , aggregate geoelements will display with the symbol defined for the . If the new renderer is of type or , the field name on the renderer must match one of the in . If this field is not found in , aggregate geoelements will draw using or . If there is no default symbol defined, aggregate geoelements will not draw.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.AggregationFeatureReduction.ShowLabels
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to display labels for aggregated geoelements, false to turn them off.
member.remarks: The default value is true.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.ClusteringFeatureReduction
member.summary: Method for aggregating and summarizing features in a point or point graphics in a in dynamic rendering mode (FeatureRenderingMode.Dynamic, or GraphicsRenderingMode.Dynamic).

remarks.para: Clustering is configured via the property of the or the of the .

Feature clustering allows you to visually represent large numbers of point geoelements in relatively small areas by aggregating them into clusters. Each cluster of two or more points is represented with an that has its own symbol. If a point feature is not clustered, it continues to draw as an individual feature using the layer's renderer. As a user navigates the map, clusters update dynamically depending on the map's scale and extent. Feature clustering only applies to layers with point geometries in a 2D map. It does not apply to layers with polyline and polygon geometries and is not supported in 3D. In such unsupported scenarios, the original features or graphics are drawn and in the case of , a is bubbled up indicating the appropriate error. The symbol for the cluster is determined by the renderer passed to and cluster symbol size is always determined by the and properties. At present, this API does not support configuring cluster symbol size from another .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.ClusteringFeatureReduction.#ctor(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Renderer)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class using the given renderer and default values for min size, max size and radius of the cluster.

remarks.para: Only , , and are currently supported for AggregationFeatureReduction.Renderer. with multiple fields is not supported. If the renderer passed is not one of the supported renderer types listed above then original features from the or original graphics from the are drawn using or respectively. In the case of , a will contain an error indicating the use of an unsupported renderer. Also note, AggregationFeatureReduction.Renderer is independent of FeatureLayer.Renderer and GraphicsOverlay.Renderer.

If you want to symbolize clusters using the same symbology used by the or , you may clone or and set it here. You can also create a new renderer of a supported type. If you create a new , clusters will draw using symbol defined for the . If the new renderer is of type or , the field name on the renderer must match one of the in . If this field is not found in , clusters will draw using or . If there is no default symbol defined, clusters will not draw.

member.param: The renderer for drawing clustered geoelements.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.ClusteringFeatureReduction.MaxSymbolSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the symbol size of the largest cluster in device-independent pixels. The default value is 70.
member.remarks: The and complement each other. If you adjust one, you should adjust the other (a larger should have a larger ). Note, if a custom renderer is defined for clusters and that renderer contains size visual variables, this property is ignored and cluster sizes are determined using the cluster renderer's size variable. Note: Changing this property causes data to be re-clustered.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.ClusteringFeatureReduction.MinSymbolSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the symbol size of the smallest cluster in device-independent pixels. The default value is 12.
member.remarks: If a custom renderer is defined for the clusters, and that renderer contains size visual variables, this property is ignored and cluster sizes are determined using the cluster renderer's size variable. Note: Changing this property causes data to be re-clustered.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.ClusteringFeatureReduction.Radius
member.summary: Gets or sets the radius in device-independent pixels of the area in which multiple points will be grouped and visualized as a single cluster. The default value is 60.
member.remarks: If you don't want cluster graphics to overlap, the should be larger than the . Note: Changing this property causes data to be re-clustered.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.FeatureReduction
member.summary: An abstract class for reducing and summarizing point geoelements such as graphics or features.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Reduction.FeatureReduction.IsEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to enable feature reduction, false to show individual geoelements.
member.remarks: The default value is true. When persisting a where is false, is persisted to the layer in the map but without any configuration or properties currently defined on the object. When loading a where is false, properties have default values and it uses the layer's renderer.
member.value: true to enable feature reduction, false to show individual geoelements.

member.summary: Gets or sets the base authentication interface for accessing an ArcGIS resource with an HTTP authentication.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.ArcGISNetworkCredential.PreAuthenticateBehavior
member.summary: Gets or sets the behavior for setting the Authorization header.
member.remarks: By default, .NET will not send credentials via the Authorization header until it receives a 401 WWW-Authenticate Basic response from the server, even if credentials have already been specified. This results in at least two requests being sent for each endpoint. This property allows you to change this behavior for this credential.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.ArcGISNetworkCredential.RealmRoot
member.summary: Gets the calculated "root" (longest common URL prefix) used for unnamed realms.
member.remarks: Although HTTP Basic and Digest authentication protocols do not inherently tie realms to URL patterns, tracking URL prefixes helps avoid unnecessary authentication challenges. Path-based matching is only used when the server does not specify a realm in the WWW-Authenticate header. This property is initialized to and may be updated when the server challenges us for additional URLs. When new URLs in the same unnamed realm are encountered, the RealmRoot is automatically shortened to maintain the longest common prefix.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.PreAuthenticateBehavior
member.summary: Determines how an should behave.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.PreAuthenticateBehavior.AfterFirstChallenge
member.summary: The credential will be added to all future HTTP requests to a server after it has been challenged for and successfully authenticates.
member.remarks: This is the standard .NET behavior when is set to true.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.PreAuthenticateBehavior.Disabled
member.summary: The credential is not added to an HTTP request until it has first been challenged for by the server. Because of this, the server will likely require re-authentication with this credential for every request.
member.remarks: This is the standard .NET behavior when is set to false.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.PreAuthenticateBehavior.Always
member.summary: The credential is added to all HTTP requests, without any prompting from the server.

remarks.para: This is useful for servers that do not challenge for authentication and instead, for example, return a different view to unauthorized users.
Credentials are sent in the Authorization header using the "Basic" scheme (RFC 7617). Other HTTP authentication schemes are not supported in this mode.

Additionally if portal uses the OAuth authentication, it needs to be registered by this method.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.AuthenticationManager
member.summary: The Authentication Manager is a singleton class that, when enabled, will manage the user credentials.

remarks.para: This is for the following resources:

list.item: ArcGIS Server resources secured using token-based authentication or using HTTP authentication. Note that only ArcGIS Server versions 10 SP 1 and greater are supported.
Secured ArcGIS.com resources (e.g. web maps).
Secured ArcGIS Portal resources.

remarks.para: A credential for accessing to an ArcGIS resource can be preset by using . In this case the credential is used for any requests to the specified resources.
If a request to an ArcGIS resource returns an authorization error, the , if not null, is called. This challenge handler can return the credential for accessing to the resource.
If the ChallengeHandler is null or if it does not return any credential, the Authorization error is returned to the caller as if the request was executed out of the AuthenticationManager.

Most generally the ChallengeHandler displays the UI allowing the user to enter the username/password for accessing the resource. Unlike for WinStore application, the API does not provide any default UI for Desktop. However, a default challenge handler is set for managing the access though OAuth when the is set.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.AuthenticationManager.Current
member.summary: Gets the single instance. This is the only way to get an instance of AuthenticationManager.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.AuthenticationManager.ChallengeHandler
member.summary: Gets or sets the component that handles the authorization errors in order to get a valid credential object.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.AuthenticationManager.Persistence
member.summary: Gets or sets the credential persistence.

remarks.para: When set, any existing credentials will be cleared. If the new persistence is not null, all persisted credentials will be copied to the via .
If the new credential persistence implements , it must be loaded before it can be assigned to the manager.

member.param: The object that describes the secure service and token endpoint.
member.remarks: Typically, the AuthenticationManager can infer the token endpoint if the URL contains either '/sharing' (portal URL) or '/rest/services' (ArcGIS service). However if the location of your server or token endpoint is non-standard use this method to register the location. If your portal uses OAuth user authentication, create an and add it to .

member.summary: Gets or sets the default suggested expiration interval (lifetime) of all tokens.

remarks.para: This value can be overridden for a specific token by setting .

Set to to request the maximum allowed expiration time. This is typically 90 days for OAuth credentials, and 14 days for ArcGIS token credentials. See Portal Security documentation to learn more about token expiration.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.AuthenticationManager.GetCredentialAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.CredentialRequestInfo,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Obtains a of the specified that can be used to access the secured resource identified by the . If required, the will be called to prompt for credentials. In case of , the user is typically challenged for a username and password which are used to generate a token. This method is called internally when a network request fails with an authentication error.
member.param: The information about the credential to get.

Indicates whether this method is being called after the first attempt to authenticate failed. This parameter affects behavior when a credential of matching already exists for the given URL. When false, existing credential is assumed to be valid and may be returned unless it expired. When true, existing credential is assumed to be invalid and will be removed from ; the will then be called to obtain a replacement credential.
member.returns: A task object representing the asynchronous get credential operation. The value of the task result is a object. The result will be null if credential request was canceled or if did not produce a credential.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.AuthenticationManager.RemoveAndRevokeAllCredentialsAsync
member.summary: Removes all credentials from the cache and revokes tokens if appropriate.
member.remarks: If one or more token credentials could not be revoked, the task will complete with an . Credentials that could not be revoked will remain in . You can examine the errors and attempt to revoke again, or use to "forget" them without revoking. Note that removing a token credential without revoking may be a security risk, because the token will remain valid and could potentially be misused before it expires.
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous removal and revoking of credential.

member.summary: Removes the given credential from the cache and revokes its token if appropriate.
member.param: The credential to remove from the cache and revoke its token if appropriate.
member.returns: A that represents the asynchronous removal and revoking of the credential.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.AuthenticationManager.RemoteCertificateValidationCallback
member.summary: Gets or sets the delegate that validates the server certificate during SSL/TLS handshake.

remarks.para: This callback can examine all SSL connections made by the Maps SDK and by to service HTTPS requests. When is null, server certificates are validated by the operating system using default policies and known certificate authorities. A custom validator can be used to override the default behavior and accept or reject server certificates based on custom criteria, such as:

list.item: Accept server credentials that would otherwise be rejected by the system. For example, your app needs to make an HTTPS connection to a development server or through a proxy with a self-signed certificate. Refer to the Ignore specific validation errors section in the .NET Security Best Practices guide for a sample implementation.

Reject credentials that would otherwise be accepted by the system. For example, your app needs to "pin" a set of specific keys or certificates under your control, rather than accept any valid credential. Refer to the Certificate pinning section in the .NET Security Best Practices guide for a sample implementation.

remarks.para: You can obtain the remote hostname from the "sender" parameter. Depending on context, the "sender" may be either System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage or System.Net.Security.SslStream.
When the sslPolicyErrors parameter is , the certificate is considered valid by system's default validation. Otherwise, details about SSL errors can be obtained from of the certificateChain parameter.

Return true from the callback to accept the server certificate, or false to reject it. The returned value is not cached and the callback is invoked each time a new connection is made. The callback should be able to handle repeated calls for the same server, including parallel calls from multiple threads.

When targeting .NET 8+, secure WebSocket connections from will use this callback. When targeting .NET Framework, WebSocket connections go through System.Net.ServicePointManager.ServerCertificateValidationCallback instead. When targeting UWP, there is no way to customize certificate validation for WebSockets.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.GenerateTokenOptions.TokenExpirationInterval
member.summary: Gets or sets the requested expiration interval for the token.

remarks.para: If set to a non-zero interval, this value overrides the default .

When working with the OAuth two-step authorization code workflow, you can use this property to specify the lifetime of the refresh token. Otherwise, this property is used for the lifetime of the access token.

Set to to request the maximum allowed expiration time. This is typically 90 days for OAuth credentials, and 14 days for ArcGIS token credentials. See Portal Security documentation to learn more about token expiration.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.OAuthApplicationCredential
member.summary: A credential to access OAuth token-secured ArcGIS resources using the application's credentials.

remarks.para: The OAuth application credential generates a short-lived access token that gives your application permission to access token-secured ArcGIS content and services, such as the ArcGIS location services.
The requires a private 'client secret'. It is important to treat this as you would your password. You must keep it confidential and we recommended not storing in your application.
If you use this as part of the secure resource challenge handling, it will be stored with the . It can then be used by all subsequent requests that have a matching URL context.

member.summary: The OAuth user configuration information used by an .

remarks.para: The portal URL, client ID, and redirect URL are required to create an OAuth user credential. See register your application and add a redirect URI for getting the client ID and redirect URL.
Use refresh token expiration and exchange intervals to configure the lifetime of an OAuth user credential.
Use show cancel button, user interface style, etc. to configure the look and feel of the OAuth login page displayed by an OAuth user credential.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.OAuthUserConfiguration.#ctor(System.Uri,System.String,System.Uri,System.Globalization.CultureInfo,System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan,System.Boolean,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.UserInterfaceStyle,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified parameters.
member.param: The URL of the portal to authenticate with.
A unique identifier associated with an application registered with the portal that assists with client/server OAuth authentication.
The URL that the OAuth login page redirects to when authentication completes.
The OAuth login page is displayed in the language specified by the given culture code. If not specified, will be used.

The requested expiration interval for the refreshToken generated by the OAuth request. Set this to for getting the maximum refresh token supported by the portal (ninety days). The max interval can be overridden by the portal administrator.

The requested exchange interval for the OAuth refresh token. Use this to exchange a refresh token before it expires. This will limit the number of times a user will have to login because of expiring tokens. If unspecified or set to 0, default exchange interval of 24 hours is used. If the exchange interval is less than , then the refresh token will never be exchanged and will eventually expire, causing the user to have to log in again. To have any effect, this should be set to a value less than the . Setting it to a value greater than the means token will never be exchanged. It is recommended to keep this interval low (a day or less) because long lived refresh tokens may increase the security risk.
The requested expiration interval for federated tokens generated using the OAuth credential.
A value indicating whether to show "Cancel" button on the OAuth login page.
Constants indicating the interface style for the OAuth login page.
A value indicating whether the OAuth login session should ask the browser for a private authentication session.

member.summary: Gets the requested exchange interval for the OAuth refresh token. Use this to exchange a refresh token before it expires. This will limit the number of times a user will have to login because of expiring tokens.

remarks.para: If the exchange interval is set to or less, then the refresh token will never be exchanged and will eventually expire, causing the user to have to log in again.
To have any affect, this should be set to a value less than the . Setting it to a value greater than the will have the same effect as setting this to .
It is recommended to keep this interval as low as possible because long lived refresh tokens may increase the security risk.
The expiration resolution is whole minutes and seconds will be ignored.

member.value: The requested exchange interval for the OAuth refresh token.

member.remarks: If this is set to , then the refresh token is valid for two weeks. The expiration resolution is whole minutes and seconds will be ignored.
member.value: The requested expiration interval for the refresh token generated by the OAuth request.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Security.OAuthTokenCredential.RefreshTokenExchangeInterval
member.summary: Gets or sets the exchange interval for the OAuth refresh token. The default is 24 hours.

remarks.para: Set to to disable automatic exchange of refresh tokens. This will cause the to be called after this credential's refresh tokens expires.

You may need to use a shorter exchange interval when refresh tokens are short-lived. Refresh tokens may expire quickly due to organization's security policy or when requesting a short . Check the property of newly-generated OAuth credentials to see if exchange interval needs to be adjusted.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.ClassBreak
member.summary: A class break object used to categorize a group of values that fall within a range of values. It represents a unique value or combination of values and a matching symbol in a .

remarks.para: The is used to categorize a group of values that fall within a range defined by and properties. A value is determined to be within this range if it is greater than the but less than or equal to the . When written as an algebraic equation, it would look like ('MinValue' < value <= 'MaxValue').
For example, assume you wanted to create a set of categories based on the following integers (0, 0, 1, 1, 2, 4, 6, 6, 7, 10).

The following table would be helpful in defining the range settings
Categorized group of valuesCountLabelMinValueMaxValue
02zero-10
1 - 23one to two02
3 - 51three to five25
6 - 104six to ten510

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.ClassBreak.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Double,System.Double,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Symbol)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with initial values.
member.param: A description of the class break. "Cities with a population under 100,000", for example.
A label for the class break. "0 - 100000", for example.
The minimum value of the range that defines the break.
The maximum value of the range that defines the break.
A symbol used to represent elements in the class break.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.ClassBreak.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Double,System.Double,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Symbol,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Symbol})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with alternate symbols.
member.param: A description of the class break. "Cities with a population under 100,000", for example.
A label for the class break. "0 - 100000", for example.
The minimum value of the range that defines the break.
The maximum value of the range that defines the break.
A symbol used to represent elements in the class break.
A collection of alternate symbols to the primary symbol. Symbols in this list should be of type .
member.remarks: A class break can have alternate symbols to the primary symbol. Alternate symbols allow you to tailor the visualization of a class break at different scales by selecting different symbols for different scales. Alternate symbols are supported only when class break's primary symbol and other symbols in alternate symbol list are of type and have defining valid min/max scales at which the symbol becomes visible. Renderer will pick only one symbol at a given map scale. If primary symbol's scale range falls within the map's scale, primary symbol is used. If not, then symbols in alternate symbols list are iterated through and first symbol matching the current map scale is picked for rendering. A symbol becomes visible if the map scale is less than or equal to symbol's minimum scale and greater than symbol's maximum scale. For more information about scale-based symbol classes and alternate symbols, see Use scale-based symbol classes.

member.remarks: Symbols in this list should be of type and must have set with valid minimum and maximum scale at which symbol becomes visible. Renderer will pick only one symbol at a given map scale. If primary symbol's scale range falls within the map's scale, primary symbol is used. If not, then symbols in alternate symbols list are iterated through and first symbol matching the current map scale is picked for rendering. A symbol becomes visible if the map scale is less than or equal to symbol's minimum scale and greater than symbol's maximum scale.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.RendererNormalizationType
member.summary: Defines the statistical normalization technique used to adjust values measured on different scales to a notionally common scale, often prior to averaging. This enumeration is used by the ClassBreaksRenderer.NormalizationType Property.

remarks.para: Normalizing is the process of dividing one numeric attribute value by another to minimize differences in values based on the size of areas or the number of features in each area. For example, normalizing (dividing) total population by total area yields population per unit area, or density.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.ClassBreaksRenderer
member.summary: A renderer that classifies numeric data into two or more ranges of values to create a visualization.

remarks.para: A class breaks renderer contains a collection of ranges (or class breaks). Each has a , , and a unique . The ranges reflect a specific , such as equal interval, natural breaks, quantile, standard deviation, and manual classification.

contains properties for setting the field that the class breaks refer to and also for controlling normalization. It also defines a default label and symbol to display for values that don't fall within any of the class breaks. You can add or remove a from this renderer via the collection.

Class breaks can be defined as continuous, where the minimum value of a break is defined by the maximum value of the previous class break. For continuous class breaks only the max value needs to be set on each .
For discontinuous class breaks, set both a min and a max value in each .

member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum value of the first class break if the renderer uses continuous class breaks.
member.remarks: This will set the minimum value of the first class break in a renderer containing continuous class breaks where each does not define a minimum value. In this case the minimum value of each break will be the maximum value of the preceding one. As the first class break has no class break before it, this value will define its minimum.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.ClassBreaksRenderer.NormalizationField
member.summary: Gets or sets the renderer's normalization field.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.ClassBreaksRenderer.NormalizationTotal
member.summary: Gets or sets the renderer's normalization total.
member.remarks: This field is the normalization total when is set to . This means that the minimum and maximum values in each class break are defined as percentages of this total.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.ClassBreaksRenderer.NormalizationType
member.summary: Gets or sets the renderer's normalization type.

remarks.para: If this property is not , then the class breaks contain a normalized min/max value instead of the actual value. In this case, the type will indicate the way in which the value in the field should be normalized before comparing with the class breaks.
The default value is .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.DashGeometricEffect
member.summary: A GeometricEffect represents a pattern of dashes and gaps for a of a line symbol .
member.remarks: Transforms the representation of a line using a pattern of dashes and gaps. Dashes are the symbolized portion of the stroke and gaps are the unsymbolized portion. A list of dash and gap distances (DIP values greater than 0) provides a template to be repeated throughout the line. A basic template consists of two distance values: the first defines the length of each dash and the second defines the length of each gap. More complex effects can be created by defining a template of multiple dashes and gaps. Template values are read as dash distance followed by gap distance throughout the pattern. Templates that contain an odd number of values will maintain the dash-gap pattern by restarting at the beginning of the template. A template of '3 2 1', for example, will be rendered as '3(dash) 2(gap) 1(dash) 3(gap) 2(dash) 1(gap) ...'. The default template is an empty list, which renders a solid line. Note that small gaps may be lost if combined with certain cap styles. A cap style like "round", for example, is applied to both ends of the dash line and may overlap in the area designated as the gap.

member.remarks: Transforms the representation of a line using a pattern of dashes and gaps. Dashes are the symbolized portion of the stroke and gaps are the unsymbolized portion. The default template is an empty list, which renders a solid line

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.DictionaryRenderer
member.summary: Renders geoelements using symbols generated from a .

remarks.para: The dictionary renderer applies symbols to features or graphics according to a set of input attribute values.The symbol primitives and logic(rule engine) for applying symbols is provided by an associated The depends on attribute names to define the symbology of each feature. The attributes reference different symbol components in the style, which are assembled to create a complex symbol. In order to display geoelements using the dictionary renderer, attributes in your data must either use the expected attribute names or be mapped to the proper style attributes.

member.param: The symbol dictionary to be used with the renderer.

A dictionary of text field overrides.

member.summary: Gets or sets the dictionary symbol style to be applied by the renderer.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.DictionaryRenderer.ScaleExpression
member.summary: Gets or sets an Arcade expression object with an expression for scaling symbols in a dictionary renderer.
member.remarks: The is evaluated at rendering time. If the expression is not valid or results in an invalid value, symbols will not be scaled and will display at their original size. For example, to scale symbols based on an attribute value, you can set with an expression like if($feature.Symbol_Set == 40, 2.5, 1), which scales the symbol size 2.5 times when Symbol_Set is 40.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.DictionaryRenderer.SymbologyFieldOverrides
member.summary: Gets or sets the dictionary of a symbology attribute and its override used to fetch a symbol from the dictionary symbol style.
member.remarks: A uses a collection of expected attribute names to build a symbol based on input values. When initialized, fields in the input dataset are mapped to the expected attribute names using case-insensitive matching. If your data uses a different name for an expected symbol attribute, use the to map an expected field to the correct field in your data. You can also exclude an expected field by providing an empty string for it in the overrides.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.DictionaryRenderer.TextFieldOverrides
member.summary: Gets or sets the map of a text attribute and its override used to fetch symbol text and placement from the dictionary symbol style.
member.remarks: A uses a collection of expected attribute names to build a symbol based on input values. When initialized, fields in the input dataset are mapped to the expected attribute names using case-insensitive matching. If your data uses a different name for an expected text attribute, use the to map an expected field to the correct field in your data. You can also exclude an expected field by providing an empty string for it in the overrides.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.DictionarySymbolStyle
member.summary: A dictionary symbol style object containing symbol primitives and rules for generating symbols from attribute values.

remarks.para: A DictionarySymbolStyle is created either from a style file on disk (an SQLite database with a .stylx extension, created with ArcGIS Pro) or from a dictionary style file hosted on ArcGIS Online or an ArcGIS Enterprise portal (also referred to as a dictionary web style).

A DictionarySymbolStyle must contain a set of symbol primitives and a rule engine that parses input fields. The DictionarySymbolStyle assembles new symbols based on the input attribute values. DictionarySymbolStyle is often used to render symbols from a military specification (such as MIL-STD 2525D or APP-6B) but can also be used with a custom style. When working with dictionary symbol styles, it's important to avoid using certain reserved strings as field names. Note that dictionary symbol styles reserve certain field names for their internal implementation. Specifically, the field name gt is reserved and geoelements rendered with a dictionary symbol style should not contain an attribute with name gt. Additionally, do not use countrylabel as a primitive name as it is reserved to manage a unique symbol/text scenario in military dictionaries.
A DictionarySymbolStyle is used in conjunction with a to symbolize geoelements in a or .

Esri builds and maintains some dictionary web styles according to the US and NATO military symbol specifications. These desktop and web styles are hosted online and are publicly available. Refer to the ArcGIS Online Dictionary Symbology Styles group for the latest versions of the styles available.

member.summary: Creates a new a from the style file at the provided location.
member.param: A path to style file
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous create operation. The value of the task result is a object.
member.remarks: This constructor is only valid for use with the newer format (Arcade-based) style files. An exception is thrown if a older format style is encountered.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.DictionarySymbolStyle.GetSymbolAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Asynchronously returns a symbol based on the provided attributes.
member.remarks: This function is useful for obtaining a single, multi-layer symbol from supplied attributes.This is commonly used to create symbols and graphics on-the-fly. For example, with mil2525d, you may want to obtain a symbol where "symbolset" is 40, "modifier1" is "Incident Qualifier : Accident", "echelon" is "Army", and so on. All of these key/value pairs can be used to obtain the specific symbol that you need. Once you have that symbol, you can apply it to a Graphic or Renderer, obtain its swatch image, or serialize to JSON. If the DictionarySymbolStyle is not loaded then initiating this task will start the load cycle.
member.param: A key-value pair for the attributes required to obtain symbol from the rule engine associated to symbol dictionary.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous save as operation. The value of the task result is a object.
