member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.DistanceCompositeSceneSymbol
member.summary: Defines a set of symbols and their visible distance range.
member.remarks: Distance composite scene symbol contain symbols with distance ranges, based on distance from the camera the symbols are rendered or not. Note that the ranges for each symbol are independent and may overlap.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.DistanceCompositeSceneSymbol.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.remarks: Distance composite scene symbol is a container of symbols with distance display ranges. Symbols within the collection can specify a minimum and a maximum display distance. When a is rendered, distance from the camera will be checked against the specified minimums and maximums to determine each symbol's visibility. Note that ranges are allowed to overlap, so it is possible for multiple symbols to be visible at the same time.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.DistanceCompositeSceneSymbol.Ranges
member.summary: Gets a mutable list of .
member.remarks: Each symbol added to this collection can specify a minimum and maximum display distance. When a feature is rendered, the distance from the camera will be checked against the specified minimum and maximum range to determine the symbols visibility. Note that symbol ranges for the contained symbols are not exclusive and may overlap.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.FillSymbol
member.summary: A fill symbol defines the appearance of features and graphics that are based on polygon geometries such as countries, provinces, or habitats.

remarks.para: is the base class for different symbols, such as and . A can also be used in combination with a to add an outline to the fill symbol.

You can specify the symbology of a single graphic using . Alternatively, you can create a that contains a collection of symbols and rules. Each rule determines which symbol is applied to a graphic or feature depending on its attribute values. To symbolize graphics, apply a renderer to the , and to symbolize features, apply the renderer to .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.FillSymbolLayer
member.summary: A base class for fill symbol layers.

remarks.para: Fill symbol layers are components of symbols that cover areal geometries. They are most often used in polygon symbols.
The following types of fill symbol layers are supported:

list.item: - fills polygonal geometry with a single solid color (that may have a level of transparency applied).
- uses an image file (specified using a URI or image data) to define the fill.
- defines a pattern of uniform parallel lines.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.GeometricEffect
member.summary: A base class for multilayer symbol geometric effect.
member.remarks: Geometric effects are components of display rules that dynamically alter the representation of a symbol layer. If a multi layer symbol has multiple layers and an effect is applied to a specific layer it will alter only that layer of the symbol. Currently only dash geometric effect is supported.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.HatchFillSymbolLayer
member.summary: A class that implements hatch fill symbol layer inside of the geometry attached to a multilayer polygon symbol.
member.remarks: The HatchFillSymbolLayer provides a similar experience to the SimpleFillSymbol, but with more control. The HatchFillSymbolLayer allows you to specify a fully-customized symbol to use as the hatch line. You can also adjust line angle, offset, and separation distance. A HatchFillSymbolLayer represents a polygon geometry filled with hatch lines. "Hatch lines" are uniformly spaced parallel lines. A HatchFillSymbolLayer, in a multilayer polygon symbol, fills the geometry of the symbol with the specified hatch lines. Each hatch line is itself a multilayer polyline symbol.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.HatchFillSymbolLayer.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.HatchFillSymbolLayer.#ctor(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MultilayerPolylineSymbol,System.Double)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class using an angle.
member.remarks: The fill rotates counterclockwise from East. Angle values can be negative.
member.param: A double value representing the angle to rotate the hatch lines, in degrees measured counterclockwise from East.
a MultilayerPolylineSymbol to use as the hatch line of the symbol layer.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.HatchFillSymbolLayer.Angle
member.summary: Gets or Sets the angle of rotation of the hatch lines in a HatchFillSymbolLayer, in degrees counterclockwise from East.
member.remarks: Get the angle of rotation of the hatch lines in a HatchFillSymbolLayer. The returned values are expressed in degrees measured counterclockwise from East. If there is no angle set, the method returns 0.0.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.HatchFillSymbolLayer.Polyline
member.summary: Gets or sets the polyline symbol used to symbolize the hatch lines of a HatchFillSymbolLayer.
member.remarks: The returned value will be a MultilayerPolylineSymbol. If there is no polyline set, the method returns a default polyline. That default polyline has a single SolidStrokeSymbolLayer, with width of 1.3333 DIPs (1.0 points) and opaque black color.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.HatchFillSymbolLayer.Separation
member.summary: Gets or sets the distance between the line symbols in the hatch pattern of a HatchFillSymbolLayer, in DIPs.
member.remarks: The returned values are expressed in DIPs. If there is no separation set, the method returns 5.3333 DIPs, converted from the internal default 4.0 points. Separation values must be non-negative.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.LineSymbol
member.summary: A base class for line symbols used to symbolize graphics and features (collectively referred to as geoelements) that have polyline geometry.
member.remarks: Symbols describe how graphics and features look on a map. Different symbols are used with different geometry types. Line symbols are used to display graphics and features that are based on polyline geometries such as boundaries, transportation routes and utility pipelines. Line symbols can also be used as an outline for fill symbols () and marker symbols ().

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.LineSymbol.Color
member.summary: Gets or sets the line symbol color.

remarks.para: When getting the color (or tint color) from a symbol, the consensus value (for all layers) is reported. If there is no consensus (one or more layers have different colors), a lack of consensus is reported. Likewise, setting a value for color on a symbol will apply that color to all layers the symbol contains.

If you don't want changes to the symbol color to propagate to some of the symbol layers it contains, you can lock the color (or tint color) for those layers. If a symbol layer is color-locked, changes to the parent symbol will not affect the symbol layer. This also means that the layer will not be considered when determining a consensus color for the symbol as a whole.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.LineSymbol.Width
member.summary: Gets or sets the thickness of the line symbol in device-independent pixels (DIPs).
member.remarks: The default value is 1.0.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.LineSymbol.AntiAlias
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether anti-aliasing should be enabled for the line symbol.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MarkerSceneSymbol
member.summary: A base class for 3D marker scene symbols used to display graphics and features (collectively referred to as geoelements) that have points or multipoint geometry.
member.remarks: Symbols describe how graphics and features look on a scene. Different symbols are used with different geometry types. Marker scene symbols are used to display graphics and features that are based on point or multipoint geometries on a 3D surface (a scene). Subclasses of this class represent specific types of marker scene symbols, such as simple symbols () or 3D models ().

member.summary: A base class for marker symbols used to display graphics and features (collectively referred to as geoelements) that have points or multipoint geometry.

remarks.para: Symbols describe how graphics and features look on a map. Different symbols are used with different geometry types. Marker symbols are used to display graphics and features that are based on point or multipoint geometries such as cities, a route destination, or fire hydrants. Subclasses of this class represent specific types of marker symbols such as , , or .
You can specify the following two types of offsets:

list.item: Offset the visible symbol from the symbol's point geometry.
Offset a callout's leader line from the symbol's point geometry.

remarks.para: For each offset, you can specify an x-offset and a y-offset in device independent pixels (DIPs) using a Cartesian coordinate system. For example, if you specify a callout x-offset of -5.0, the callout's leader line will offset to the left along the x-axis by five points.

By default, the center of a image is placed at the location. If the image is a pushpin, for example, you may want the needle of the pushpin to end at the location. In this situation, you need to offset the symbol to make the needle's end coincide with that location.
You can control the rotation of the marker symbol using the . However, you must also consider whether this rotation is relative to the map or screen ().

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MarkerSymbol.Angle
member.summary: Gets or sets the angle (in degrees) that the marker symbol is rotated by.
member.remarks: The marker symbol can be rotated relative to the map or the screen, depending on the .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MarkerSymbol.AngleAlignment
member.summary: Gets or sets the indicates whether the marker symbol is rotated relative to the screen or map.

remarks.para: If the value is , the marker symbol is rotated relative to the map and will be affected by the map's rotation. If the value is , the marker symbol is rotated relative to the user's screen and is unaffected by the map's rotation.

When the is , each is aligned with the MapView. If the MapView rotates, every marker symbol on the will stay in the same orientation as the MapView. Even if you set and , they are not respected when the is set to static.
When the is set to , each rotates independently of a MapView rotation. If you set and , they are respected.
The default value is .

member.summary: Gets or sets the callout leader's X offset relative to the marker symbol.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MarkerSymbol.LeaderOffsetY
member.summary: Gets or sets the callout leader's Y offset relative to the marker symbol.

member.summary: Gets or sets the offset X of the marker symbol relative to the graphic or feature's point geometry.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MarkerSymbol.OffsetY
member.summary: Gets or sets the offset Y of the marker symbol relative to the graphic or feature's point geometry.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MarkerSymbolLayer
member.summary: A base class for marker symbol layers, representing a symbol layer used to display a marker.

remarks.para: MarkerSymbolLayer draws a shape or picture at a specific location relative to point geometry. A marker symbol layer is most often used in point symbols, but they can also be included in polyline and polygon symbols to draw a symbol at locations along lines and outlines, or within areas (the center of a polygon, for example).
The following types of marker symbol layers are supported:

list.item: - Vector geometry defines the shape and appearance of the marker.
- Uses an image file (specified using a URI or image data) to define the marker.

remarks.para: This symbol layer controls many common operations vital for working with markers. Anchors, angles, offsets, and size properties are controlled on MarkerSymbolLayer.

The MarkerSymbolLayer has methods affecting both 2D and 3D markers. Some properties apply only to 3D markers others only affect 2D markers. Properties that affect the marker's position in screen space, not world space. This means that the x-axis for a 2D marker runs from negative-x on the West edge of the screen to positive-x on the East edge of the screen. Negative-y to positive-y runs from the South edge of the screen to the North edge of the screen.

Properties that apply only to 3D markers affect the marker's position in geographic space, rather than screen space. This means that the x, y, and z-axes to which the class methods are the axes of the world, with the following layout: from negative to positive, the x-axis spans from West to East, the y-axis from South to North, and the z-axis from directly into the surface to the sky.

While a simple text symbol () can be used to display a character as a marker, proper display is dependent on the referenced font being available on the machine or device running the app. If the exact font is not found, which is likely to occur on many mobile devices, a fallback mechanism is used to find the best available font to use. A character marker symbol authored from ArcGIS Pro, however, is represented by a vector marker symbol. This symbol stores the geometry that represents the character, is not dependent on the font to render the shape of the marker, and displays as expected on all devices.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MarkerSymbolLayer.Anchor
member.summary: Gets or sets the anchor object of a marker symbol layer.
member.remarks: The determines how is anchored. SymbolAnchor specifies the marker's anchor point and anchor placement mode. The default symbol anchor is at XYZ(0, 0, 0) and uses 'relative' mode. See the for a detailed description of the object.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MarkerSymbolLayer.Heading
member.summary: Gets or sets the marker symbol layer's heading: the angle of rotation about the anchor point.

remarks.para: The input value can be any real number. The default value is 0. This property affects markers in both maps and scenes. Positive angles rotate the marker counterclockwise.

Angle for is measured in degrees counterclockwise from 0 to 360. Marker symbol layers can have a separate heading value defined (which is also 0 by default). Setting a heading for a marker symbol layer does not affect the angle of the point symbol. When an angle value is set for the point symbol, however, that value is applied cumulatively to all the marker symbol layers it contains. The heading for individual symbol layers must be re-fetched in order to see the updated value. In 2D, the rotation applies around the anchor point of the 2D marker. For 3D markers, the rotation applies around the z-axis.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MarkerSymbolLayer.OffsetX
member.summary: Gets or sets the marker symbol layer's x-offset.
member.remarks: The return value may be any real number (default is 0). This property affects markers in both Maps and Scenes.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MarkerSymbolLayer.OffsetY
member.summary: Gets or sets the marker symbol layer's y-offset.
member.remarks: The return value may be any real number (default is 0). This property affects markers in both Maps and Scenes.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MarkerSymbolLayer.Size
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of marker symbol layer.
member.remarks: The return value may be any real number. The default value is 13.333 DIPs (10 points). When getting the size from a multilayer point symbol, the value reported is the largest size of all the symbol layers it contains. The symbol size is updated when its symbol layer sizes change (if there is a new largest layer size). When setting a new size at the point symbol level, the size of the largest symbol layer is given the provided value, and all the smaller layers are sized proportionately. An individual symbol layer can be given a specific size without affecting the other layers.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MaterialFillSymbolLayer
member.summary: A fill used to render 3D volumetric meshes. It can also render the surfaces of 2D polygon geometries in a scene view.

remarks.para: A is a symbol layer used to shade a geometry with a material that defines a color by blending the color property with the geometry's color/texture information based on the selected .

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.remarks: Creates a material fill symbol layer. The color of the created material layer will initialize to white, RGBA(255,255,255, 255), color mix mode as multiply and edges as null. With the default color and color mix mode, the color mixing result is identical to the original color/texture of the meshes or geometries.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.ModelSceneSymbol
member.summary: Used to display point or multipoint geoelements in a scene using a 3D model.

remarks.para: By default, the symbol will be positioned using the centroid of the model object. Note that this may not be the same as the center of its bounding extent. Refer to the 'Symbol types' topic in the developers guide for a list of supported 3D model types. Model scene symbols cannot be used with a static graphics rendering mode ().
The supported model file types are:

list.item: 3D Max (.3ds)
Collada (.dae)
Filmbox (.fbx)
GL Transmission Format (.gltf, .glb)
Wavefront (.obj)

remarks.para: A model symbol is used to provide a realistic three-dimensional visualization for scene features. Create a ModelSceneSymbol using a 3D model file, passing the URL of the file and a scale factor to the model symbol constructor. Depending on the model file's default orientation, you may have to rotate the symbol to get your model in the desired orientation.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MultilayerPointSymbol
member.summary: A point symbol built with multiple layers of symbols to display graphics and features that have point or multipoint geometry.

remarks.para: A can contain multiple symbol layers that are combined to create different appearances, for example, concentric circles or a pushpin with a star drawn in a different color. You can access and modify the symbology of layers using . You can also change properties such as the , , and . For more information about multilayer symbols, refer to Symbol layers.

If you create a from a collection of objects, the first symbol layer in the collection will be drawn first, with the second symbol layer on top of the first, and so on. Note that symbol layers in a multilayer symbol are in reverse order of how they appear in the JSON representation.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MultilayerSymbol
member.summary: A base class for multilayer symbols. Multilayer symbols are built from multiple layers of symbols, which combine to let you add complex symbols to maps and scenes.

remarks.para: Multilayer symbols can be returned from feature layers based on web maps, feature services (with Use Advanced Symbology set to true), or mobile map packages. They can also be created from JSON by calling . You can programmatically create and change them using the , , and subclasses. You can access the layers that comprise the symbol through and modify their properties, such as the color of a multilayer symbol. Some subclasses also expose additional symbol properties.

The symbol layers that comprise the symbol can be of different types. For example, a polyline geometry may be drawn with a that has line (stroke) symbol layers that draw the line geometry, and also point (marker) symbol layers that are drawn at specified point locations relative to the line geometry. When you save an ArcGIS Pro map as a web map, any multilayer symbols in the map are converted to simple symbols. In general, point symbols are converted to picture marker symbols optimized for the web, and line and polygon symbols are simplified while representing the original symbol as closely as possible. If you're authoring a feature service from ArcGIS Pro, however, both the original symbols and the simplified symbols are stored. The stored symbols allow clients that support advanced symbols to render the features as originally symbolized, while those that do not support advanced symbols (such as ArcGIS Online Map Viewer) can use the simple symbols for display. Having both sets of symbols allows you to retain the advanced symbology where available and still share the feature service as widely as possible. Symbols used by web scenes are also multilayer but use a different symbol specification than ArcGIS Pro and this API. When you read symbols from a web scene in your app, you get a representation of the multilayer symbol.

If you create a multilayer symbol from a collection of objects, the first symbol layer in the collection will be drawn first, with the second symbol layer on top of the first, and so on. Note that symbol layers in a multilayer symbol are in reverse order of how they appear in the JSON representation.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MultilayerSymbol.Color
member.summary: Gets or sets the consensus color of all layers in the symbol (excluding those that are color-locked).
member.remarks: A may have many symbol layers. This property represents the value of all color and tint color properties of constituent symbol layers, excluding any in a , as those symbol layers are outlines. The property defines the color and tint color of symbols contained in . Constituent symbol layers whose colorLocked property is true do not change color as a result of this function. To change outline colors, modify the outline symbol layer colors individually.

member.remarks: Symbol layers in a multilayer symbol are in reverse order of how they appear in the JSON representation from the server.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.PictureFillSymbolLayer.TintColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the tint color of a picture fill symbol layer.

remarks.para: The tint color is applied to the entire image by multiplying each RGB color channel in the image by the corresponding color channel of the tint color. Tinting the image white (R=255, G=255, B=255) with any transparency will leave the native colors.

When getting the tint color from a symbol, the consensus value is reported. If there is no consensus (one or more layers have different colors), a value is reported for the color. Likewise, setting a value for tint color on a symbol will apply that tint color to all layers the symbol contains.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.PictureMarkerSymbol.Opacity
member.summary: Gets or sets the opacity for the picture marker symbol.
member.remarks: The value ranges from 0.0 (fully transparent) to 1.0 (opaque). The default value is 1.0.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.PictureMarkerSymbol.Width
member.summary: Gets or sets the image width.
member.remarks: The width of the symbol spanning from the left to the right side of the image. The default value is 0.0. You can use this property to override the width of the symbol. Note that if you set the or to 0.0, then the picture fill symbol will adopt the height and width of the loaded image.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.PictureMarkerSymbol.Height
member.summary: Gets or sets the height of the picture marker symbol in device-independent pixels (DIPs).
member.remarks: The height of the symbol spanning from the bottom to the top of the image. The default value is 0.0. You can use this property to override the height of the symbol. Note that if you set the or to 0.0, then the picture fill symbol will adopt the height and width of the loaded image.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.PictureMarkerSymbol.Uri
member.summary: Gets or sets the URI of the picture marker symbol.

remarks.para: The URI is the address location of where the image is being stored. This address can come from an online source or from a locally stored location. In order to use a from a URI, the symbol must be loaded asynchronously. Any properties that are changed while this symbol is loading will persist once the image has loaded.

If this symbol was created from a URI, this property has a value; it can be changed as long as the symbol has not been used yet. If this symbol was created from image data instead, this property is null and it cannot be set.

member.exception: This symbol was created from image data and its cannot be set.
Property cannot be set to null.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.PictureMarkerSymbolLayer
member.summary: A struct that implements picture marker symbol layers.

remarks.para: A PictureMarkerSymbolLayer represents a symbol layer used to place a picture marker on a point geometry.

This symbol layer, in a MultilayerPointSymbol, places a picture marker at the location of the point feature. The class supports changing dimensions of the marker, specifying the image (or URI linking to the image) to use as the marker, as well as the general marker options provided by the base class.

This symbol layer is similar to the Web Scene specification's IconSymbol3DLayer, when the IconSymbol3DLayer references an image. is also similar to the Web Map specification's . As a symbol layer, can be combined with other symbol layers in a , whereas cannot.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.PictureMarkerSymbolLayer.#ctor(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.RuntimeImage)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class from a RuntimeImage.

remarks.para: The is set up with following default properties: Anchor at (0, 0), offset of (0, 0), size of 13.33 DIPs (10 points), an heading of 0 degrees, and an empty URI. If the image is valid, the function sets the image on the symbol layer, and sets the load status of the to .

member.param: A loaded RuntimeImage.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.PictureMarkerSymbolLayer.#ctor(System.Uri)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class from a RuntimeImage.
member.remarks: This is set up the following default properties: Anchor at (0, 0), offset of (0, 0), size of 13.33 DIPs (10 points), an angle of 0 degrees, width and height of (0, 0), and a null image. The function sets the URI to the string provided.
member.param: The URI location of the image to use in the symbol layer.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.PictureMarkerSymbolLayer.Uri
member.summary: Gets the Uri of a picture marker symbol layer.
member.remarks: If there is no Uri set, the method returns an empty string.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.PictureMarkerSymbolLayer.Image
member.summary: Gets the image of a picture marker symbol layer.
member.remarks: If there is no image set, value is null.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.PictureMarkerSymbolLayer.TintColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the tint color of a picture marker symbol layer.

remarks.para: The tint color is applied to the entire image by multiplying each RGB color channel in the image by the corresponding color channel of the tint color. Tinting the image white (R=255, G=255, B=255) with any transparency will leave the native colors.

When getting the tint color from a symbol, the consensus value is reported. If there is no consensus (one or more layers have different colors), a value is reported for the tint color. Likewise, setting a value for tint color on a symbol will apply that tint color to all layers the symbol contains.

member.summary: The rotation value is clockwise from North.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.RotationType.Arithmetic

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Renderer
member.summary: A base class for renderers that use a collection of one or more symbols to display features in a or graphics in a .

remarks.para: If the renderer contains more than a single symbol, it uses the rules to determine the symbol to apply to each geoelement, based on one or several attribute values. There are three basic types of renderers:

list.item: - simple renderers use a single symbol for all geoelements.
- unique value renders define a different symbol for each unique value of an attribute.
- class break renderers define a different symbol for each range of numeric values.

remarks.para: To symbolize and display the geoelements, apply the renderer to the , the , or the .

A can contain graphics with different geometry dimensions, such as a and . A , however, can only provide symbols with one geometry dimension. Therefore, if you apply a renderer to a graphics overlay, only graphics with the specified geometry dimension are displayed. All other graphics are not visible.
If you apply a directly to a graphic, the symbol displays in preference to the .
For raster data, rendering is handled by a .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Renderer.RotationExpression
member.summary: Gets or sets the expression describing how 's attributes are translated into a rotation to be applied to the .
member.remarks: When an attribute name is specified in the rotation expression, it is enclosed in square brackets, for example: [Rotation].

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Renderer.RotationType
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the rotation calculated from the is relative to the screen or map.

remarks.para: The options are:

list.item: - rotation is along the x-axis, with positive rotation being counter-clockwise.
- rotation is along the y-axis, with positive rotation being clockwise.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Renderer.SceneProperties
member.summary: Gets or sets the scene properties for the renderer.
member.remarks: This can be used to make changes to how a is displayed in a scene view.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Renderer.UnknownJson
member.summary: Gets unknown data from the source JSON.
member.remarks: Unknown JSON is a dictionary of values not defined in the ArcGIS specification used to create this object but found in the source JSON. If the object is written back to JSON, any unknown JSON data is not persisted. The ArcGIS specification may be for a web map, web scene, REST API, and so on.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Renderer.UnsupportedJson
member.summary: Gets unsupported data from the source JSON.
member.remarks: Unsupported JSON is a dictionary of values defined in the ArcGIS specification used to create this object but not currently used in this API. If the object is written back to JSON, any unsupported JSON data is persisted. The ArcGIS specification may be from a web map, web scene, REST API, and so on.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Renderer.GetSymbol(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Data.GeoElement)
member.summary: Returns the symbol the renderer uses to represent the supplied .
member.remarks: If the renderer is a and its associated references a style file hosted on ArcGIS Online or an ArcGIS Enterprise portal (also referred to as a web style), this method returns null. If the layer has rendered and the symbol is already drawn on the view, this method returns a valid . If you want to get the symbol before the layer is rendered, use .
member.param: The input .
member.returns: The used to represent the GeoElement.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Renderer.GetSymbol(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Data.GeoElement,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns the symbol used to visualize the given GeoElement with override attributes from renderer.

remarks.para: If is set to true, this method will get the symbol from the renderer and override the symbol properties with the overrides available on the renderer. These include visual variable size, color, opacity and rotation. If the override expression or attributes contain any information not known to either the renderer or observation (such as "scale"), that override will not be applied. If the override attributes are applied successfully, a symbol with overridden properties will be returned else the original symbol will be returned.

If the renderer is a and its associated references a style file hosted on ArcGIS Online or an ArcGIS Enterprise portal (also referred to as a web style), this method returns null. If the layer has rendered and the symbol is already drawn on the view, this method returns a valid . If you want to get the symbol before the layer is rendered, use .

member.param: The input .
A value indicating whether override attributes from renderer is applied.
member.returns: The used to represent the GeoElement.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SolidFillSymbolLayer
member.summary: A class that implements solid fill symbol layer.

remarks.para: A SolidFillSymbolLayer represents a symbol layer used to fill a polygon geometry with a solid color. It can have transparency applied.
If a SolidFillSymbolLayer is the only symbol layer in a , it will fill the geometry with the specified color.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SolidFillSymbolLayer.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.remarks: Creates a solid fill symbol layer. The color of the created layer will initialize to black, RGBA(0, 0, 0, 255).

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SolidFillSymbolLayer.#ctor(System.Drawing.Color)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a given color.
member.remarks: Creates a solid fill symbol layer that uses the provided color.
member.param: the desired color for the symbol layer.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SolidFillSymbolLayer.Color
member.summary: Gets or sets the color of a solid fill symbol layer.

remarks.para: A representing the color used in the solid fill. If a color is not explicitly set, the default will be black, ARGB(255, 0, 0, 0).

When getting the color from a symbol, the consensus value (for all layers) is reported. If there is no consensus (one or more layers have different colors), a value is reported for the color. Likewise, setting a value for color on a symbol will apply that color to all layers the symbol contains.

If you don't want changes to the symbol color to propagate to some of the symbol layers it contains, you can lock the color for those layers. If a symbol layer is color-locked, changes to the parent symbol will not affect the symbol layer. This also means that the layer will not be considered when determining a consensus color for the symbol as a whole.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SolidStrokeSymbolLayer
member.summary: A class for stroke symbol layers with customized geometric effects. Currently it supports only dash effects. Commonly used in line symbols to describe the appearance of the line, or in polygon symbols as an outline.

remarks.para: A represents a polyline geometry symbolized with a solid color.

This symbol layer, in a , colors the geometry of the line with the specified solid color. The class also allows setting a collection of geometric effects, which modify the stroke in various ways (see ).

The is used to represent the Web Scene specification's LineSymbol3DLayer and PathSymbol3DLayer. LineSymbol3DLayer maps to the 'strip' 3D line style of , and PathSymbol3DLayer maps to the 'tube' 3D line style.

The provides a similar user experience to the , but since it is a symbol layer, can be used in a . The simple line styles of the are represented here by the , but all dash patterns are custom. A solid stroke symbol layer in a multilayer polygon symbol will act as the outline of the fill.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SolidStrokeSymbolLayer.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

remarks.para: Create a solid stroke symbol layer with no parameters. The default line will have the following default properties: width of 4.0 points (5.333 DIPs), 'round' caps, 'strip' 3D line style, a solid opaque black color, and no geometric effects.
Note that width is measured in DIPs for all line styles except which is measured in meters.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SolidStrokeSymbolLayer.#ctor(System.Double,System.Drawing.Color)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.Create a solid stroke symbol layer with width and color.
member.remarks: Create a solid stroke symbol layer with a stroke width and a color. The function sets the width and color on the newly- created solid stroke. The other properties are defaulted as follows: caps are 'round,' and the 3D line style is 'strip.'
member.param: The desired color for the layer.
The desired width of the layer defined in DIPs for all line styles except which is measured in meters.

member.remarks: Create a solid stroke symbol layer with a stroke width, a color, and a collection of geometric effects. The function sets the width, color, and geometric effects on the newly-created solid stroke. The other properties are defaulted as follows: caps are 'round,' and the 3D line style is 'strip'.
member.param: The desired color for the layer.
The effects to be applied to the layer, e.g., dash patterns.
The desired width of the layer defined in DIPs for all line styles except which is measured in meters.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SolidStrokeSymbolLayer.#ctor(System.Double,System.Drawing.Color,System.Collections.Generic.IList{Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.GeometricEffect},Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayerLineStyle3D)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with width, color, geometric effects and 3D line style.
member.remarks: Create a solid stroke symbol layer with a stroke width, a color, a collection of geometric effects, and a 3D line style. The function sets the width, color, geometric effects, and 3D line style on the newly-created solid stroke. Stroke caps default to 'round'.
member.param: The desired width for the symbol layer defined in DIPs for all line styles except which is measured in meters.
The desired color for the symbol layer.
The effects to be applied to the symbol layer, e.g., dash patterns.
The desired 3D line style.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SolidStrokeSymbolLayer.Color
member.summary: Gets or sets the color of a solid stroke symbol layer.

remarks.para: The solid color of the . The stroke will render with the specified color. When getting the color from a symbol, the consensus value (for all layers) is reported. If there is no consensus (one or more layers have different colors), a value is reported for the color. Likewise, setting a value for color on a symbol will apply that color to all layers the symbol contains.

If you don't want changes to the symbol color to propagate to some of the symbol layers it contains, you can lock the color for those layers. If a symbol layer is color-locked, changes to the parent symbol will not affect the symbol layer. This also means that the layer will not be considered when determining a consensus color for the symbol as a whole.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SolidStrokeSymbolLayer.GeometricEffects
member.summary: Gets the collection of geometric effects held by a solid stroke symbol layer.
member.remarks: The default value is an empty collection.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayer
member.summary: A StrokeSymbolLayer represents a symbol layer that, when applied to a polyline geometry, draws a stroke along that geometry. The StrokeSymbolLayer can also control drawing the outline of a polygon geometry.
member.remarks: Applied to a polyline geometry, the StrokeSymbolLayer will draw a segment of stroke adhering to the selected visual properties for each line segment. Similarly, as the outline of a polygon geometry, the StrokeSymbolLayer draws a stroke segment for each line segment of the polygon outline. This symbol layer controls many common operations vital for working with strokes. Cap style and line style 3D are controllable on the StrokeSymbolLayer. These properties provide the ability to pick between various options for rendering strokes. Line style 3D applies only in 3D, but cap style and width have effects in both 2D and 3D.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayer.CapStyle
member.summary: Gets or sets the cap style of stroke symbol layer.
member.remarks: The default cap style is 'round,' meaning that the ends of the stroke are rounded off. Cap styles affect strokes in Map Views, as well as the 'strip' and 'tube' line styles in Scene Views.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayer.LineStyle3D
member.summary: Gets or sets the 3D line style of the stroke symbol layer.
member.remarks: The default 3D line style is 'strip,' meaning that the 3D stroke is flat. 3D line style has no effect on strokes in Map Views, but does affect strokes in Scene Views.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayer.Width
member.summary: Gets or sets the width of a stroke symbol layer.
member.remarks: The return value may be any non-negative number (default is 0). A larger width means a larger stroke. Width is measured in DIPs for all line styles except which is measured in meters.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayerCapStyle
member.summary: The list of possible cap styles.
member.remarks: The cap style describes the way that line symbol layers will terminate when combined with a geometry. The term "cap" refers to the end of the line. The options here control the shape that cap will take. In dynamic rendering mode, caps are always rendered with the "butt" style.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayerCapStyle.Butt
member.summary: Applying the "butt" cap style will terminate the line ending exactly where the geometry ends. The end cap will be squared off. That is, two 90 degree angles form a butted ending to the symbol.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayerCapStyle.Round
member.summary: Applying the "round" cap style will terminate the line ending with a semicircle of radius equal to the stroke width. That semicircle will be centered at the line endpoint.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayerCapStyle.Square
member.summary: Applying the "square" cap style will extend the termination of the line ending past the end of the geometry. The end cap will be squared off. That is, two 90 degree angles form a squared ending to the symbol.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayerLineStyle3D
member.summary: The list of possible 3D line styles.
member.remarks: The 3D line style describes the way that line symbol layers will render in 3D. The property refers to the rendering style of a 3D stroke layer. In a scene view, changing this property will change the fundamental appearance of the stroke layer. Only the 3D line style is supported for static rendering mode.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayerLineStyle3D.Tube
member.summary: The tube style causes the stroke to appear as a 3D tube. A "tube" here refers to a solid circular cylinder, following the given geometry.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.StrokeSymbolLayerLineStyle3D.Strip
member.summary: The strip style causes the stroke to appear as a flat strip of surface. Note that the cap style for this line is always StrokeSymbolLayerCapStyle_butt. A "strip" here refers to a flat 2D surface with more significant width than a line, though it is presented in 3D.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.Symbol
member.summary: A symbol defines the appearance of features and graphics that are displayed in a .

remarks.para: is the base class for a number of different symbols, such as , , , and , which are subclasses for more specialized symbols.

You can specify the symbology of a single graphic using . Alternatively, you can create a that contains a collection of symbols and rules. Each rule determines which symbol is applied to a graphic or feature depending on its attribute values. To symbolize graphics, apply a renderer to the , and to symbolize features, apply the renderer to .

There are two models for defining symbols in your map: simple and advanced (multilayer). In general, simple symbols are single-layer symbols that provide basic representations, such as marker, line, fill, text, or picture. Advanced symbols are composed of one or several layers that can be defined individually and combined to create complex representations. Both of these are more fully described as follows:

list.item: Simple symbols follow the web map specification and you work with them through the simple symbol classes. These are the symbols you get from web maps or from feature services when advanced symbology is turned off. Simple symbols can be created for points (marker symbols), lines (line symbols), and polygons (fill symbols). Each of the simple symbol types provides an enumeration of pre-defined styles that can be applied to the symbol.

Advanced symbols, accessed through multilayer symbol classes, follow the ArcGIS Pro symbol model also referred to as CIM symbols. These symbols come from feature services (that use advanced symbology), mobile style files, the dictionary renderer, and mobile map packages. You can also build your own advanced symbols for points, lines, and polygons.

remarks.para: The ArcGIS Pro web map publishing tool offers two choices when saving a web map. You can either choose to save advanced multilayer CIM symbols in the web map, or choose to downgrade the symbols to save simple symbols to ensure compatibility between all clients. If you choose to downgrade, point symbols are converted to picture marker symbols optimized for the web, and line and polygon symbols are simplified while representing the original symbol as closely as possible. If you're authoring a feature service from ArcGIS Pro or ArcGIS Desktop, however, both the original symbols and the simplified symbols are stored. This allows clients that support advanced symbols to render the features as originally symbolized, while those that do not support advanced symbols (such as ArcGIS Online Map Viewer) can use the simple symbols for display. Having both sets of symbols allows you to retain the advanced symbology where available and still share the feature service as widely as possible.

If your app works primarily with web maps that you want to look the same throughout the platform, your app should use the simple symbols API. Otherwise, make sure your users understand that advanced symbols render slightly differently on clients that don't support advanced symbology.

If your maps are used only this API and ArcGIS Pro, you can use advanced symbols exclusively. When rendered by this API, advanced symbols are vectorized. This allows them to scale better on devices with high resolution screens.
You can set to control whether the map uses advanced symbols (when available) or always renders with simple symbols.
A swatch is an image that is used to display this symbol. Properties of a swatch include:

list.item: Height, length of image from bottom to top side.
Width, length of image from left to right side.
Dots Per Inch (dpi), the resolution of the image.
Color, interior color of the image.
Geometry, shape of the image.

remarks.para: Depending on the of a or different types of symbols will be used.

member.summary: Creates a swatch image of this symbol, with the specified image size, dpi, background color, and geometry.

remarks.para: and may need to be adjusted if is different from its default value of 96. This method does not scale the symbol in order to fit or fill the entire image. The symbol is rendered at its actual size, which may result in some cropping or padding.

The should be specified DIPs, with the point {0,0} located at the centerpoint of the swatch image. The X-axis increases towards the right-hand side of the swatch image. The Y-axis increases towards the top of the swatch image. Geometry type should correspond to symbol type: for , for , and for . Given geometry's spatial reference is ignored.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAnchor
member.summary: Implements the anchor of a symbol layer, including placement mode and x, y anchor values.
member.remarks: SymbolAnchor contains the properties necessary to define the anchor for a symbol layer. These anchor properties define both the anchor values (x, y) and the placement mode. This can be passed to MarkerSymbolLayer objects to control marker anchor properties.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAnchor.#ctor(System.Double,System.Double,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAnchorPlacementMode)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class and sets up the properties.
member.remarks: The function sets up the SymbolAnchor with the provided arguments. InvalidArgument exception is raised when either the x or y value is NAN.
member.param: The anchor placement mode to use for the anchor values.
The x-component of the anchor point.
The y-component of the anchor point.
member.exception: x or y value must be a number.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAnchor.PlacementMode
member.summary: Gets the anchor placement mode of the symbol anchor object.
member.remarks: The SymbolAnchorPlacementMode determines how Runtime interprets the SymbolAnchor anchor values. See the SymbolAnchorPlacementMode documentation for a description of the effects of each mode. The default mode is 'relative.'

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAnchor.X
member.summary: Gets the x-component of the symbol anchor object.
member.remarks: The x-component of the SymbolAnchor can be any real number. The units of the value depend on the anchor placement mode of the SymbolAnchor. The default value is 0, regardless of placement mode.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAnchor.Y
member.summary: Gets the y-component of the symbol anchor object.
member.remarks: The y-component of the SymbolAnchor can be any real number. The units of the value depend on the anchor placement mode of the SymbolAnchor. The default value is 0, regardless of placement mode.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAnchorPlacementMode
member.summary: The list of possible symbol anchor placement modes.
member.remarks: SymbolAnchorPlacementMode enumerates the ways in which symbol anchor values may be specified. Different placement modes control how anchor values are interpreted.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAnchorPlacementMode.Relative
member.summary: Specify anchor with relative values. This mode causes anchor values to be interpreted as percentages, relative to the origin of the symbol. Percentage values must be expressed as fractions between [0, 1]. A 50% (.5) x-anchor, for example, moves the symbol layer anchor in the positive-x direction an amount equal to 50% of the symbol layer size.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAnchorPlacementMode.Absolute
member.summary: Specify anchor with absolute values. This mode causes anchor values to be interpreted as absolute units (DIPs) rather than percentages.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAngleAlignment
member.summary: Defines the way the rotation angle is used by the marker symbol

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAngleAlignment.Screen
member.summary: Rotate the marker relative to the screen, usually 'Up'.
member.remarks: If the map rotates, the marker symbol will not rotate with the map but stay 'upright'.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolAngleAlignment.Map
member.summary: Rotate the marker relative to the map, usually 'North'.
member.remarks: If the map rotates, the marker symbol will rotate with the map.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolLayer
member.summary: A base class to represent symbol layers, which are the fundamental components used to build multilayer symbols.

remarks.para: The available symbol layer types correspond to the primitive geometry they are designed to render: marker layers for point, stroke layers for line, and fill layers for area or polygons. Layer types, however, are not applied exclusively to the corresponding geoelement geometry type. A marker symbol layer, for example, could be applied to a polyline feature to render its vertices. Each layer type has a unique set of properties to control its display. A symbol can contain one or several symbol layers of the same or different type. A symbol used to render polygons, for example, might contain a fill symbol layer to render the polygon interior, a stroke symbol layer for rendering the polygon outline, and a marker symbol layer to render polygon vertices. A symbol to render a polyline might contain several stroke layers of varying widths. If a symbol contains a layer that cannot render the type of geometry to which it is applied, that symbol layer is ignored when rendering.

The following table lists symbol types and symbol layers:
Symbol typeTypical symbol layersRole
Point symbol
Marker layers
Draw relative to the point geometry
Polyline symbol
Marker layers
Draw at specified locations relative to the line geometry
Stroke layers
Draw relative to the line geometry
Polygon symbol
Marker layers
Draw relative to the polygon outline or in the interior, depending on placement setting
Stroke layers
Draw relative to the polygon outline
Fill layers
Draw relative to the polygon interior
Symbol and layer property interaction

Most properties are only accessible on the symbol layer. There are some properties, however, that are also exposed for the symbol as a whole. Properties such as color and size, for example, can be modified either for an individual symbol layer, or for the symbol itself. For such properties, the value provided for the symbol may have an effect on the corresponding property for the symbol layers it contains. Similarly, the property value for a multilayer symbol might be determined from the values of the individual symbol layers it contains. There are no notifications from a symbol or symbol layer when a property has been changed. If your app needs to respond to property changes in a symbol (as a result of a propagated change, for example) you will need to reread property values to identify changes.
Unknown symbol layers or properties

The multilayer symbol specification is complex and continues to evolve. It is possible that some symbol layers (or properties) do not expose an API or are not known to the this API. In these cases the symbol will be handled as follows:

list.item: If a symbol can be rendered, but there is no public API, the base class for the appropriate symbol layer is returned in the layer collection.

The multilayer symbology specification for an existing symbol layer type may have been updated to include properties that are not recognized by this API. The symbol layer is created and rendered using the recognized properties. Any properties that cannot be identified are ignored.
In the event that a new (unrecognized) symbol layer is encountered, the symbol is ignored and is not added to the symbol layer collection.

remarks.para: Edge rendering visualization is specific for 3D visualizations. It can be used to emphasize the form of 3D objects and to improve the three-dimensional spatial understanding. Edges are currently accessible only on .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolReferenceProperties
member.summary: Symbol properties that changes how symbols behave when attached to a renderer.
member.remarks: SymbolReferenceProperties contains conditional properties that are not used directly by the symbol but rather by the renderer to change how a symbol is visualized based on the state of the view and the data. For example, and will be used by class breaks and unique value renderers to determine if a class break or unique value is visible at a certain scale. Additional symbols can be assigned as alternatives to the primary symbol. This allows the map author to control which symbol is displayed at certain scales.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolStyle
member.summary: A symbol style object.

remarks.para: A SymbolStyle is created either from a style file on disk (an SQLite database with a .stylx extension, created with ArcGIS Pro) or from a style file hosted on ArcGIS Online or an ArcGIS Enterprise portal (also referred to as a web style).

When used by itself, the symbol style supports two key workflows. One is to return a symbol based on a provided set of key values. Since each symbol in the style has a key that uniquely identifies it, you can pass an array of keys into to return a new built from the individual symbols represented by those keys. You might use this symbol to create a new .

The other common workflow is to use a standalone symbol style to search for symbol primitives. Symbol primitives are the individual symbols that compose more complex symbols. This is achieved using . You could created a symbol picker app, for example, that searches for all symbols in the style that have the tag "maritime" in it. The search results contain symbols that you could use directly. You could also extract their individual symbol components to create a new multilayer symbol.

member.param: Physical location of the .stylx file. Need to provide the full path (e.g. ../../../mil2525c/mil2525c.stylx).
member.returns: The symbol style at the specified path.

member.remarks: Use this constructor if you have a reference to a web style portal item.
member.exception: is thrown if item passed is not a valid symbol web style.

If null is passed, ArcGIS online is used as the default portal.
Esri provides a set of webstyles "out of the box" for localized use with ArcGIS Online and Enterprise. An overview of styles and symbols currently in production is available for 3D and 2D..

Tip: Click on the symbol icon to find it's associated style name. Registered style name can also be found in the item json of the symbols style. For example refer to item json for EsriThematicShapesStyle web style hosted on ArcGIS Online.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolStyle.GetDefaultSearchParametersAsync
member.summary: Returns a Style Symbol Search Parameters object providing the default search parameters from the database.
member.remarks: This asynchronous task retrieves all the valid input search parameters for a style. This means you get all of the possible input values for categories, keys, names, symbolClasses, and tags. This is an expensive task that should be used sparingly.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous save as operation. The value of the task result is a collection of object.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolStyle.SearchSymbolsAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolStyleSearchParameters)
member.summary: Returns a collection of objects.
member.remarks: This asynchronous task searches for symbol primitives in the symbol style. The input searchParameters define what is searched for. For example, you could search for all symbols that have the tag 'airspace'. You can also set the match to be strict or not, which will determine if the search uses '=' or 'LIKE' for each parameter.
member.param: The style symbol search parameters used to search primitives in the symbol dictionary.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous save as operation. The value of the task result is a collection of object.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.SymbolStyle.GetSymbolAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})
member.summary: Asynchronously returns a symbol based on the provided list of keys.
member.remarks: This function is useful for obtaining a single multilayer symbol from supplied keys. This is commonly used to create symbols and graphics on-the-fly. For example, assume XYZ style has a symbol with key "abc" which is the central symbol and another symbol with key "pqr", which could be the modifier/echelon placed at some offset from central geometry. So if you provide these two keys as attributes then symbol style will find and assemble a symbol accordingly. Once you have that symbol, you can apply it to a Graphic or Renderer, obtain its swatch image, or serialize to JSON. If the Symbol style is not loaded then starting this task will start the load cycle.
member.param: of type value. It is the list of keys required to obtain a symbol from the style.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous getSymbol as operation. The value of the task result is a object.

member.summary: Search parameters used to search for symbol primitives in a symbol dictionary.
member.remarks: SymbolStyleSearchParameters allow you to specify different parameters when searching a symbol dictionary with For example, you may want to search for any symbols where the category is "2", the name contains "destroyed", and the tags contain "Protection Lines". You need to pass this handle to all style symbol search parameters functions. This object allows you to set the parameters in order to perform a search on symbol dictionary.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.TextSymbol
member.summary: Defines how text is displayed using characteristics such as font, size, color, and position.

remarks.para: To display text in a MapView or , create a . Specify whether a string or attribute value is displayed by the . Define the appearance and position of the text using , , , or , for example. Position the text by applying it to a based on any type of geometry - point, multipoint, polyline, or polygon. Add the graphic to the collection and add the to the MapView or collection of graphics overlays.
is also used to define the appearance of layer and grid based labels:

list.item: Layers such as , , or contain label definitions that specify how labels are displayed. You can use to define a label appearance within a scale range. To display these labels, add a collection of these label definitions to a layer.
MapView can display a coordinate system grid with specific label styles at different resolutions. You can define the appearance of these labels using .

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class (or unique combination of values) with alternate symbols.
member.param: The description of the unique value. "Parcels zoned for residential use", for example.
The label for the unique value. "Residential", for example.
The symbol used to represent elements with this unique value.
A collection of object values.
A collection of alternate symbols for the unique value. Only are supported as alternates. Symbols in this list should be of type .
member.remarks: A unique value can have alternate symbols to the primary symbol. Alternate symbols allow you to tailor the visualization of a unique value at different scales by selecting different symbols for different scales. Alternate symbols are supported only when unique value's primary symbol and other symbols in alternate symbol list are of type and have defining valid min/max scales at which the symbol becomes visible. Renderer will pick only one symbol at a given map scale. If primary symbol's scale range falls within the map's scale, primary symbol is used. If not, then symbols in alternate symbols list are iterated through and first symbol matching the current map scale is picked for rendering. A symbol becomes visible if the map scale is less than or equal to symbol's minimum scale and greater than symbol's maximum scale. For more information on scale-based symbol classes and alternate symbols see Use scale-based symbol classes.

member.summary: A unique value renderer symbolizes geoelements with a distinct symbol for each unique data value in an attribute.

remarks.para: A unique value renderer contains a collection of . Each has a and a collection of to define a value or combination of values.

This is typically used to visualize categories or data types. For example, you can use a unique value renderer to symbolize zoning designations: yellow for "Residential", purple for "Industrial", and green for both "Forests" and "Open land".

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.VectorMarkerSymbolElement
member.summary: The represents a component of a vector marker. A single VectorMarkerSymbolElement is a combination of a symbol and a geometry.
member.remarks: The symbol and geometry properties must create a meaningful combination to have a visual effect. For example, a MultilayerPolygonSymbol does not draw anything if the corresponding geometry is a point, but does draw if the geometry is a polygon. The symbol held by the VectorMarkerSymbolElement is itself a MultilayerSymbol. The geometry of the does not pertain to the geography of the Map or Scene. Rather, the geometry property describes the shape of the held by the VectorMarkerSymbolElement. The coordinate values defining the geometry are in points. This behavior facilitates creating a custom VectorMarkerSymbolElement of custom shape and symbol, then combining it with zero or more other elements to form a vector marker symbol element. To create a red square, for example, construct a VectorMarkerSymbolElement with a MultilayerPolygonSymbol that contains a red and a square geometry. When combined in a and placed in a a red square displays.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.VectorMarkerSymbolElement.#ctor(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Geometry.Geometry,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.MultilayerSymbol)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a geometry and symbol.
member.remarks: This constructor creates a VectorMarkerSymbolElement using the geometry and symbol arguments provided.
member.param: The geometry that defines the shape of the symbol.
The element's symbol.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.VectorMarkerSymbolElement.Geometry
member.summary: Gets or sets the geometry of the vector marker symbol element.
member.remarks: The default value is nullptr. When rendering, the symbol draws according to the shape of the geometry, which is defined relative to other elements in the layer. The coordinates used to define element geometry are not in real units.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.VectorMarkerSymbolElement.Symbol
member.summary: Gets or sets the symbol of the .
member.remarks: The default value is null. When rendering, the symbol draws according to the shape of the geometry.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.VectorMarkerSymbolLayer
member.summary: The VectorMarkerSymbolLayer represents a marker comprising vector graphics.

remarks.para: The VectorMarkerSymbolLayer is constructed from a collection of which define the building blocks of the vector marker. If it is the sole symbol layer in a , this symbol layer places a vector marker at the location of the point feature.

While a simple text symbol () can be used to display a character as a marker, proper display is dependent on the referenced font being available on the machine or device running the app. If the exact font is not found, which is likely to occur on many mobile devices, a fallback mechanism is used to find the best available font to use. A character marker symbol authored from ArcGIS Pro, however, is represented by a vector marker symbol. This symbol stores the geometry that represents the character, is not dependent on the font to render the shape of the marker, and displays as expected on all devices.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.VectorMarkerSymbolLayer.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IList{Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.VectorMarkerSymbolElement})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a collection of vector marker symbol elements.
member.remarks: This function creates a VectorMarkerSymbolLayer, then sets the given vector of VectorMarkerSymbolElement as the collection comprising the vector marker.
member.param: The collection of elements of type VectorMarkerSymbolElement.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Symbology.VectorMarkerSymbolLayer.VectorMarkerSymbolElements
member.summary: Gets the collection of vector marker symbol elements held by the vector marker symbol layer.
member.remarks: The default value is an empty collection. An empty collection of elements renders nothing. If not empty, the returned collection contains the component elements of the vector marker.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters
member.summary: A class that describes geocode method parameters.
member.remarks: Class holding the parameters to support the geocode operation for a geocoding service or local locator.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters.Categories
member.summary: Gets a mutable list of categories from which addresses should be returned.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters.CountryCode
member.summary: Gets or sets a value representing the country. Providing this value increases geocoding speed. Acceptable values include the full country name, the ISO 3166-1 2-digit country code, or the ISO 3166-1 3-digit country code. A list of supported countries and codes is available here.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters.IsForStorage
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the results of the operation will be persisted.
member.remarks: Specifies whether the results of the operation will be persisted. The default value is false, which indicates the results of the operation cannot be stored. If you store the results, in a database for example, you need to set this parameter to true.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters.MaxResults
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of candidates to be returned by a search, up to the maximum number allowed by the service. By default all found candidates will be returned. The world geocoding service allows up to 20 candidates to be returned for a single request.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters.MinScore
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum score value.
member.remarks: Determines the minimal score value (score threshold) [0..100].

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters.OutputLanguage
member.summary: Gets or sets the output language.
member.remarks: Determines the language of returned candidate labels.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters.OutputSpatialReference
member.summary: Gets or sets the spatial reference of the returned locations. If not specified, the coordinates of the returned locations will be in the same spatial reference as the service.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters.PreferredSearchLocation
member.summary: Gets or sets the preferred search location.
member.remarks: The center point of the preferred area where candidates will be searched.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters.ResultAttributeNames
member.summary: Gets a mutable list of attribute names.
member.remarks: Gets a list of attribute names that should be returned for every candidate. Specify an empty list to return only default attributes. An "*" means all attributes will be returned.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeParameters.SearchArea
member.summary: Gets or sets the geographic area for which searching for locations will be limited to.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeResult
member.summary: Describes a match candidate returned from a geocode or reverse geocode operation.
member.remarks: GeocodeResult provides information about the candidate, such as a collection of requested attribute values, user-friendly label text, and locations suited for accurate match display or for routing input.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeResult.Attributes
member.summary: Gets a collection of attributes as requested by or .
member.remarks: Available attributes depend on the data stored with the locator, and can include things like place name, URL, phone number, and so on.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeResult.DisplayLocation
member.summary: Gets the location of the candidate suitable for display on a map.
member.remarks: For example, this may provide a more precise rooftop location of a house, whereas represents the nearest street location.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeResult.Extent
member.summary: Gets an extent suitable for zooming the map to display the candidate.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeResult.InputLocation
member.summary: Gets the used to .
member.remarks: Only applicable for results of reverse-geocode operations.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeResult.Label
member.summary: Gets the user-friendly text that describes this result.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeResult.RouteLocation
member.summary: Gets nearest street location for the result.
member.remarks: Use this location if you want to use the candidate as a stop in a route. For example, may provide a more precise rooftop location of a house, whereas will provide the nearest street location.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.GeocodeResult.Score
member.summary: Gets a value that indicates how well the address was matched.
member.value: The score is in a range between 0 (no match) and 100 (perfect match).

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.LocatorTask
member.summary: Represents a task that enables geocoding and reverse geocoding against an ArcGIS locator.
member.remarks: Geocoding is the process of assigning a location, usually in the form of coordinate values (points), to an address by comparing the descriptive location elements in the address to those present in the reference material. Addresses come in many forms, ranging from the common address format of a house number followed by the street name and succeeding information to other location descriptions such as postal zone or census tract. An address includes any type of information that distinguishes a place. Reverse geocoding is the process of determining the closest address to a location usually given in the form of a coordinate value (point).

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geocoding.LocatorTask.SuggestAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves a list of possible address variants for a given partial address.
member.remarks: Geocoding suggestions provide a mechanism for implementing character-by-character auto-complete. Using a text input and optional , suggested addresses can be quickly found and presented to the user. Suggestions are not limited to addresses, they can also be used for POIs (Points of Interest). To return a focused set of suggestions, set the or the . Using the is similar to how ArcGIS Pro limits the suggestions returned.
member.param: The partial address for which to get suggestions.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous suggestion operation. The value of the task result contains a read-only collection of objects.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geoprocessing.GeoprocessingFeatures
member.summary: A features (or records) input or output parameter.
member.remarks: Corresponds to GPRecordSet and GPFeatureRecordSetLayer parameter types on the service. A feature contains a and may contains extra attributes on collection. A record (or a row) does not contain and information is stored in attributes on collection. When Features are returned as an output parameter the returned results are provided as a read-only list of Features using . These features can be visualized on a map using temporary s on a or using on a . If used geoprocessing service is configured to use a result map server, then was populated and it can be used to visualize the results using the symbology predefined by the service. When providing as an input to the geoprocessing task, features can be provided directly from the that was returned from the . If there is a need to create a new set of features or records, can be used.

member.remarks: The URL is for input parameters only. For output parameters this is empty. For input it must be a URL that is accessible to the geoprocessing service so it can get the feature set. If this property is set, it will override the property.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geoprocessing.GeoprocessingFeatures.CanGetOutputFeatures
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether features can be returned from the service of an output parameter.
member.remarks: This property applies to output parameters. It indicates the parameter is an output parameter that can get features from the geoprocessing service. Depending on the type of the service the property may already be available. If features are not present then this property indicates that you can use to request features and asynchronously update the property.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geoprocessing.GeoprocessingFeatures.Features
member.summary: Gets or sets the features that are sent to or returned from the geoprocessing service.
member.remarks: For an input parameter, this contains features to be sent to the geoprocessing service. This property will be ignored if property is set. For an output parameter, this can contain features returned from a geoprocessing service depending on the service configuration. It can be null for services that have a map service, in which case calling will get the features from the service or can be used to visualize the results without loading them to the client.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geoprocessing.GeoprocessingLinearUnit
member.summary: A linear unit input or output parameter.
member.remarks: Corresponds to GPLinearUnit parameter type on the service. Geoprocessing tasks support a set of linear units defined in the enumeration. When a parameter is created with one of the supported the value is sent to the service using defined linear unit type. When a parameter is created with a , the value is converted to meters if the type is not supported. For example if provided is international miles, the value is converted to meters since only the US Survey Miles unit is supported in geoprocessing. After is created, the used linear unit type can be checked from . When is created with a supported , then no conversion takes place and the value is sent to the service using the defined type. When is returned as an output parameter, you can get using method.
