member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geoprocessing.GeoprocessingParameters
member.summary: GeoprocessingParameters contains input parameters that are sent to the target geoprocessing task.
member.remarks: GeoprocessingParameters contains both input parameters and environment variables that are used in the target geoprocessing task. At a minimum all parameters that are set as required on the published task must to be provided to run the task successfully. Parameters that have default values can be overridden by providing a new value in the inputs dictionary. Optional parameters can be provided the same way. also contains environment variables for the geoprocessing task such as and .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geoprocessing.GeoprocessingParameters.#ctor(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geoprocessing.GeoprocessingExecutionType)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the given execution type.
member.param: A indicating the tasks execution type.
member.remarks: Note that the execution type needs to match the expected execution type of the geoprocessing task being run as indicated by the service's metadata. When are created is used to define how the targeted geoprocessing task is being run on the service. If the geoprocessing service is set to use synchronous Execute endpoint then needs to be used. If the geoprocessing service is set use asynchronous Submit endpoint then needs to be used. The execution type defines how the geoprocessing task is run on the service and in both cases ArcGIS Runtime uses asynchronous model to communicate with the service.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geoprocessing.GeoprocessingParameters.ExecutionType
member.summary: Gets the geoprocessing parameters' object execution type.

member.summary: Gets or sets the output spatial reference.
member.remarks: This property is the spatial reference that will be used for any output geometries. If neither output spatial reference nor process spatial reference are supplied, the input geometries' spatial reference will be used. If output spatial reference is not set but process spatial reference is, this will be used instead.

member.summary: Gets or sets the process spatial reference.
member.remarks: This property is the spatial reference that will be used to perform any geometry operations. This spatial reference will also be used for output geometries if output spatial reference is not set. If is set but is not, outputs are returned in the spatial reference which is defined in .

member.summary: GeoprocessingResult contains output parameters returned from the service.

remarks.para: Single output parameters can be found from dictionary where the key of the item is the name of the output parameters and the value is the returned .

If used geoprocessing service is configured to use result map server then might be populated. is a dynamic map service that accompanies a geoprocessing service to visualize the geoprocessing results. Each geodataset output parameter of a geoprocessing task will be added as a layer to a map service. The visualization of the layer is determined by the symbology on geoprocessing service. are mainly used to visualize or but in some cases is used as a reference to a geodataset which is added to the result map service as a layer.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geoprocessing.GeoprocessingTask
member.summary: GeoprocessingTask is used to run geoprocessing task that is published as a web service.
member.remarks:
GeoprocessingTask is the main component when running geoprocessing tasks. The task might be published through ArcGIS Server or Local server (on the platforms that supports it) using either synchronous execute or asynchronous submit execution type. When is created it is important to match to the value on the service.
Create using full URL to the target geoprocessing task endpoint.
Create providing corresponding
Create needed s and add them to where key is the name of the parameter and value is created parameter.
Set environmental variables on if needed.
Create with .
Start listening for status and message changes using event.
Run targeted geoprocessing tool using which returns when finished.
Handle returned values accordingly. Access output parameters through dictionary where key is the name of the output parameter and the value is returned parameter. If used geoprocessing service is configured to use result map service might be created for you.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1
member.summary: A job is a long-running asynchronous operation performed by an ArcGIS Server asynchronous service operation.

remarks.para: This is the base class for a range of long-running jobs classes, such as , , , , and . You can create a job instance from its associated task using a set of parameters. For example, to create a , call and provide a set of . A newly instantiated job has a status of .

Call to initiate the job on the server. If the server accepts the job, it assigns a unique job id (available from the ) and executes the job. At this point, the job status is . The running job periodically polls the server to get messages and progress of the job. While the job is in progress, you can listen for changes to the , display its collection of , and report the to show the percentage of the job that has been completed.

When the job is done, you can examine the to determine if it was successful. If the job failed (), information about the failure is available in the . If the job succeeds (), its specific type of result is returned. For example, a successful returns a , a successful returns a , and so on.

If necessary, you can pause a job and re-start it using and , respectively. Note, you should always cancel jobs that are no longer needed by calling . This sends a cancel request to the server and helps reduce unnecessary server load.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1.Messages
member.summary: Gets the job's status messages
member.remarks: When a new messages is added the event is fired.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1.Error
member.summary: Gets the error if one occurred during execution of the job. If no error has occurred, then null is returned.
member.remarks: The error is available when the is .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1.GetResultAsync
member.summary: Gets the job's result
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous get result operation. The value of the task result is an object of type T.

remarks.para: For a successfully completed job, the job's result returns an collection of , typically the collection is empty. If individual edits failed then the result collection provides these errors grouped by each table using instances which in turn contain each edit error.
Only errors are reported, the feature edit results do not contain information about successful adds, deletes or updates.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1.ServerJobId
member.summary: Gets the server job ID of the job

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1.Status
member.summary: Gets the status of the job.
member.remarks: When status changes the event is fired.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1.Progress
member.summary: Gets the current progress of the job as a percentage toward completion
member.remarks: When the progress value changes the event is fired.
member.value: The job's current completion percentage. Zero indicates that the job has not been started, and 100 indicates completion.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1.CancelAsync
member.summary: Cancels this job and waits for any asynchronous server-side operations to be canceled.

remarks.para: The job is canceled and will result in a status after all cancellation tasks have completed. For jobs running on a server, a cancel request is sent for the associated . You should always cancel unneeded jobs (for example when exiting your app) to avoid placing unnecessary load on the server. Examples of server- side jobs include:
In addition, the is composed of several server-side jobs, depending on the types of layers in your . Canceling this high-level job will also send a cancel request to the underlying server jobs.
Upon calling this method, the is immediately set to .

member.returns: The task returns true if the job was successfully canceled, false otherwise. The task always completes without an error. Returns false if server jobs were requested to cancel but the response was not successful. Also returns false if the job has reached or status.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1.Pause
member.summary: Pauses execution of the job
member.returns: false if already paused or cannot be paused; otherwise, true.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1.Start
member.summary: Starts the job if the job is not started or paused.
member.returns: A value indicating whether the job was successfully started. false is returned if the job is already running, has completed, of if there's an error.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1.ToJson
member.summary: Converts the job into a JSON representation
member.returns: A JSON string representation of a job.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Job`1.CheckStatusAsync
member.summary: Initiates a request to check the status of a job while executing on a server
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous status check operation. The value of the task result is a bool indicating whether the check was successful.
member.remarks: The task completes after the status has been checked. If the job is polling the server for status and is in a time gap, then calling this method will ensure a request is sent right away. The result can be false if the job is not checking server status - for example, if the job status is paused or uploading.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.JobMessageSeverity.Info
member.summary: An informative message is generated during the job's execution, such as a job uploading data, job progressing on a server or job results. This message type never indicates a problem.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.JobMessageSeverity.Warning
member.summary: A warning message is generated when a job experiences a situation that may cause a problem during its execution or when the result may not be what you expect. For example, when the job has failed to take a layer offline from an online map. Generally the job will run to successful completion even if there are one or more warnings.

member.summary: An error message indicates a critical event that caused the job to fail. The error instance is also available from the job's property.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ClosestFacilityParameters.StartTimeUsage
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that determines whether the is used as the time of departing from the facility or incident, or time of arriving at the facility or incident.
member.remarks: Default value is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ClosestFacilityParameters.TravelDirection
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that specifies the between facilities and incidents.
member.remarks: The direction of travel may influence the facilities found if the network contains one-way streets or impedances based on the direction of travel. For instance, it might take 10 minutes to drive from a particular incident to a particular facility, but the journey might take 15 minutes traveling in the other direction, from the facility to the incident, because of one-way streets or different traffic conditions. The default value is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ClosestFacilityParameters.TravelMode
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that specifies the to use when computing the routes.
member.remarks: A travel mode represents a collection of network settings, such as travel restrictions and U-turn policies, that determine how a pedestrian, car, truck, or other medium of transportation moves through the network.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ClosestFacilityTask
member.summary: Class that represents a task that can find the closest facilities around any location (incidents) on a network.
member.remarks: The closest facility task uses a transportation network to measure the cost of traveling between incidents and facilities and determines which are nearest to one other. Finding the closest hospital to an accident, the closest police cars to a crime scene, and the closest store to a customer's address are all examples of closest facility problems. When finding closest facilities, you can specify how many to find and whether the direction of travel is toward or away from them. Once you've found the closest facilities, you can display the best route to or from them, return the travel cost for each route, and display directions to each facility. Additionally, you can specify an impedance cutoff beyond which the task should not search for a facility. For instance, you can set up a closest facility problem to search for hospitals within 15 minutes drive time of the site of an accident. Any hospitals that take longer than 15 minutes to reach will not be included in the results. The task can be created for an ArcGIS Network Analyst service hosted in the cloud on ArcGIS Online or on-premises with ArcGIS Server.

member.summary: Gets the maximum distance that Network Analyst searches when locating or relocating a point onto the network.
member.remarks: Distance is measured in meters. The search looks for suitable edges or junctions and snaps the point to the nearest one. If a suitable location isn't found within the maximum locating distance, the object is marked as unlocated. Corresponds to "maxSnapTolerance" attribute in the REST API.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.CostAttribute
member.summary: Represents an attribute used to measure and model impedance on the network, such as travel time (transit time on a street).

remarks.para: Network analysis often involves the minimization of a cost (also known as impedance) during the calculation of a path (also known as finding the best route). Common examples include finding the fastest route (minimizing travel time) or the shortest route (minimizing distance).
Cost attributes are a property of the underlying network dataset.

These attributes are apportionable along an edge; that is, they are divided proportionately along the length of an edge. For example, if travel time is modeled as a cost attribute, traversing half an edge will take half the time as does traversing the whole edge: if the travel time to traverse the edge is 3 minutes, it takes 1.5 minutes to traverse half the edge.

member.summary: Gets the cost attribute's parameters.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.DirectionManeuver.FromLevel
member.summary: Gets the logical level at the start of the maneuver.

remarks.para: For maneuvers that go up or down levels, such as going from floor 1 to floor 2 in a building's stairwell, the value signifies the start level of the maneuver. For example, to go from floor 1 to floor 2, the would be 1 and the would be 2. If these values are in the source data, the directions engine can give a descriptive instruction for the maneuver, such as "go up the stairs." If the values are not provided, a -1 value is used, signifying that no levels are defined for maneuvers.

The and properties are defined to provide a way to filter directions based on logical level division. For example, a 3D routing application might render a 2D map with route lines filtered by vertical level instead of displaying overlapping lines on different levels.

The and properties are populated from source data and not calculated by the directions engine. If the source data does not contain LevelFrom and LevelTo fields, the properties will be populated with -1 values

remarks.para: For maneuvers that go up or down levels, such as going from floor 1 to floor 2 in a building's stairwell, the value signifies the ending level of the maneuver. For example, to go from floor 1 to floor 2, the would be 1 and the would be 2. If these values are in the source data, the directions engine can give a descriptive instruction for the maneuver, such as "go up the stairs." If the values are not provided, a -1 value is used, signifying that no levels are defined for maneuvers.

The and properties are defined to provide a way to filter directions based on logical level division. For example, a 3D routing application might render a 2D map with route lines filtered by vertical level instead of displaying overlapping lines on different levels.

The and properties are populated from source data and not calculated by the directions engine. If the source data does not contain LevelFrom and LevelTo fields, the properties will be populated with -1 values

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.PointBarrier
member.summary: A point barrier object used to restrict route solving.
member.remarks: A class that represents a point location to be avoided (or penalized) by a route. PointBarrier may represent a restricted location to be avoided by a route (e.g., a car accident) or a location at which some additional delay occurs if visited along a route (e.g., a railroad crossing).

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.PolygonBarrier
member.summary: A polygon barrier object is used to restrict route solving.
member.remarks: A class that represents a polygon feature to be avoided (or penalized) by a route. Polygon_barrier may represent a regional area of interest within the network to be avoided (i.e., not entered) by a route (e.g., a flood zone) or to penalize/delay a route within any portions of the area traversed by the route (e.g., a busy downtown area or a bad neighborhood).

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.PolylineBarrier
member.summary: A polyline barrier is used to restrict route solving.
member.remarks: A class that represents a linear feature to be avoided (or penalized) by a route. Polyline_barrier may represent a linear feature (e.g., a subpath in the network) to be avoided (i.e., not crossed or followed) by a route or to penalize/delay a route along any portions of the feature traversed by the route (e.g., a stretch of interstate with temporarily reduced speed limits due to local construction along an adjacent lane).

member.summary: Gets start time in UTC.
member.remarks: To calculate the departure time for the timezone of the start location, add the . Note that this might be a different timezone than if the start location is in a different timezone than the device. You can also use to get the arrival time in the time zone of the device.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.Route.EndTime
member.summary: Gets end time in UTC.
member.remarks: To calculate the arrival time for the timezone of the destination, add the . Note that this might be a different timezone than if the location of the destination is in a different timezone than the device. You can also use to get the end time in the time zone of the device.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.Route.EndTimeShift
member.summary: Gets the time-zone shift for the end time.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.RouteShapeType
member.summary: Indicates the route shape type. This parameter is applicable only if is set to true.

remarks.para: No matter which value you choose for the RouteShapeType parameter, the best route is always determined by minimizing the travel time or the travel distance, never using the Euclidean distance (or as the crow flies distance) between the stops. This means that only the returned route shapes are different, not the underlying streets that are searched when finding the route.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.RouteShapeType.None
member.summary: Do not return any shapes for the routes. This value can be useful in cases where you are only interested in determining the total travel time or travel distance of the route. For example, if your application has already calculated the route and after some time your application needs to only calculate the expected time of arrival (ETA) to the destination, you can set the parameter to true and the RouteShapeType parameter to None.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.RouteShapeType.StraightLine
member.summary: Return a straight line between the stops.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.RouteShapeType.TrueShapeWithMeasures
member.summary: Return the exact shape of the resulting route that is based on the underlying streets and include route measurements that keep track of the cumulative travel time or travel distance along the route relative to the first stop. When this value is chosen for the RouteShapeType parameter, each point that makes up the route shape will include an M value along with X and Y values. The M value, also known as a measure value, indicates the accumulated travel time or travel distance at that point along the route. The M values can be used to determine how far you have traveled from the start of the route or the remaining distance or time left to reach the destination. The M values store travel time if the is set to TravelTime and store the travel distance if the is set to Kilometers or Miles.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.RouteTask.CreateParametersAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Data.FeatureCollection)
member.summary: Imports route parameters from feature collection and then adjusts imported parameters to current Route task.
member.remarks: Travel mode would be null if it could not be created from the route layer settings for the route task's network. In case if travel mode is null then client can override it by another travel mode (for example obtained from ). Accumulate attributes from Route Info table going to be applied only if they exist in transportation network restriction and cost attributes.
member.param: The feature collection object that contains stops, route info, and barriers tables. Stops table is required.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous create default parameters operation. The value of the task result is a object.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.RouteTaskInfo.MaxLocatingDistance
member.summary: Gets the maximum distance that Network Analyst searches when locating or relocating a point onto the network.

remarks.para: Distance is measured in meters.
The search looks for suitable edges or junctions and snaps the point to the nearest one.
If a suitable location isn't found within the maximum locating distance, the object is marked as unlocated.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaFacility
member.summary: A class that describes service area facility.
member.remarks: Facilities represent locations from which service areas need to be calculated. For example, a fire station can be set as a facility to find which areas it can serve within a 10 minute service area. A hospital can be set as the facility to find what proportion of the population is within a 2 mile driving distance.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaFacility.#ctor(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Geometry.MapPoint)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Defines

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaFacility.CurbApproach
member.summary: Gets or sets the curb approach.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaFacility.DistanceToNetworkLocation
member.summary: Gets distance to network location in meters.
member.remarks: The distance to network location in meters can be populated by service area task.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaFacility.Geometry
member.summary: Gets service area facility's location using geographical point.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaFacility.ImpedanceCutoffs
member.summary: Gets the impedance cutoffs value.
member.remarks: Cutoffs specify the quantity of the impedance to apply. They constrain the extent of the service area to be calculated. For example, if you apply breaks of 5, 10, and 15 when the impedance is set to Time, the service area will include those streets that can be reached within 5, 10, and 15 minutes. The value specified in the impedance cutoffs property overrides the Default Cutoffs analysis setting. If no value for the impedance cutoffs property is specified, service area are generated for the facility based on the Default Cutoffs setting.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaFacility.LocationStatus
member.summary: Gets location status.
member.remarks: Facility location's status can be populated by service area task.

member.summary: Gets or sets the service area facility's name.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaFacility.NetworkLocation
member.summary: Gets or sets the network location.
member.value: The service area facility's location on the network that can be populated by service area task.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaFacility.FacilityId
member.summary: Gets or sets the facility ID.
member.remarks: This is a caller-supplied foreign key that can be used to associate output facilities with input facilities.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaFacility.CurrentBearing
member.summary: Gets or sets the current bearing in degrees.
member.remarks: Current bearing in degrees, measured clockwise from true north. Typical values are 0 to 360 or , negative values will be subtracted from 360 (e.g. -15 => 345), values greater than 360 will be have 360 subtracted from them (e.g. 385 => 25). For this property to be used the also has to be set.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaFacility.CurrentBearingTolerance
member.summary: Gets or sets the current bearing tolerance in degrees. Valid values are 0 to 180 or .

member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation latency.

member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation speed in meters per second.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaOverlapGeometry
member.summary: Service area overlap geometry.
member.remarks: Specifies the behavior of service-area output from multiple facilities in relation to one another.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaOverlapGeometry.Overlap
member.summary: Creates individual polygons or sets of lines for each facility. The polygons or lines can overlap each other.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaOverlapGeometry.Dissolve
member.summary: Joins the polygons of multiple facilities that have the same cutoff value into a single polygon. Lines will not overlap each other.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaOverlapGeometry.Split
member.summary: Area is assigned to the closest facility so polygons or lines do not overlap each other.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaParameters
member.summary: Describes the service area parameters.
member.remarks: Class holding the parameters of a service area to be solved by . The default parameters for a can be retrieved by calling method.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaParameters.AccumulateAttributeNames
member.summary: Gets accumulate attribute names.
member.remarks: Gets the accumulate attribute names e.g. "Minutes", "Miles", etc. Accumulate attributes are useful when more information other than the default impedance attribute is required.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaParameters.DefaultImpedanceCutoffs
member.summary: Gets the default cutoff value.
member.remarks: Cutoffs specify the quantity of the impedance to apply. They constrain the extent of the service area to be calculated. For example, if you apply breaks of 5, 10, and 15 when the impedance is set to Time, the service area will include those streets that can be reached within 5, 10, and 15 minutes. The cutoffs you specify in the defaultBreaks property are applied to all facilities that don't contain their own cutoffs.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaParameters.GeometryAtCutoff
member.summary: Gets or sets geometry at cutoff.
member.remarks: Specifies the behavior of service area output for a single facility when multiple cutoff values are specified. This parameter does not apply to line output. Default value will be returned on error.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaParameters.GeometryAtOverlap
member.summary: Gets or sets geometry at overlap.
member.remarks: Specifies the behavior of service-area output from multiple facilities in relation to one another.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaParameters.OutputSpatialReference
member.summary: Gets or sets output spatial reference.
member.remarks: All route's geometries will be returned in this spatial reference in the event of a successful solve.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaParameters.PolygonBufferDistance
member.summary: Gets or sets polygon buffer distance.
member.remarks: The polygon buffer refers to the distance from the road the service area polygon should extend when no other reachable roads are nearby, similar to a line buffer size. This is useful if the network is very sparse and you don't want the service area to cover large areas where there are no features.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaParameters.PolygonDetail
member.summary: Gets or sets polygon detail.
member.remarks: Specifies the level of detail of the output polygons. If your analysis covers an urban area with a grid-like street network, the difference between generalized and standard polygons will be minimal. However, for mountain and rural roads, the standard and detailed polygons may present significantly more accurate results than generalized polygons.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolygon
member.summary: A class that describes the single service area polygon.
member.remarks: The service area polygon stores the resultant service area polygon, which cover the area of the network that can be reached within the given time, distance, or other travel-cost cutoff.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolygon.FromImpedanceCutoff
member.summary: Gets minimum impedance cutoff value.
member.remarks: A service area polygon's minimal impedance value. For example, a time impedance value of "5 10 15" specifies service areas of 5, 10, and 15 minutes intervals. You can get polygon which covers area from 5 to 10 minutes interval.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolygon.Geometry
member.summary: Gets service area polygon's geometry.
member.remarks: Gets the geometry for the polygon in case if is true.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolygon.ToImpedanceCutoff
member.summary: Gets maximum impedance cutoff value.
member.remarks: A service area polygon's maximal impedance value. For example, a time impedance value of "5 10 15" specifies service areas of 5, 10, and 15 minutes intervals. You can get polygon which covers area from 5 to 10 minutes interval.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolygonCutoffGeometry
member.summary: Service area polygon cutoff geometry.
member.remarks: Specifies the behavior of service area output for a single facility when multiple cutoff values are specified. This parameter does not apply to line output.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolygonCutoffGeometry.Rings
member.summary: Each polygon includes only the area between consecutive cutoff values. It does not include the area between the facility and any smaller cutoffs. For instance, If you create 5- and 10-minute service areas, then the 5-minute service area polygon will include the area reachable in 0-5 minutes, and the 10-minute service area polygon will include the area reachable between 5 and 10 minutes.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolygonCutoffGeometry.Disks
member.summary: Each polygon includes the area reachable from the facility up to the cutoff value, including the area reachable within smaller cutoff values. For instance, If you create 5- and 10-minute service areas, then the 10-minute service area polygon will include the area under the 5-minute service area polygon.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolygonDetail
member.summary: Service area polygon detail.
member.remarks: Specifies the level of detail of the output polygons. If your analysis covers an urban area with a grid-like street network, the difference between generalized and standard polygons will be minimal. However, for mountain and rural roads, the standard and detailed polygons may present significantly more accurate results than generalized polygons.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolygonDetail.Generalized
member.summary: Creates generalized polygons using the hierarchy present in the network data source in order to produce results quickly.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolygonDetail.Standard
member.summary: Creates polygons with a standard level of detail. This is the default.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolygonDetail.High
member.summary: Creates polygons with a higher level of detail for applications in which very precise results are important.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaPolyline
member.summary: A class that describes the single service area polyline.
member.remarks: The service area polyline stores the resultant service area polyline, which the represents the network edges that can be reached within the given impedance.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTask
member.summary: Instances of this class represent tasks that can compute service areas.
member.remarks: A Service Area task allows you to calculate areas that can be serviced (reached) from a given location. A service area is a region that encompasses all accessible streets, that is, streets that lie within a specified impedance. For instance, the 10-minute service area for a facility includes all the streets that can be reached within 10 minutes from that facility.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.DefaultImpedanceCutoffs
member.summary: Gets default impedance cutoffs.
member.remarks: Cutoffs specify the quantity of the impedance to apply. They constrain the extent of the service area to be calculated. For example, if you apply breaks of 5, 10, and 15 when the impedance is set to Time, the service area will include those streets that can be reached within 5, 10, and 15 minutes.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.DefaultTravelModeName
member.summary: Gets default travel mode's name.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.GeometryAtCutoff
member.summary: Gets geometry at cutoff.
member.remarks: Specifies the behavior of service area output for a single facility when multiple cutoff values are specified. This parameter does not apply to line output. Default value will be returned on error.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.GeometryAtOverlap
member.summary: Gets geometry at overlap.
member.remarks: Specifies the behavior of service-area output from multiple facilities in relation to one another.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.NetworkName
member.summary: Gets network name.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.OutputSpatialReference
member.summary: Gets output spatial reference.
member.remarks: The spatial reference that will be used for output geometry during service area solving.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.PolygonBufferDistance
member.summary: Gets polygon buffer distance.
member.remarks: The polygon buffer refers to the distance from the road the service area polygon should extend when no other reachable roads are nearby, similar to a line buffer size. This is useful if the network is very sparse and you don't want the service area to cover large areas where there are no features.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.PolygonDetail
member.summary: Gets polygon detail.
member.remarks: Specifies the level of detail of the output polygons. If your analysis covers an urban area with a grid-like street network, the difference between generalized and standard polygons will be minimal. However, for mountain and rural roads, the standard and detailed polygons may present significantly more accurate results than generalized polygons.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.RestrictionAttributes
member.summary: Gets the restriction attributes.
member.remarks: Gets the names of set restriction attributes e.g. "Oneway". Key in the result dictionary is name of restriction attribute.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.ReturnPolygons
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether to return polygons.
member.remarks: Specifies the type of output to be generated. Service area output will contain polygon features encompassing reachable area(s).

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.ReturnPolylines
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether to return polylines.
member.remarks: Specifies the type of output to be generated. Service area output will contain line features representing the roads reachable before the cutoffs are exceeded.

member.summary: Gets start time.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.ServiceAreaTaskInfo.MaxLocatingDistance
member.summary: Gets the maximum distance that Network Analyst searches when locating or relocating a point onto the network.
member.remarks: Distance is measured in meters. The search looks for suitable edges or junctions and snaps the point to the nearest one. If a suitable location isn't found within the maximum locating distance, the object is marked as unlocated. Corresponds to "maxSnapTolerance" attribute in the REST API.

member.summary: A class that represents a location to be visited along a route.
member.remarks: The Stop class contains location inputs for the Route_task. The Route_task will create a route between two or more Stops. A Stop object is not an arbitrary bag of properties, but contains only the properties needed for routing (for example, a Stop object does not have address information).

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.TransportationNetworkDataset
member.summary: A transportation network dataset of a map or scene stored in a mobile map or scene package.

remarks.para: A transportation network, such as a street, pedestrian, or railroad network, models networks that typically permit travel in both directions. Map and scene authors can use ArcGIS Pro to create a mobile map or scene package that includes transportation networks. For more information, see Create a mobile map package or Author an mobile scene package.

If you create a map or scene from one of these packages, you can obtain its from the read-only collection of . To perform a route, closest facility, or service area network analysis on the transportation network, you construct the respective , , or , using this .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.UTurnPolicy
member.summary: Indicates the u-turn policy. Use this to restrict or permit the route from making U-turns at junctions.
member.remarks: In order to understand the parameter values, keep in mind that a junction is a point where only two streets intersect each other. If three or more streets intersect at a point, it is called as an intersection. The end of a cul-de-sac is deemed to be a dead-end.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.UTurnPolicy.NotAllowed
member.summary: U-turns are prohibited at all junctions, intersections and dead-ends. Note that even when this parameter value is chosen, the route can still make U-turns at stops. If you wish to prohibit U-turns even at the stops, you can set the appropriate value for the CurbApproach attribute of the stops.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.UTurnPolicy.AllowedAtDeadEnds
member.summary: U-turns are prohibited at all junctions and intersections and are permitted only at dead ends.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.UTurnPolicy.AllowedAtIntersections
member.summary: Allow backtracking at intersections.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.NetworkAnalysis.UTurnPolicy.AllowedAtDeadEndsAndIntersections
member.summary: Allow backtracking at dead ends and intersections.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.DownloadPreplannedOfflineMapJob
member.summary: A job to download the map area from an online map as part of the preplanned offline workflow.

remarks.para: This job is part of the ahead-of-time offline workflow. Use this job to download the offline map to your device so that you can work offline without a network connection. Note that offline map areas may also be referred to as preplanned map areas.
To make a , use the as follows:

list.item: Construct and load the with a web map that is enabled for offline use. For more information about enabling web maps for offline use see Take web maps offline.
If necessary, check the web map's offline capabilities by using .
Select an offline map area from the collection of offline map areas returned by .
Obtain the default for the offline map area by calling .
Adjust any parameters, as required.
Use the to create a by calling . You must specify a path to a folder where the map content will be stored on the device.

remarks.para: Start the and monitor progress. Upon completion, the job's provides the offline map along with the details of any errors incurred during the download process.
See the class for full information on how to work with jobs.

member.remarks: The directory path is the path that was supplied as a parameter to . The job will fail if the last directory in the path cannot be created or is not empty.

member.summary: Gets the used to create this job.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.DownloadPreplannedOfflineMapParameters
member.summary: The parameters that specify how an offline map area is downloaded from an online map using a .

remarks.para: The online map must be a web map either on ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Enterprise. Note that the API refers to an offline map area as a .

To generate default parameters that match the online map's offline settings, call . You can then modify the parameters with any desired changes. Alternatively, you can generate an empty set of parameters using and set the parameters individually. To create a , pass the parameters to the method and provide a path to a folder where the map content is stored on the device.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the given .
member.param: The preplanned map area representing the area to be downloaded for offline use

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.DownloadPreplannedOfflineMapParameters.ContinueOnErrors
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the should continue running in the event of failure to take a layer offline.
member.remarks: If this property is true, failure to take a layer or table offline will not fail the job, the failure will be exposed in the job result. If this property is false, failure to take a layer or table offline will fail the job and no more layers or tables will be taken offline. The layer or table's error will be available as the job's error. The default value is true.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.DownloadPreplannedOfflineMapParameters.IncludeBasemap
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not a basemap will be included in the offline map.

remarks.para: If you do not want a basemap in the offline map then set this property to false.
After loading the offline map, your application can programmatically insert a basemap into the map. Note that, a programmatically inserted basemap will not be persisted in the map.
If you want a basemap in the offline map then set this property to true. You can choose to either:

list.item: Download and use the basemap that is part of the . This is the default.
Use the local basemap on the device by setting the .

remarks.para: The default value is true.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.DownloadPreplannedOfflineMapParameters.PreplannedMapArea
member.summary: Gets or sets the that will be downloaded for offline use.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.DownloadPreplannedOfflineMapParameters.ReferenceBasemapDirectory
member.summary: Gets or sets the path to a directory on the device where the local basemap file is located.

remarks.para: Set this property to use a basemap which is already on the device (rather than downloading it). The directory should only be set when is defined.
This property supports any directory specified as either:

list.item: An absolute path.
A path relative to the parent directory of the generated mobile map package (see downloadPath for .

remarks.para: If the directory does not exist, or does not contain the specified basemap, the will fail.
Note that, the mobile map package will store either a relative or absolute path to the reference basemap file so that it can be opened in the future.

A relative path typically begins with "." to denote the parent directory of the resulting mobile map package or ".." to move up one directory. The parent directory of the mobile map package will be set when calling .

For example, to download a mobile map package to "[my root path]/my_offline_maps/my_mobile_map_package", which uses a reference basemap in "[my root path]/other_maps/my_basemap.tpk", you would set the relative path as follows:

list.item: to "[my root path]/my_offline_maps/my_mobile_map_package"
to "../other_maps"
to "my_basemap.tpk"

remarks.para: Note that, this property is ignored if is false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.DownloadPreplannedOfflineMapParameters.ReferenceBasemapFilename
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of a local basemap file on the device which can be used rather than downloading an online basemap.

remarks.para: This property can be read directly from settings applied by the author of the online webmap ( ) or set by user code to a file known to be on the device.
The local basemap filename must end with .tpk, .tpkx, or .vtpk since these are the supported file formats.
To use the specified local basemap rather than downloading, the property must also be set.
If the directory does not exist, or does not contain the specified basemap, the will fail.
Note that this property is ignored if is false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.DownloadPreplannedOfflineMapParameters.UpdateMode
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that describes how the offline map will obtain feature updates from the online service.

remarks.para: The preplanned workflow offers three modes for updating the feature data in your offline map:

list.item: Sync feature changes directly with online feature services.
Download a set of scheduled update packages (geodatabase delta files that are cached on the server) from the online map area.
Download scheduled updates from the online map area and upload new features to the feature service.

remarks.para: To register your mobile geodatabases for syncing with online feature services, set this property to . Syncing allows you to upload as well as download changes, so this approach should be used when you need full control to edit your data. As you sync changes directly with the back-end services, you will receive the most up-to-date feature data available. This is the default mode if you construct the .

To download a set of cached updates (delta files) from the online map area, set this property to . With this approach, updates are generated whenever the online map area is refreshed - for example according to its update schedule. Updates are prepared once and cached for download by all users of the offline map area. Sharing a single set of cached updates, rather than performing individual sync operations, reduces the load on the back-end services - this makes this approach scalable for large deployments. As updates are only downloaded, this approach can only be used with read-only workflows. The updates also reflect a snapshot of the feature data at the time the online map area was refreshed - rather than the most up-to-date information. The web map author must configure the online map to store cached updates for this approach.

To use a hybrid approach where you download scheduled updates and upload new features, set this property to . This approach combines the scalability of downloading scheduled updates with the ability to collect and share data in the field. Note that this approach is strictly add only, meaning that you cannot delete or edit features once they have been uploaded to the feature service.
If you use a of or , with a web map that does not support scheduled updates, the resultant will fail with an error.

member.value: Describes how the offline map will obtain feature updates from the online service.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.EsriVectorTilesDownloadOption
member.summary: Enumerates options for downloading an Esri vector tile basemap service.

remarks.para: When taking an Esri vector tiled basemap service offline (e.g. to create a on your device), you can choose from different versions of the service depending on your use case.

Primary considerations are the download speed and the size of the resulting .vtpk file. Among other things, file size is influenced by the amount of fonts downloaded from the service. Esri vector tiled basemap layers make use of fonts as part of their style resources - see the Vector Tile Style documentation. When you take a vector tiled layer offline, these fonts are downloaded as part of the .

Esri services also offer an alternative version with a reduced font package size which will reduce the download size of a vtpk by around 80 Mb. You can view a set of reduced font vector tile styles which use the reduced font services here. If you are creating a web map that is only intended for taking maps offline and not for online-viewing, these "for export" versions of the services are recommended.
This enumeration describes options for controlling which set of fonts are downloaded. Note that these options only apply to Esri vector tile basemap services.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.EsriVectorTilesDownloadOption.UseOriginalService
member.summary: The complete set of vector tile resources for the original service, including the full set of fonts, will be downloaded.
member.remarks: Downloading all font resources increases the download size of a .vtpk. However, this option ensures that the offline vector tile layer displays the same as the original online service.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.EsriVectorTilesDownloadOption.UseReducedFontsService
member.summary: An alternative service that uses a reduced set of font resources, supporting a limited set of language characters, will be downloaded.

remarks.para: Downloading the smaller set of font resources reduces the download size of a .vtpk by around 80 Mb. This omits the large Arial Unicode font, that is used to support many characters for the full world basemap. This makes this option unsuitable for some locales or when your offline vector tiled layer includes certain character sets. For example, this option will not display Chinese, Japanese, Thai, Korean or Georgian characters. You should confirm that this option is suitable for your specific use-case.
You can view a set of vector tile styles which use the reduced font services here. This option is not recommended for Esri basemap services that do not have an equivalent "for export" version.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.EstimateTileCacheSizeJob
member.summary: A job that estimates the size of a tile cache (.tpk or .tpkx) that can be exported from a map or image service.

remarks.para: Use the to get the approximate size of a tile cache for a specific set of . Note that time required to create a tile cache varies depending on the extent requested, the number of levels requested, the network connection speed, and so on. When planning a large offline data capture event, generate a smaller tile cache to get an idea of how long caching will take.
To make an , use the as follows:

list.item: Confirm that the map or image service can export tiles by checking for the compact version 2 format (.tpkx) and for the legacy format (.tpk).
Construct and load the with the map or image service.
Create a to define the area of interest that you wish to take offline.
Obtain the default by calling with the area of interest and a minimum and maximum scale for the tile cache.
Use the to create an by calling .

remarks.para: Start the and monitor progress. Upon completion, the job's provides an estimate of the number and size of files in the tile cache.
See the class for full information on how to work with jobs.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.ExportTileCacheParameters
member.summary: The parameters that specify how a tile cache (.tpkx) is exported from an ArcGIS map or image service using an .

remarks.para: To generate parameters populated with values from the underlying service, call . You can then modify these parameters with any desired changes. Alternatively, you can generate an empty set of parameters using and set the parameters individually.

Before you export the tiles you should ensure that your device has sufficient space for the tile cache. Generate an by passing the parameters to . Run this job to get an estimate of the tile cache size found in the .
To export the tile cache, create an , pass the parameters to the method, and provide a path where the .tpkx file can be downloaded to on the device.
You can also use the to provide more fine-grained control over the tiles that are exported when an offline map is generated. For more information, see the class.

member.summary: Gets or sets the area of interest geometry.
member.remarks: The supported geometry types for the area of interest are and . The area of interest must have a spatial reference. Where a is supplied, tiles will be filtered according to the polygon geometry, which can help reduce the size of the resulting tile package. Note that the filtered set of tiles may vary, depending on the underlying service. The area of interest may be set to null to specify retrieval of the full tile cache extent.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.ExportTileCacheParameters.CompressionQuality
member.summary: Gets or sets the compression quality. If the compression quality is not set, is returned.

remarks.para: Compression quality is a value from 0 to 100 or . A lower number reduces the size of the exported tile package, but reduces the quality. This applies to JPEG format tiles. If the value is NaN the service will use its default compression quality. The default compression value for the service is accessible on the services metadata under .
The default value for this property is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.ExportTileCacheParameters.LevelIds
member.summary: Gets a mutable list of levels of detail indices to be included in the exported .
member.remarks: You must include at least one Level ID. The values should correspond to Level IDs in the service's tiling scheme. You can specify consecutive levels (for example 1,2,3,4,5) or skip some levels (for example for the ranges 1-3 and 7-9 you would specify 1,2,3,7,8,9).

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.ExportTileCacheTask
member.summary: A task used to export a tile cache (.tpk or .tpkx). Use this in conjunction with a map or image service to generate and download tile packages.

remarks.para: To confirm whether a map or image service supports exporting tiles, check:

list.item: property for the legacy compact format (.tpk).
property for the compact version 2 format (.tpkx).

remarks.para: See for information on creating a layer from a local tile cache.

When using Esri provided image basemaps (such as the World Imagery basemap, ) an alternative service which supports exporting tiles may be used instead. For example, the World Imagery Service () will be exported using a corresponding export-enabled service ().

Similarly, when using elevation data from Esri (such as Terrain 3D), an alternative service that supports exporting tiles may be used instead. For example, the (Terrain 3D) will be exported using a corresponding (export-enabled service). The resulting tile cache can then be used to create an offline in a scene.

Note that these export-enabled services are not intended for use as an online basemap or elevation source,and should only be used for exporting tiles for offline use. The export enabled services require authentication to export tiles.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.ExportTileCacheTask.CreateDefaultExportTileCacheParametersAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Geometry.Geometry,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Creates and returns new instance.

remarks.para: This function is asynchronous because it makes use of the service metadata to build a object. Calling the function will trigger load of the , unless it's already loaded.

The supported geometry types for the area of interest are and . The area of interest must have a spatial reference. Where a is supplied, tiles will be filtered according to the polygon geometry, which can help reduce the size of the resulting tile package. Note that the filtered set of tiles may vary, depending on the underlying service.
The value of must be larger than the value of , unless they are 0.
A value of 0 will result in this method choosing the service's smallest level number, typically level 0.

Similarly, a of 0 will result in the service's largest level number being used, representing the closest in view being visible when taken offline. If is between the scales of tile levels the previous smallest level is used.

If is between tile levels the next level is taken to ensure it is displayed. For example a simple service has 4 levels: level 0 scale 2000000: level 1 scale 1000000; level 2 scale 500000; level 3 scale 250000.
A of 0 and of 0 selects all levels 0,1,2,3.
A of 750000 (between levels 1 and 2) and a of 25000 (at level 3) will select levels 1,2,3.
A of 0 and a 750000 (between 1 and 2) will select levels 0,1,2.
A of 1000000 and a of 0 will select all levels from 1 onwards 1,2,3.
Be careful when combining a large extent or a wide range of scales, this can result in the export failing due to exceeding the services maximum export tile count.

member.param: A geometry specifying the area to be exported.
The map scale '1:minScale' which determines how far out to take offline. Can be 0 for the furthest out.
The map scale '1:maxScale' which determines how far in to take offline. Can be 0 for the closest in the service allows.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous create export tile cache parameters operation. The value of the task result is the .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.ExportTileCacheTask.ExportTileCache(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.ExportTileCacheParameters,System.String)
member.summary: Start and return a new export tile cache job.

remarks.para: The resulting job will export tiles from the service, referenced by the property (or its export-enabled alternative), to a local tile cache at the . The format of the tile cache is determined by the file extension supplied in the parameter.
If the download file path ends with ".tpk" the tile cache will use the legacy compact format.
If the download file path ends with ".tpkx" the tile cache will use the current compact version 2 format.
If the service does not support exporting tiles, the job will fail with .

member.param: Export tile cache parameters to be used in exporting.
Downloaded tile cache file path that ends with .tpk or .tpkx, depending on the desired format.
member.returns: A task offline job of type .

Where a is supplied, tiles will be filtered according to the polygon geometry, which can help reduce the size of the resulting tile package. member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.ExportVectorTilesParameters.EsriVectorTilesDownloadOption
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that describes how Esri vector tiled basemap layers will be downloaded.

remarks.para: This property lets you choose how to download Esri vector tiled basemap layers. This property only applies when taking an Esri vector tile basemap service offline.
When taking an Esri vector tiled basemap service offline (e.g. to create a on your device), you can choose from different versions of the service depending on your use case.

Primary considerations are the download speed and the size of the resulting .vtpk file. Among other things, file size is influenced by the number of fonts downloaded from the service. Esri vector tiled basemap layers use fonts as part of their style resources - see vector-tile-style documentation. When you take a vector tiled layer offline, these fonts are downloaded as part of the .

Esri services also offer an alternative version with a reduced font package size. This omits the large Arial Unicode font, which greatly reduces the download size. You can view the set of reduced font vector tile services here.

list.item: If you choose , the full set of resources used by the original online service, including all fonts, will be downloaded. This increases the download size of the but ensures that the offline layer will display the same as the original online data.

If you choose , the smaller set of fonts used by the alternative, for export, version of the service will be downloaded. This reduces the download size of the but is not suitable for all locales or when your offline vector tiled layer includes certain language characters. For example, this option will not display Chinese, Japanese, Thai, Korean or Georgian characters. If an alternative, for export version of the service cannot be found, the original service will be used.

remarks.para: The default value is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.ExportVectorTilesParameters.MaxLevel
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum level of detail to export.
member.remarks: The vector tiles export will always take level 0 and all levels up to and including the value of . The larger the level the more tiles will be included. This must be set to a valid value >= 0.
member.value: The maximum level of detail.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateGeodatabaseParameters.AttachmentSyncDirection
member.summary: Gets or sets the direction for attachments to be synchronized.

remarks.para: This parameter is available when the returns true. This parameter defines how attachments will be synced and is only applicable if the feature service has attachments.

list.item: Attachment edits can be both uploaded from the client and downloaded from the service when syncing.

Attachment edits can only be uploaded from the client when syncing. This is useful in cases where the data collector does not want to consume space with attachments from the service, but does need to collect new attachments.
Attachment edits are never synced from either the client or the server.

remarks.para:
When is set to true, you can set to either (default) or . In this case, create replica includes attachments from the service.
When is set to false, you can set to either or (default). In this case, the geodatabase does not include attachments from the service.
Attachment sync direction is set during the operation and cannot be overridden during .

member.summary: Gets or sets a value that specifies the generation and synchronization mode of Utility Network data from the service to an offline geodatabase.

remarks.para: Feature layers and tables are always included in the generation and synchronization of the geodatabase. Utility Network System data can be optionally included.
When this property is set to something other than , the schema of all data layers and tables in the feature service is required to support querying associations or tracing offline.

You can filter feature data using by:
setting to
specifying a
setting to true
Excluding data layers or including system layers of the feature service in the will result in an error in the .
Use to check which synchronization mode the feature service supports. If is true, may be used. If is also true, may be used.
Setting this property to something other than allows offline association queries.
Setting this property to also enables offline tracing.

When a replica is created with tracing support from an ArcGIS Enterprise 11.2 or earlier feature service, the must be . As of ArcGIS Enterprise 11.3, feature services configured with edit capabilities will support .
The default value is . Utility Network System data will not be included.
This property can be used at the same time as specifying other additional data types to be included.

member.value: The generation and synchronization mode of Utility Network data from the service to an offline geodatabase.

remarks.para: Feature layers and tables are always included in the generation and synchronization of the geodatabase. Check to see if Contingent Value data is available from the service.
The default value of is true, meaning Contingent Value data is included.
This property can be used at the same time as specifying other additional data types to be included.

member.value: true to include Contingent Value data from the service, when available.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateGeodatabaseParameters.SyncAnnotations
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether Annotation data is to be included from the service, when generating, or downloading to, an offline geodatabase.

remarks.para: Feature layers and tables are always included in the generation and synchronization of the geodatabase. Annotation data can be optionally included when downloading from the service.

Once this data is taken offline to a local geodatabase, any subsequent inserts, edits or deletions will NOT be applied to the server data when re-synchronized. If features linked to the annotation have been changed, the server WILL apply the feature-linked-annotation updates. Another download from the server will replace any offline inserts, edits or deletions of Annotation features.
The default value is true. Annotation data will be included.
can be used to check whether Annotation data is available from the service.
This property can be used at the same time as specifying other additional data types to be included.

member.value: true if Annotation data is to be included; otherwise, false.

can be used to check whether Dimension data is available from the service.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapJob
member.summary: A job that generates and downloads the offline map for a specific area of interest from a web map that is enabled for offline use.

remarks.para: This job is part of the on-demand offline workflow. Use this job to generate and download the offline map to your device so that you can work offline without a network connection.
To make a , use the as follows:

list.item: Construct and load the with a web map that is enabled for offline use. For more information about enabling web maps for offline use see Take web maps offline.
If necessary, check the web map's offline capabilities by using .
Create a to define the area of interest that you wish to take offline.
Obtain the default for the area of interest by calling .
Use the to create a by calling . You must specify a path to a folder where the map content will be stored on the device.

remarks.para: Start the and monitor progress. Upon completion, the job's provides the offline map along with the details of any errors incurred during the generate and download process.
See the class for full information on how to work with jobs.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameterOverrides
member.summary: Class that gives access to the individual parameters objects that will generate the various geodatabase, vector tile and tile cache packages that make up the data of an offline map.

remarks.para: This object allows the user more fine-grained control over the generation of an offline map than the options presented in the standard . For example, the parameters overrides allows the user to adjust specific layers so that they: use a different spatial extent or scale range; use custom attribute or geometry settings; are omitted from the download. An instance of this object can be created from overall map settings in by calling . This object gives access to the individual parameter objects via three dictionaries containing generate geodatabase parameters, export tile cache parameters and export vector tile parameters. These can be accessed using , and respectively. The dictionaries contain the various parameters objects mapped against an . An represents the service that a particular parameters object is intended for. , , or can be used to construct a key given its respective layer, table, or utility network. The returned key can be used to extract the dictionary. The type of dictionary is indicated by the key's property. Note that a given parameters key/value pair may be shared by multiple layers in the map: for example a single could represent the online settings for multiple feature layers that all access the same underlying feature service.

All entries in the dictionaries should correspond to layers, table, or utility network instances in the . If a dictionary entry is removed the corresponding layers, tables, or utility network will be removed from the offline map without raising an error.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameterOverrides.ExportTileCacheParameters
member.summary: Gets the dictionary with keys of and values of .
member.remarks: All entries in the dictionary should correspond to instances in the online . If a dictionary entry is removed the corresponding layers will be removed from the offline map without raising an error.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameterOverrides.ExportVectorTilesParameters
member.summary: Gets the dictionary with keys of and values of .
member.remarks: All entries in the dictionary should correspond to instances in the online . If a dictionary entry is removed the corresponding layers will be removed from the offline map without raising an error.

member.summary: Gets the dictionary with keys of and values of .
member.remarks: All entries in the dictionary should correspond to , , or instances in the online . If a dictionary entry is removed the corresponding layers, tables, or utility network will be removed from the offline map without raising an error.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameterOverrides.OnlineLayers
member.summary: Gets a mutable list of layers that will not be taken offline and will continue to reference online services.

remarks.para: In some situations, you may wish to prevent a layer from being taken offline even though it is supported. For example, by continuing to reference the live online service for a , you can benefit from the most up-to-date information. You can choose to keep layers from the map's operational layers or basemap layers collections online.

To make the offline map continue to reference an online layer add it to the collection. By adding a to this list, you indicate that the layer will not be taken offline but will instead continue to reference the online service. Be aware that the resulting offline map will require a network connection and may also require authentication.
Note that if a is included in this collection it will not be taken offline - you should update the relevant parameters to remove the details pertaining to that layer:

list.item: can be removed to prevent an from being taken offline.
can be removed to prevent an from being taken offline.
can be removed for an entire feature service or updated to remove an individual using .

remarks.para: If a feature service contains many tables in the web map, you can choose to keep specific layers online by adding them to the collection and updating the to remove the specific layers. Any remaining feature layers or stand-alone tables that you wish to take offline can be configured using the for the service in .
When you create a object, this collection will be populated according to the setting in the original parameters.

list.item: If you set to then this collection will be empty.
If you set to then this collection will contain each online-only in the web map (e.g. those which cannot be taken offline).
If you set to then this collection will contain each that the web map author selected to remain online.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameterOverrides.OnlineTables
member.summary: Gets a mutable list of tables that will not be taken offline and will continue to reference online services.

remarks.para: In some situations, you may wish to prevent a non-spatial table from being taken offline even though it is supported. For example, by continuing to reference the live online , you can benefit from the most up-to-date information.

To make the offline map continue to reference an online table, you add it to the collection. By adding a to this list, you indicate that the table will not be taken offline but will instead continue to reference the online source. Be aware that the resulting offline map will require a network connection and may require authentication.
Note that if a is included in this collection it will not be taken offline - you should update the to remove that table.

If a feature service contains many tables in the web map, you can choose to keep specific tables online by adding them to the collection. Any remaining tables which you wish to take offline can be configured using the service's in the .
When you create a object, this collection will be populated according to the setting in the original parameters.

list.item: If you set to then this collection will be empty.
If you set to then this collection will contain each online-only in the web map. For example, tables that cannot be taken offline because they are not sync-enabled.
If you set to , then this collection will contain each that the web map author selected to remain online.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters
member.summary: The parameters that specify how an offline map is generated and downloaded from an online map using a .

remarks.para: The online map must be a web map hosted on ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Enterprise.

To generate parameters with appropriate default values which take into account the online map and the data it contains, call ). You can then modify the parameters with any desired changes. Alternatively, you can generate an empty set of parameters using and set the parameters individually. To create a , pass the parameters to the method and provide a path to a folder where the map content will be stored on the device.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.AreaOfInterest
member.summary: Gets or sets the area of interest for the offline map.
member.remarks: This is used for the extent of offline tile caches, geodatabases and the offline map's property. The supported geometry types for the area of interest are and . The area of interest must have a spatial reference. Where a is supplied, features and tiles will be filtered according to the polygon geometry, which can help reduce the size of the resulting offline map. Note that the filtered set of tiles may vary, depending on the underlying service.
