member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.MaxScale
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum scale for how far in to extract tiles from tiled services. The default value of 0 means extract the levels down to the most detailed.

remarks.para: This is used as input for levels of detail for and .
This is not applicable to offline data in .
A MinScale of 100000 and a MaxScale of 500 will select level IDs between 1:500 and 1:100,000 scale.
A MinScale of 0 and a MaxScale of 500 will select all global level IDs down to 1:500 scale.
A MinScale of 100000 and a MaxScale of 0 will select level IDs from 1:100,000 scale to the most detailed available level.
If is used in conjunction with , this property is superseded for individual layers tile caches.

Note that this property only sets the levels of detail to download for tiled data. The offline basemap layers will use the same max scale as in the online web map (either defined by the service or on the layer itself) to allow zooming in.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.MinScale
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum scale for how far out data will be in tile caches. The 0 default means extract all the available detailed levels to global scales.

remarks.para: This is used as input for levels of detail for .
This is not applicable to offline data in or .
A MinScale of 100000 and a MaxScale of 500 will select level IDs between 1:500 and 1:100,000 scale.
A MinScale of 0 and a MaxScale of 500 will select all global level IDs down to 1:500 scale.
A MinScale of 100000 and a MaxScale of 0 will select level IDs from 1:100,000 scale to the most detailed available level.
If is used in conjunction with , this property is superseded for individual layers tile caches.

Note that this property only sets the levels of detail to download for tiled data. The offline basemap layers will use the same min scale as in the online web map (either defined by the service or on the layer itself).

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.OnlineOnlyServicesOption
member.summary: Gets or sets a value describing how data that requires an online service will be handled when taking a map offline.

remarks.para: Online layers and tables cannot be taken offline in the following situations:

list.item: The layer or table is using a service that cannot be taken offline. For example, or .

The layer or table is using a service that supports offline, such as an ArcGISFeatureLayer, but synchronization has not been enabled on the service ( is set to false). Services utilize synchronization to take layers and tables offline and synchronize changes.

remarks.para: Online services that cannot be taken offline are referred to as online-only services. Online-only services require network connectivity and may involve authentication. Examples of online-only services include:

list.item: Dynamic information, such as changing weather or traffic conditions, that delivers frequent and regular updates to the app.
Large datasets that would be impractical to take offline to a device.

remarks.para: This property allows you to specify how online-only data will be handled when taking the map offline. There are three options:

list.item: means that any data that cannot be taken offline will be omitted from the offline map. Choosing this option means that your offline map will not require a network connection but may not include all of the data from the original web map.

means that data that cannot be taken offline will be accessed by URL in the offline map. Choosing this option means that your offline map will retain all of the information from the original web map but it will require a network connection and may require authentication.
means that layers and tables will be taken offline, remain online or be omitted from the offline map according to the map author's intent as defined in the web map.

remarks.para: The default value is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.ReferenceBasemapDirectory
member.summary: Gets or sets the path to a directory on the device where the local basemap file is located.

remarks.para: Set this property to use a basemap that is already on the device (rather than downloading it). The directory should only be set when the parameters have a defined.

This property supports any directory specified as either:
An absolute path.
A path relative to the parent directory of the generated mobile map package (see downloadPath for ).
If the directory does not exist, or does not contain the specified basemap, the will fail.
Note that, the mobile map package will store either a relative or absolute path to the reference basemap file so that it can be opened in the future.

A relative path typically begins with "." to denote the parent directory of the resulting mobile map package or ".." to move up one directory. The parent directory of the mobile map package will be set when calling .

For example, to generate a mobile map package to "[my root path]/my_offline_maps/my_mobile_map_package", which uses a reference basemap in "[my root path]/other_maps/my_basemap.tpk", you would set the relative path as follows:

list.item: to "[my root path]/my_offline_maps/my_mobile_map_package"
to "../other_maps"
to "my_basemap.tpk"

remarks.para: Note that this property is ignored if is false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.ReferenceBasemapFilename
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of a local basemap file on the device that can be used rather than downloading an online basemap.

remarks.para: If the directory does not exist, or does not contain the specified basemap, the will fail.

The local basemap filename must end with .tpk, .tpkx, or .vtpk since these are the supported file formats. This property can be read directly from settings applied by the author of the online webmap ( ) or set by user code to a file known to be on the device.
This property will be populated from online settings when created with .
To use the specified local basemap rather than downloading, the property must also be set.
Note that this property is ignored if is false.

remarks.para: If is used in conjunction with this property is superseded.
This property is valid when the service resource sync capabilities includes supportsAttachmentsSyncDirection otherwise it is ignored.
This property works in conjunction with and in some cases may have its value overridden by .

The table below shows the valid combinations of values for layer attachment options and attachment sync direction. The notes column explains where the behavior for a given layer may differ from the values set for these properties.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.ContinueOnErrors
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the should continue running in the event of failure to take a layer offline.
member.remarks: If this property is true, failure to take a layer or table offline will not fail the job, the failure will be exposed in the job result. If this property is false, failure to take a layer or table offline will fail the job and no more layers or tables will be taken offline. The layer or table's error will be available as the job's error. The default value is true.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.DestinationTableRowFilter
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether tables will contain all rows or can be filtered to a smaller set of related rows.

remarks.para: When the property is (the default) this can substantially reduce the number of rows in offline tables to only those that are related to rows from other layers or tables. Only tables present in are considered as candidates for filtering. This does not apply when the table:

list.item: Is the origin of a relationship and not a destination.
Is a destination of a relationship from a layer.
Is a table which is not in the map.
Is editable and is true.
Has a and is true.

remarks.para: The relationships of a table are accessible via and the property.
The default value is .
If set to then all rows in tables are returned.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.EsriVectorTilesDownloadOption
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that describes how Esri vector tiled basemap layers will be downloaded.

remarks.para: This property lets you choose how to download Esri vector tiled basemap layers. This property only applies when taking an Esri vector tile basemap service offline.
When taking an Esri vector tiled basemap service offline (e.g. to create a on your device), you can choose from different versions of the service depending on your use case.

Primary considerations are the download speed and the size of the resulting .vtpk file. Among other things, file size is influenced by the number of fonts downloaded from the service. Esri vector tiled basemap layers use fonts as part of their style resources - see vector-tile-style documentation. When you take a vector tiled layer offline, these fonts are downloaded as part of the .

Esri services also offer an alternative version with a reduced font package size. This omits the large Arial Unicode font, which greatly reduces the download size. You can view the set of reduced font vector tile services here.

If you choose , the full set of resources used by the original online service, including all fonts, will be downloaded. This increases the download size of the but ensures that the offline layer will display the same as the original online data.

If you choose , the smaller set of fonts used by the alternative, for export, version of the service will be downloaded. This reduces the download size of the but is not suitable for all locales or when your offline vector tiled layer includes certain language characters. For example, this option will not display Chinese, Japanese, Thai, Korean or Georgian characters. If an alternative, for export version of the service cannot be found, the original service will be used.
The default value is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.IncludeBasemap
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not a basemap will be included in the offline map.

remarks.para: If you do not want a basemap in the offline map then set this property to false. After loading the offline map, your application can programmatically insert a basemap into the map. Note that, a programmatically inserted basemap will not be persisted in the map.
If you want a basemap in the offline map then set this property to true. You can choose to either:

list.item: Generate and download the online basemap using the . This is the default.
Use a local basemap on the device by setting the .

remarks.para: The default value is true.
This property is superseded if the is used in conjunction with .

member.value: true if the basemap will be included in the offline map; false otherwise.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.IsDefinitionExpressionFilterEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether feature layer definition expressions are used when taking feature layers and tables offline.

remarks.para: A value of true allows to reuse the SQL where clause in as a filter when generating offline geodatabases.
Applying the definition expression may reduce the number of features taken offline for display and sync.
If the value is false this could result in a larger geodatabase than is required to display the feature layer.
The default value is true.
For tables the definition expression is taken from the property .
If is used in conjunction with this property is superseded.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GenerateOfflineMapParameters.UpdateMode
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating how the offline map will support synchronization with online services.

remarks.para: A value of instructs the to create offline geodatabases that support syncing with online feature services.

A value of instructs the to disable data synchronization for generated offline geodatabases. In this case, no synchronization replicas will be created on corresponding feature services. This reduces the load on the feature server and frees the developer from needing to unregister server replicas when they are no longer needed.
The default value is .
If is used in conjunction with this property is superseded by the property of the overridden layer.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GeodatabaseDeltaInfo
member.summary: Represents the delta files created to synchronize changes to a .

remarks.para: A delta geodatabase is a file that contains the changes that have occurred since a mobile replica was last synchronized with its feature service. See the document Synchronize Replica for an overview of the delta files used in synchronization.
There are two types of delta geodatabase:

list.item: Local edits, performed on the user's device, are exported as an "upload" delta that is applied to the originating feature service.
Online edits, performed by other users are requested as a "download" delta which is then applied to the local replica geodatabase.

remarks.para: Delta geodatabases allow you to troubleshoot sync problems. For example, you can inspect the geodatabase changes or you could send the file to the administrator of the feature service.
To access the after a sync has completed, set one of the following properties to true:

list.item: . This will populate the property.
. This will add instances to the property.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GeodatabaseDeltaInfo.DownloadDeltaPath
member.summary: Gets the full file path of the downloaded delta file, including the file name and extension.
member.remarks: This property is the full path to the download delta geodatabase downloaded from the server during a bidirectional or download sync. The file will be saved to the user's temp directory and will be subject to the system's policy for cleanup of temporary files. This property will be null if there were no server changes to download or the sync direction is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GeodatabaseDeltaInfo.FeatureServiceUri
member.summary: Gets the feature service that is used to sync changes to the geodatabase specified by .

member.summary: Gets the file path to the that is used to sync changes, including the file name and extension.
member.remarks: The path refers to a .geodatabase file that is used to sync changes to the service specified by . Each sync process can create an "upload" delta file ( ) and/or a "download" delta file ( ).

member.summary: Gets the full file path of the generated upload delta file, including the file name and extension.
member.remarks: This property is the full path to the upload delta geodatabase created from the local during a bidirectional or upload sync. The file will be saved to the user's temp directory and will be subject to the system's policy for cleanup of temporary files. This property will be null if there were no changes to upload or the sync direction is only.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GeodatabaseSyncTask
member.summary: A task to generate and synchronize a mobile geodatabase with a sync-enabled ArcGIS feature service.

remarks.para: Mobile geodatabases are designed for querying and viewing feature data when your app is offline. While offline, users can edit a mobile geodatabase locally and later synchronize changes with its feature service, assuming the associated service supports synchronization. While offline, the geodatabase keeps track of all local edits. When you synchronize the geodatabase with the service, the service receives the geodatabase's local edits, and the geodatabase receives the service's remote edits.

This task allows you to build apps that can download data from an ArcGIS feature service that has been enabled for offline use into a geodatabase. Here are the typical steps to download data from sync-enabled ArcGIS feature service into a geodatabase file on your device:
Construct and load the with a URL to a sync-enabled ArcGIS feature service.
Create a to define the area of interest that you wish to take offline.
Obtain the for the area of interest by calling .
Create a by calling . Specify a path where the mobile geodatabase (.geodatabase file) is downloaded to.
Start the . Upon job completion, obtain the from .

The geodatabase may contain feature layers, annotation layers, related records, non-spatial tables, and attachments. You can create, update, or delete this data according to the capabilities of the source feature service. The geodatabase is registered with the online feature service so that you can synchronize their data when a network connection to the feature service is available. Your app business logic will determine whether the synchronization just downloads changes from the feature service (), just uploads changes made to the geodatabase (), or uploads and downloads changes (). Here are the typical steps to synchronize data between the geodatabase file and the source feature service:
Create a with the .
Obtain the for the geodatabase by calling .
Create a by calling ). Specify the synchronization direction.
Start the . Upon job completion, examine the from to determine whether the synchronization was successful.

When the geodatabase is no longer needed, you should unregister it to clean up the tracking metadata stored on the feature service. Unregistered geodatabases cannot be re-registered. Here are the steps to unregister a geodatabase:
Create and load a from the downloaded geodatabase file using .
Examine to check that the geodatabase is sync-enabled. If this is false, no further action is needed.
Create a with the .
Unregister the geodatabase using .

There is another option for working with mobile geodatabases. You can place a copy of a sync-enabled geodatabase onto your device and register it with the associated service using . This will give the geodatabase its own replica ID and you will be able to synchronize the geodatabase with its service.

If you want to download a portion of a web map and its content, instead of individual ArcGIS feature services, consider using the . You can then synchronize all of the map's geodatabase content together using the .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GeodatabaseSyncTask.ExportDeltaAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Data.Geodatabase,System.String)
member.summary: Generates an upload delta geodatabase file containing all the changes since the last sync.

remarks.para: A delta geodatabase contains the changes that have occurred since a mobile replica was last synchronized with the feature service. See the document Synchronize Replica for an overview of the delta files used in synchronization. An "upload" delta geodatabase contains the local edits, performed on the user's device, to be sent to the originating feature service.
When you run this task, any local edits in the will be exported to the specified output path. The output path should include the full path and file name (ending in ".geodatabase") for the export.
The delta file created by this method is not sent to the server and has no impact on any subsequent sync job. You can call this method repeatedly without affecting the state of the local geodatabase.
The task returned by this method has a boolean result that indicates whether or not a delta geodatabase was created.
You should not execute more than one sync on a geodatabase at the same time. This includes any operations that export/import deltas from the local :

member.param: The geodatabase to generate a delta from.
The path and filename to save the generated delta to.
member.returns: A task that generates a geodatabase delta and has a result type of bool. This bool indicates whether a delta was generated or not.

member.summary: Creates a job to synchronize a geodatabase back to a service.

remarks.para: For a successfully completed job, the job's result returns an collection of , typically the collection is empty. If individual edits failed then the result collection provides these errors grouped by each table using instances which in turn contain each edit error.
You should not execute more than one sync on a geodatabase at the same time. This includes any operations that export/import deltas from the local :
An exception will be thrown if there is an active transaction on the geodatabase during sync.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GeodatabaseSyncTask.SyncGeodatabase(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.SyncDirection,System.Boolean,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Data.Geodatabase)
member.summary: Creates a job to synchronize a geodatabase back to its source ArcGIS feature service using the given sync direction and rollback on failure boolean value.

remarks.para: For a successfully completed job, the job's result returns a collection of , typically the collection is empty. If individual edits failed then the result collection provides these errors grouped by each table using instances which in turn contain each edit error.
If the given sync direction is not compatible with the geodatabase sync task's feature service, the returned job will fail.
You should not execute more than one sync on a geodatabase at the same time. This includes any operations that export/import deltas from the local :
An exception will also be thrown if there is an active transaction on the geodatabase during sync.

true to rollback all changes to the service and/or the geodatabase if the job fails, false to accept any of the changes up until the point when the job fails.

For a geodatabase with a SyncModel of , all geodatabase layers are included. A service is editable if it has capabilities that include any of create, update or delete. In this case both and are supported sync directions. Alternatively, a service that does not have create, update or delete capability is considered read-only and only will be supported. member.remarks: After unregistering a geodatabase it can no longer be synced back to the service. This is often used prior to deleting a local geodatabase. This operation is not related to , which is for registering copies of a sync-enabled geodatabase.

member.summary: Unregisters a geodatabase from a service removing the service's replica id. This overload uses the geodatabase's sync ID rather than a reference to the geodatabase itself.
member.remarks: This method is used to unregister a geodatabase from a service using the sync ID. This is commonly used to enable cleanup of the service after the local geodatabase has already been deleted. See . After unregistering a geodatabase it can no longer be synced back to the service. This operation is not related to , which is for registering copies of a sync-enabled geodatabase.
member.param: The sync ID of the geodatabase to unregister.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous unregister geodatabase operation.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.GeodatabaseSyncTask.RegisterGeodatabaseAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Data.Geodatabase)
member.summary: Registers a copy of a sync-enabled geodatabase with an ArcGIS feature service to allow the copy to synchronize changes.
member.remarks: This method is typically used as part of services pattern workflow, sometimes known as a pre-planned workflow. A sync-enabled geodatabase is generated centrally and not modified or synced. Copies of this original geodatabase are distributed and loaded onto each user's device. The copy must first be registered with the service to allow the server to identify changes in subsequent sync operations. One of the main benefits of this workflow is a reduction in server load to generate geodatabases for many clients. It is important not to sync the copy before registering to ensure the server can maintain consistent state on each copy. This operation is not the opposite of which is used to remove a geodatabase from the service prior to deletion.
member.param: a geodatabase to register.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous register operation.

A "download" delta geodatabase contains the online edits which will be imported to the local geodatabase.
The task returned by this method returns an array of .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapSyncParameters.KeepGeodatabaseDeltas
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the upload or downloaded delta geodatabases are retained or removed at the end of the sync job.

remarks.para: A delta geodatabase is a file that contains the changes that have occurred since a mobile replica was last synchronized with its feature service. See the document Synchronize Replica for an overview of the delta files used in synchronization.
There are two types of delta geodatabase:

list.item: Local edits, performed on the user's device, are exported as an "upload" delta that is applied to the originating feature service.
Online edits, performed by other users are requested as a "download" delta which is then applied to the local replica geodatabase.

remarks.para: Delta geodatabases allow you to troubleshoot sync problems. For example, you can inspect the geodatabase changes or you could send the file to the administrator of the feature service.

You can choose to retain both the uploaded and downloaded delta geodatabases once the sync job has completed. Set to true to retain both delta geodatabases and set to false to have them deleted at the end of the sync job. Deltas will be retained regardless of whether the job succeeds or fails. Note that when this property is set to true, the upload delta geodatabase will only be available if there are changes to upload and the sync direction is or . The download delta geodatabase will only be available when there are changes to download and the sync direction is or . The default value is false.
No geodatabase deltas will be retained if your geodatabase uses a scheduled updates workflow.

member.value: true if the upload or downloaded delta geodatabases are removed at the end of the sync job, otherwise false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapSyncParameters.PreplannedScheduledUpdatesOption
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that determines whether scheduled updates will be downloaded from an online map area and applied to the map's data.

remarks.para: If the mobile geodatabases in your offline map were not registered for sync you can choose to download and apply those updates by setting this property to . You specify whether an offline map will make use of scheduled updates by setting the property before downloading.
The default value is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapSyncParameters.RollbackOnFailure

member.remarks: The default is false (no rollback on failure).

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapSyncParameters.SyncDirection

remarks.para: This property applies to any geodatabases that are registered for feature service synchronization.
It does not apply to geodatabases that use scheduled updates.
The default value is synchronization.
If you select , any download only geodatabases (e.g. with = false) will not be updated and you will receive an error in the associated .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapSyncResult.IsMobileMapPackageReopenRequired
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the mobile map package must be closed and reopened with a new instance to allow the updates to take effect.

remarks.para: In some cases, applying updates may require files such as mobile geodatabases to be replaced with a new version. When reopen is required, this property will be true and you need to:

list.item: Release all instances of the and its maps and layers. For example, you should remove any instances from the MapView and remove layers and geodatabases from custom views such as lists and tables of contents. This will allow files to be closed.
Call to close the mobile map package instance and files.
Create a new instance with the same path.
Load the new . This will apply updates to geodatabases that have been replaced via a scheduled updates workflow.

remarks.para: If your offline map was created using , be aware that immediately following replacement, the new geodatabase will not contain the new features that have been synced up to the feature service. The uploaded features can be downloaded after the next scheduled update has executed and changes are available for download.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapSyncTask
member.summary: A task to synchronize an offline map's geodatabases with its originating sync-enabled ArcGIS feature services.

remarks.para: You can take a map offline from a web map using the . This can be an offline map created ahead-of-time or an on-demand map. For more information, see the Ahead-of-time vs on-demand workflows. The offline map is stored on the device so its data can be displayed, queried, and edited whilst the device is offline.

You can update the data content by synchronizing the offline map with its source web map. Edits to offline feature data can be posted to the source web map, and any edits to the source web map's feature data content can be downloaded and applied to the offline map. This brings the source web map content and the offline map content into alignment. Edits to features, non-spatial data, related data, and attachments can be synchronized. A network connection must be present for synchronization. Here are typical steps to synchronize data between the offline map and its source web map:
Create a with the offline map ().
Obtain the for the offline map by calling . Optionally modify these (for example, to specify a synchronization direction).
Create a by calling .
Start the . Upon job completion, examine the from to determine whether the synchronization was successful.

If there are attribute or geometry level conflicts on a feature during synchronization, the most recently synchronized edit will be applied. For example, if both user A and user B edit the same feature while offline, if user A synchronizes their edits first, then user B synchronizes their edits, the updated feature will represent the edits made by user B.

Synchronization errors typically occur because of network connectivity issues during the sync process. The synchronization mechanism is robust to these types of errors, however, and they can be resolved by synchronizing again when a reliable network connection becomes available.

If you created the offline map using the ahead-of-time workflow, it may support a synchronization workflow known as update packages. Update packages optimize updating the data contents of a downloaded offline map to the latest contents of the source web map. For more information, see Update packages.
If you want to download and synchronize individual feature services to a single offline geodatabase, instead of taking a map offline, then you can use the .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapSyncTask.CheckForUpdatesAsync
member.summary: Returns a for the offline map that was used to construct this task.

remarks.para: The returned provides high level information on what updates are available for this offline map. Information is provided on:

list.item: Online changes that can be applied to update your offline map.
Local changes from your offline map that can be sent back to the online services.

remarks.para: Calling this method provides high-level information on the available updates. It can help you to determine whether to call immediately, based upon factors such as current disk space and network availability. Examine these properties before starting the potentially time-consuming offline map sync process.
The resulting provides a snap-shot of available updates when this method was called. To check for new updates you need to call this method again.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapTask
member.summary: A task used to take a map offline.

remarks.para: Taking a map offline involves downloading an online map and its data, including all the supported layers, tables, renderers, web map configuration etc. so that it can be used offline without a network connection. There are two workflows available for taking maps offline:

list.item: The ahead-of-time workflow (also sometimes referred to as the preplanned workflow).
The on-demand workflow.

remarks.para: Ahead-of-time workflow. In this workflow the author of a web map defines geographical areas (offline map areas) to take offline.ArcGIS Online, or ArcGIS Enterprise, uses these to prepare offline map content that is stored online. Download the offline map content to a device using the . Note that this API refers to an offline map area as a . For more information about authoring offline map areas, see Take web maps offline.

On-demand workflow. Here the app passes a specific area of interest to a to generate and download the map content to the device. Using this workflow, you can choose to keep online only services (e.g. those that require a network connection) in your offline map. Be aware that an offline map which uses online only services will require a network connection and may require authentication.

member.summary: Returns a of created from the specified area of interest.

remarks.para: The supported geometry types for the area of interest are and . The area of interest must have a spatial reference.

Where a is supplied, features and tiles will be filtered according to the polygon geometry, which can help reduce the size of the resulting offline map. Note that the filtered set of tiles may vary, depending on the underlying service.
The returned parameters min scale and max scale are zero so data will be taken at the full range of scales the services support.
The returned has its property initialized from the offline map task's portal item, if that is set.
The ItemInfo's thumbnail will be copied from the portal item's thumbnail.
The default parameters will be populated using the values in the online map's (if present).

If the map being taken offline contains a and is in an ArcGIS Enterprise 11.1 or later service, the Offline Capability selected for the such as "Trace utility network features" will automatically be reflected in the default parameters created by this method. If the map being taken offline contains a and is in an earlier version of ArcGIS Enterprise, the property is set to for the containing the . Use to change the if desired.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapUpdateCapabilities
member.summary: Describes supported methods for obtaining updates for a preplanned map area.
member.remarks: You can identify the supported methods by creating an with the offline map () and examining the . You can determine whether the offline map is configured to use the update packages () and to sync directly with feature services ().

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapUpdateCapabilities.SupportsScheduledUpdatesForFeatures
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether a preplanned map area supports downloading of read-only scheduled update packages.

remarks.para: If this property is true, updates are generated whenever the online map area is refreshed. For example, the offline map area could be refreshed according to its update schedule. For this approach the web map author must configure the online map to perform scheduled updates.

Updates are prepared once and cached for download by all users of the offline map area. Sharing a single set of cached updates, rather than performing individual sync operations, reduces the load on the back-end services. This approach is scalable for large deployments. As updates are only downloaded, this approach can only be used with read-only workflows. The updates reflect a snapshot of the feature data at the time the web map's offline map area was refreshed (for example, according to the update schedule). They do not reflect the most up-to-date feature data.
If this property is false, no updates will be available for download.

member.value: true if an offline map supports downloading of read-only scheduled update packages, otherwise false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapUpdateCapabilities.SupportsSyncWithFeatureServices
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether an offline map references feature services that are sync enabled.

remarks.para: This type provides high level information on what updates are available for an offline map. Update information covers both:

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapUpdatesInfo.DownloadAvailability
member.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether there are changes available to download.

remarks.para: If this property is then there are online updates to apply to your offline map.
If this property is then there are no online updates available for your offline map.

Note that the availability of updates to download can only be determined for offline maps that use the scheduled updates workflow. See and . If your offline map is set up to download changes directly from the feature services (for example using or created with a ), then this property will be .

member.value: Indicates whether there are changes available to download.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapUpdatesInfo.IsMobileMapPackageReopenRequired
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the mobile map package must be reopened after applying the available updates.

remarks.para: In some cases, applying updates may require files, such as mobile geodatabases, to be replaced with a new version.

If your offline map was created using , be aware that immediately following replacement, the new geodatabase will not contain the new features that have been synced up to the feature service. The uploaded features can be downloaded after the next scheduled update has executed and changes are available for download.

member.value: true if reopening is required after update; otherwise, false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapUpdatesInfo.ScheduledUpdatesDownloadSize
member.summary: Gets the total size in bytes of update files to download for a scheduled updates workflow.

remarks.para: The scheduled updates workflow allows read-only updates to be stored with the online map area and downloaded to your device at a later time. Updates can cover several sets of changes to the online geodatabase and can cover multiple geodatabases in an offline map. This property is the total download size of all files required to update your offline map.
You can use this information to determine whether you want to download updates immediately - for example based on available disk space or network availability.
If there are no updates available, or your offline map does not use a scheduled updates workflow, this property will be 0.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineMapUpdatesInfo.UploadAvailability
member.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether there are local changes to upload.

remarks.para: If your offline map contains local edits that can be uploaded to online feature services, this property will be .
If there are no local changes, or your offline map does not support syncing with feature services, this property will be .

member.summary: Enumerates whether offline data has updates, has no updates, or that the availability of updates cannot be determined.

member.summary: It is not possible to determine whether updates are available, for example, because the operation is not supported.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineUpdateAvailability.Available
member.summary: There are updates available.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OfflineUpdateAvailability.None

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OnlineOnlyServicesOption
member.summary: Enumerates the possible options for dealing with online-only services (those that cannot be taken offline).

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OnlineOnlyServicesOption.Exclude
member.summary: Online layers and tables that cannot be taken offline will be excluded when taking a map offline.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OnlineOnlyServicesOption.Include
member.summary: Online layers and tables that cannot be taken offline will be included when taking a map offline and continue to reference the online service.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.OnlineOnlyServicesOption.UseAuthoredSettings
member.summary: A given layer or table will be taken offline, included as online content, or excluded according to the settings in the web map.

remarks.para: When a web map is created, the author has the option to pre-select which layers should remain online. This can include:

list.item: Choosing to leave offline-enabled layers online (for example because they contain data which is frequently updated).
Choosing whether online only layers should be included in the offline map or not.

remarks.para: If no settings are provided in the web map, the default behavior will omit any online only layers or tables from the offline map. This matches the option.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.PreplannedMapArea.PackagingStatus
member.summary: Gets a value that describes the packaging status of the online map area.

remarks.para: This property allows you to check whether an online is in a state and ready to be downloaded.
If you attempt to load a that is not the will fail to load with a .
There are four possible values:

list.item: indicates the map area is ready for download and the data has been packaged.
indicates that the online map area is not yet ready to be downloaded. The packages required for the map area have not been packaged yet. This status will be available after the fails to load.

indicates that an error was encountered while preparing the online map area or its packages. In this scenario, the map area cannot be downloaded until the web map author has resolved the errors. This status will be available after the fails to load.
indicates that the metadata for the map area has not been fetched. This will be the value when the map area is .

remarks.para: If the packaging status is you can check it again following these steps:

list.item: Create a new instance using the in the .
Create a new using the portal item .
Load the and check .

member.value: A flag that describes the publishing status of the online map area.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.PreplannedMapArea.UpdateCapabilities
member.summary: Gets a value that describes the methods for obtaining updates that are supported for this preplanned map area.

remarks.para: There are two approaches for updating the features in a preplanned offline map area:

list.item: Syncing directly with feature services.
Downloading scheduled updates (or update packages) from the online map area.

remarks.para: Choose the approach that best suits your workflow, then select the appropriate . For more details on how to update an offline map, see the .

If is true, you can sync directly with feature services. This mode gives you full control over when you upload and download changes. However, performing a download sync places significant load on the backing feature service, so this approach does not scale well for very large deployments. Set the to to use this approach.

If is true, you can download read-only update packages from the online map area. Updates are prepared on a regular schedule and cached for download by all users of the offline map area. Sharing a single set of cached updates, rather than performing individual sync operations, reduces the load on the back-end services. This approach is scalable for large deployments but does not support offline editing. Set the to to use this approach.
Note that scheduled updates is an optimization that must be enabled by the web map author when the map area is created.

If both of the above are true, and the feature services in your online map support creating new features, you can adopt a hybrid update model where you download read-only update packages while uploading new features directly to the feature service. This approach combines the scalability of downloading scheduled updates with the ability to collect data in the field. However, note that this approach is strictly add only, meaning that you cannot delete or edit features once they have been uploaded to the feature service. Set the to to use this approach.
This property will return null until the preplanned map area is loaded.

member.value: Describes the methods for obtaining updates that are supported for this preplanned map area.

member.value: A collection of portal items for packages related to this preplanned map area.

member.summary: No feature updates will be performed.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.PreplannedUpdateMode.SyncWithFeatureServices
member.summary: Changes, including local edits, will be synced directly with the underlying feature services.

remarks.para: This mode gives you full control over when you upload and download changes. However, performing a download sync places additional load on the backing feature service, so this approach does not scale well for larger deployments.
This mode is available when the include .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.PreplannedUpdateMode.DownloadScheduledUpdates
member.summary: Scheduled, read-only updates will be downloaded from the online map area and applied to the local mobile geodatabases.

remarks.para: Updates are prepared on a regular schedule and cached for download by all users of the offline map area. Sharing a single set of cached updates, rather than performing individual sync operations, reduces the load on the back-end services. This approach is scalable for large deployments but does not support offline editing.
This mode is available when the include .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.PreplannedUpdateMode.DownloadScheduledUpdatesAndUploadNewFeatures
member.summary: Scheduled, read-only updates are downloaded from the online map area and applied to the local mobile geodatabases. Newly added features can also be uploaded to the feature service.

remarks.para: This is an advanced workflow that improves the scalability of feature services. When available, applicable scheduled update packages are downloaded and merged into a local geodatabase. This allows for efficient downloading of changes. New features are uploaded using feature sync, which is an efficient operation in the feature service.

Note that when the required update packages are not available, the local geodatabase will be replaced with a new copy. indicates that a new copy of a geodatabase will be downloaded. Local adds on your device must be uploaded to the feature service before the database can safely be replaced.

Be aware that immediately following replacement, the new geodatabase may not contain the new features that have been synced up to the feature service, as this relies on a subsequent scheduled update to download to the client. The new features will be retrieved when the next scheduled update is downloaded and applied.
This mode is available when the include both and and the feature services in your map support creating new features.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Offline.SyncGeodatabaseParameters.KeepGeodatabaseDeltas
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the upload or downloaded delta geodatabases will be removed at the end of the sync job.

remarks.para: A delta geodatabase is a file that contains the changes that have occurred since a mobile replica was last synchronized with its feature service. See the document Synchronize Replica for an overview of the delta files used in synchronization.
There are two types of geodatabase deltas:

list.item: Local edits, performed on the user's device, are exported as an "upload" delta that is applied to the originating feature service.
Online edits, performed by other users are requested as a "download" delta which is then applied to the local replica geodatabase.

remarks.para: Delta geodatabases allow you to troubleshoot sync problems. For example, you can inspect the geodatabase changes or you could send the file to the administrator of the feature service.

You can choose to retain both the uploaded and downloaded delta geodatabases once the sync job has completed. Set to true to retain both delta geodatabases and set to false to have them deleted at the end of the sync job. Deltas will be retained regardless of whether the job succeeds or fails. Note that when this property is set to true, the upload delta geodatabase will only be available if there are changes to upload and the sync direction is or . The download delta geodatabase will only be available when there are changes to download and the sync direction is or . The default value is false.

remarks.para: If this is a junction to a non-spatial edge connectivity association, this property represents how far the junction is located along the non-spatial edge, from 0.0 (the edge's start point) and 1.0 (the edge's end point).

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNamedTraceConfiguration
member.summary: A set of predefined trace options that can be used by to simplify configuring and running a trace operation.
member.remarks: Named trace configurations are created in the utility network and allow clients to perform trace analytics without needing to understand the data model. Applications can simply allow the user to choose from a set of named trace configurations that provide tracing capabilities. The set can be configured for different workflows, and a single application can be used by multiple customers and even across multiple problem domains.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetwork.DirtyAreaTable
member.summary: Gets the dirty area table of the .

remarks.para:
A read-only table that can be used to query features that represent either:
a new or modified feature in the utility network that has not yet been validated in the network topology
an error that resulted from enabling or validating network topology or updating subnetworks
A dirty area is created when modifications are made to feature geometry, asset group or asset type fields, network attribute fields, associations, or terminal configuration information.
The dirty area table is null until the utility network is loaded or if the utility network does not support the network state.

member.value: The dirty area table of the .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetwork.GetAssociationsAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityElement,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously retrieves a list of all s in the geodatabase for a given .
member.remarks: The result is a list of all associations; connectivity, containment, and structural attachment associations that include the given instance. The method does not return a complete picture of connectivity; features that are connected by geometric coincidence are not returned. Note that the list returned can contain associations that have not yet been validated and are therefore not yet included in the topological index.
member.param: The whose associations are to be returned.
A with which to cancel the operation if required.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous retrieval of all the s if available. Otherwise, an empty list is returned.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetwork.ValidateNetworkTopology(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Geometry.Envelope)
member.summary: Returns a job that when started will validate the utility network topology within the provided extent.

remarks.para: This is a second overload method for . Given that GeoprocessingExecutionType is not specified, the default is GeoprocessingExecutionType.synchronousExecute.
The job that is returned is dormant and needs to be explicitly started.
When working with an enterprise geodatabase, only a single session can run the validate operation at a time in the default version.
See Enable and validate network topology errors for more information on validation errors.

member.param: The extent of the area to be validated.
member.returns: A to validate network topology.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetwork.ValidateNetworkTopology(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Geometry.Envelope,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Tasks.Geoprocessing.GeoprocessingExecutionType)
member.summary: Returns a job that when started will validate the utility network topology within the provided extent.

remarks.para: The job that is returned is dormant and needs to be explicitly started.
When working with an enterprise geodatabase, only a single session can run the validate operation at a time in the default version.
See Enable and validate network topology errors for more information on validation errors.

member.param: The extent of the area to be validated.

param.name: geoprocessingExecutionType
param.para: A indicating the job execution type. The value passed in must be either or .
Running with of is the preferred execution mode due to this mode being significantly faster to start up. For larger jobs can lead to timeouts, at which point should be used.

member.returns: A to validate network topology.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetwork.CanAddAssociationAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityAssociation)
member.summary: Checks if the utility association can be added to the utility network.

remarks.para:
Checks that all of the following conditions are met:
the utility network is in a valid state and supports the association type.
the specified association only includes elements that are accessible and that have the necessary association role and terminal selection.
a utility rule exists that permits their association.
the current user has the necessary permissions.

member.param: The association to be checked.
member.returns: true if the association can be added to the utility network, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetwork.CanAddAssociationAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityAssociationType,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityElement,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityElement)
member.summary: Checks if a utility association with the specified type between the specified utility elements can be added to the utility network.

remarks.para:
Checks that all of the following conditions are met:
the utility network is in a valid state and supports the association type.
the specified association only includes elements that are accessible and that have the necessary association role and terminal selection.
a utility rule exists that permits their association.
the current user has the necessary permissions.

member.param: The type of association between the and .
The first participant in the association.
The second participant in the association.
member.returns: true if the association can be added to the utility network, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetwork.AddAssociationAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityAssociation)
member.summary: Adds an association between two utility elements in this utility network.

remarks.para: Adding an association may also update the features that participate in the association.
Use to determine if you can successfully add this association.

This method requires that:
The association type is supported by the utility network schema. Note that less than 4 does not support the following association types:
The association elements are accessible. Use to check feature accessibility to ensure it has not been filtered out.
The has the necessary role for the association type. A can participate in a containment association or a can participate in a structural attachment association.
If the association's type is , the must be set for elements with more than one terminal available in the
The association is supported by a utility rule. Use to determine if the can be made between the and using their corresponding .

member.param: The association to add.

returns.para: A that has no return value.

One of the following exceptions may be set on the returned :
, if this method is called on a utility network schema version that does not support the association type.
, if this method is called after the geodatabase is closed.
, when the specified association:
includes an element that is not accessible or does not belong in this utility network.
has a that lacks the necessary role for the association type.
type is connectivity and includes an element without a terminal or its default terminal could not be determined.
is not supported by any utility rule.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetwork.CanDeleteAssociations
member.summary: Checks whether associations can be deleted.

remarks.para:
Checks that all of the following conditions are met:
the utility network is in a valid state and allows editing association.
the current user has the necessary permissions.

member.returns: true if associations can be deleted, false otherwise.

, if this method is called on a utility network schema version that does not support the association type.
, if this method is called after the geodatabase is closed.
, when the specified association includes an element that is not accessible or does not belong in this utility network.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkAttribute
member.summary: An instance of this class represents a network attribute in a Utility Network.

remarks.para: A Network Attribute is an attribute that is copied and stored in the topological index.

The utility network tracing task can read and make decisions using network attributes stored directly in the topological index, without needing to fetch the individual features to get their attributes, the topological index provides performance that is orders of magnitude faster.
The network attributes in a topological index are limited to specific data types and aggregate size.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkAttribute.DataType
member.summary: Gets the for the .
member.remarks: The network attributes in a topological index are limited to specific data types.

member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the network attribute has been added by the system.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkAttribute.Name
member.summary: Gets the network attribute's name.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkAttribute.Domain
member.summary: Gets the associated with this network attribute.
member.remarks: If no domain is assigned, null is returned.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkAttribute.IsApportionable
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the network attribute should be apportioned across the length of linear features.
member.remarks: For example, if a point is chosen 25% of the distance along an edge, 25% of the attribute's value is assigned to the 25% (shorter) part of the edge, and 75% of the attribute's value is assigned to the 75% (longer) part of the edge.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkAttribute.IsSubstitution
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the network attribute is used as a substitution for another network attribute at tap features.
member.remarks: If this value is true, this network attribute is the network attribute to substitute for at tap features. Taps are special features, identified with the "Subnetwork Tap" category, that work with attribute substitution. See the online help for more information.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkAttribute.NetworkAttributeToSubstitute
member.summary: Gets the network attribute that is substituted for this network attribute at tap features.
member.remarks: This property is only set if is set to true. Taps are special features, identified with the "Subnetwork Tap" category, that work with attribute substitution. See the online help for more information.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkCapabilities
member.summary: Defines the supported capabilities for a utility network.

remarks.para: These non-configurable capabilities indicate which utility network operations are supported. They are dependent upon the utility network service capabilities or the contents of the geodatabase.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkCapabilities.SupportsQueryAssociations
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this utility network can query associations.

remarks.para: Access to the system-defined associations table is required to enable querying associations.

The associations table is available for a utility network if:
Utility network feature service grants access to the association table
Partial or full utility network model was requested when replica geodatabase was created
Association table was included with the stand-alone geodatabase exported from ArcGIS Pro

member.value: true if this utility network can query associations, false otherwise.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkCapabilities.SupportsTrace
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this utility network can run a trace.

remarks.para: Access to network topology is required to enable tracing capability.

The network topology is available for a utility network if:
Utility network feature service has enabled network topology
Full utility network model was requested when replica geodatabase was created and the network topology is enabled
Network topology was included and enabled with the stand-alone geodatabase exported from ArcGIS Pro

member.value: true if this utility network can run a trace, false otherwise.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkCapabilities.SupportsNetworkState
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this utility network supports network state.

remarks.para:
The network state is supported for a utility network if:
Connected to a utility network feature service
Full utility network model was requested when replica geodatabase was created
Network topology was included with the stand-alone geodatabase exported from ArcGIS Pro

member.value: true if this utility network supports network state, false otherwise.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkCapabilities.SupportsValidateNetworkTopology
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this utility network can validate network topology.
member.remarks: Access to a feature service that has network topology enabled is required to validate network topology.
member.value: true if this utility network can validate network topology, false otherwise.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkDefinition
member.summary: An instance of this class represents the metadata of a Utility Network.
member.remarks: Provides metadata (e.g domain networks, network sources, and more) about a Utility Network.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkDefinition.Categories
member.summary: Gets the collection of utility network categories in the utility network metadata.
member.remarks: A is used to define a characteristic of an asset in a network. The Instances in this collection incorporate semantics of the utility network for subnetwork management and tracing operations.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkDefinition.NetworkAttributes
member.summary: Gets the network attributes in the utility network definition.
member.remarks: A is an attribute that is copied and stored in the topological index. The utility network tracing task can read and make decisions using network attributes that are stored in the topological index.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkDefinition.NetworkSources
member.summary: Gets the network sources in the utility network definition.
member.remarks: instances represent various sources of network information such as structures, lines, junctions, and associations.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkDefinition.SchemaVersion
member.summary: Gets the utility network schema version number reported by the utility network metadata.
member.remarks: Only certain schema versions are supported by this API.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkDefinition.DomainNetworks
member.summary: Gets the collection of domain networks in the utility network metadata.
member.remarks: Domain networks organize features in a utility network based on what utility service they provide, such as natural gas, water, electricity, or structural elements like poles or conduits.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkDefinition.TerminalConfigurations

member.summary: Gets the representing the service territory area used to define the .
member.remarks: This is the extent of the network topology (i.e., the extent of the service territory plus a small buffer).

member.summary: Queries for a subset of objects that apply to a set of .

remarks.para: Use this method to return network rules applicable to features of specific asset types. Limiting these asset types to those features being edited can substantially reduce the number of network rules returned.
These asset types will be matched against the of the following rule elements:
If the collection of is empty or no matching rules are found for these asset types, an empty collection of objects is returned.

The property contains the collection of instances in the utility network definition.

member.summary: An enumeration of the various usage types of a .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.Device
member.summary: Network devices, such as transformers or valves.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.Junction
member.summary: Miscellaneous junctions not typically tracked as assets, such as taps.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.Line
member.summary: Lines such as conductors, pipes, or fiber strands.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.Assembly
member.summary: Assemblies such as switchgear.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.SubnetLine
member.summary: System-generated subnetwork lines.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.StructureJunction
member.summary: Point structures, such as poles.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.StructureLine
member.summary: Linear structures, such as ducts, conduits, or buffer tubes.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.StructureBoundary
member.summary: Polygonal structures, such as substations or town border stations.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.JunctionObject
member.summary: Non-spatial junctions, such as a transceiver in a fiber network

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.EdgeObject

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.StructureJunctionObject
member.summary: Non-spatial structure junction, such as a duct port in a manhole.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityNetworkSourceUsageType.StructureEdgeObject
member.summary: Non-spatial structure edge, such as a duct in a duct bank.

remarks.para: The propagated values can be tested to allow or disallow further traversal.
In the example of phase propagation, open devices along the network will restrict some phases from continuing along the trace.
Propagators only apply to subnetwork-based traces (, , and so on).
is set in the .

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRule
member.summary: Defines a permissible association between features in a utility network.

remarks.para: Network rules help maintain data integrity. They are imposed at the asset group, asset type, and terminal levels for the entire utility network to manage the correctness of assets in the network by allowing you to select which types of features can be connected or associated. A network rule supporting the type of association between features must exist or these features cannot be associated.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRule.FromElement
member.summary: Gets the first participant in a network rule.
member.remarks: This may describe a container or structure feature, or any feature from which a connection to another feature can be made.
member.value: The first participant in a network rule.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRule.RuleId
member.summary: Gets the ID of this network rule.
member.value: The ID of this network rule.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRule.RuleType
member.summary: Gets the of this network rule.
member.value: The of this network rule.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRule.ToElement
member.summary: Gets the second participant in a network rule.
member.remarks: This may describe a content or attachment feature, a container feature in a nested containment, or any feature from which a connection to another feature can be made.
member.value: The second participant in a network rule.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRule.ViaElement
member.summary: Gets an optional intermediate participant in a network rule.
member.remarks: This is available only in a network rule and is commonly used to describe a junction feature without a terminal.
member.value: An optional intermediate participant in a network rule.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRuleElement
member.summary: Specifies the type of features participating in a network rule.

remarks.para: A rule element describes the type of features to which a network rule is applied.
A rule element participating in a network rule can be one of the following:

Attachment network rules place a structure feature in the and attachment features in the . Containment network rules place a container feature in the and content features in the . If nested containment is allowed, another container feature may be placed in the . Connectivity network rules with a have a junction feature.
Network rules that support terminal connectivity use these rule elements to define which terminal the rule is applicable for.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRuleElement.AssetGroup
member.summary: Gets the asset group that contains the asset type bound by a network rule.
member.value: The asset group that contains the asset type bound by a network rule.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRuleElement.AssetType
member.summary: Gets the asset type bound by a network rule.
member.value: The asset type bound by a network rule.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRuleElement.NetworkSource
member.summary: Gets the network source that contains the asset group and asset type bound by a network rule.
member.value: The network source that contains the asset group and asset type bound by a network rule.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRuleElement.Terminal
member.summary: Gets the terminal of the asset type where a network rule is applicable.
member.remarks: Only applicable to asset types with terminals and network rules that support terminal connectivity.
member.value: The terminal of the asset type where a network rule is applicable.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRuleType
member.summary: An enumeration of the various types of network rules that determine permissible feature associations in a utility network.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRuleType.JunctionJunctionConnectivity
member.summary: A network rule governing connectivity between junction features.

remarks.para: This connectivity network rule constrains the types of junction features that can be connected to another junction feature based on its asset type.
It supports terminal connectivity and affects associations.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRuleType.Containment
member.summary: A network rule governing containment.

remarks.para: Containment network rule constrains the types of features that can be contained in a given container feature based on its asset type.
The of this network rule represents a container and the represents a content or another container if nested containment is allowed.
It does not support terminal connectivity and affects associations.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRuleType.Attachment
member.summary: A network rule governing structural attachment.

remarks.para: Structural attachment network rule constrains the types of features that can be attached to a given structure feature based on its asset type.
The of this network rule represents a structure and the represents the attachments.
It does not support terminal connectivity and affects associations.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRuleType.JunctionEdgeConnectivity
member.summary: A network rule governing line-end connectivity to a device or junction.

remarks.para: This connectivity network rule constrains the types of junction features that can be connected to edge features.
The of this network rule represents a junction and the represents an edge.

It supports terminal connectivity and affects the following associations:
, which allows a junction to be connected to the end point of an edge
, which allows a junction to be connected along the midspan of an edge
, which allows a junction to be connected to the start point of an edge

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityRuleType.EdgeJunctionEdgeConnectivity
member.summary: A network rule governing connectivity between two edges and a junction.
member.remarks: This connectivity network rule constrains the types of edge features that can be connected using an intermediate junction feature.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilitySubnetworkControllerType.Sink

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTier
member.summary: Tiers demarcate a logical level within a network.
member.remarks: For example, in an electric distribution network, there may be Subtransmission, Medium Voltage and Low Voltage tiers.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTier.Name
member.summary: Gets the name of the tier.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTier.Rank
member.summary: Gets the numeric rank of the tier.
member.remarks: It is possible for several tiers to have the same numeric rank. For example, in an electric distribution network, Radial Medium Voltage and Radial Multifeed Medium Voltage tiers would have the same rank. When doing a trace between a source tier and a target tier, the source and target tiers define a upper and lower tier value range for a trace. The trace will stop if it encounters a tier whose rank is outside the range specified by the source and target tiers.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTierGroup
member.summary: Tier groups provide an extra level of organization for tiers.
member.remarks: For example, a gas network may be divided into two tier groups - Transmission and Distribution. Each of these tier groups would contain a set of tiers specific to that group. For example, Distribution Pressure and Distribution Isolation might be tiers within the Distribution tier group.

member.summary: Specifies the type of topology that makes up a definition.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTierTopologyType.Radial
member.summary: A radial network. Edges and junctions spread out radially from a single source.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTierTopologyType.Mesh
member.summary: A mesh network. Edges and junctions are connected to multiple sources in an interconnected lattice.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTierType
member.summary: Describes the tier type of a domain network.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTierType.Hierarchical
member.summary: Tiers within this domain network are hierarchical.
member.remarks: In hierarchical domain networks, tiers are nested within one another, so features existing in subnetworks for a lower tier naturally participate in all higher tiers. For example, in a gas network, a valve isolation zone is within a pressure zone, which in turn exists within a system zone. A feature in the isolation zone also exists in the pressure zone and in the system zone.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTierType.Partitioned
member.summary: Tiers within this domain network are partitioned.
member.remarks: Features in partitioned domain networks only exist in one tier. The relationship between tiers is ordered and linear. Features can exist in one or multiple subnetworks within one tier.
