member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.IgnoreBarriersAtStartingPoints
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to ignore barriers at starting points.
member.remarks: The default is false. One example of how this can be used is with an upstream protective device trace. The first call to the trace will return the first upstream device that meets the protective device criteria. To find the next device upstream, you would set a starting point on the device returned by the first trace. With set to false, this second trace would immediately stop at the starting point, since it meets the trace termination criteria. Setting this option to true will allow the trace to ignore the starting point, and continue to the subsequent device upstream.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.IncludeBarriers
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include barriers in the trace results or subnetwork.
member.remarks: The default is true.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.IncludeContainers
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include containment features in the trace results or subnetwork.
member.remarks: The default is false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.IncludeContent
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include content in the trace results or subnetwork.
member.remarks: The default is false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.IncludeStructures
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include structure features in the trace results or subnetwork.
member.remarks: The default is false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.ValidateConsistency
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to validate network consistency as part of the trace operation.
member.remarks: If set to true, trace operations will fail if dirty areas are encountered during the trace. The default is true.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.Traversability
member.summary: Gets or sets the traversability conditions for the trace.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.UseDigitizedDirection
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the trace uses the flow direction.

remarks.para: This property is only applicable for upstream and downstream traces in Utility Network versions 7 or higher.

The direction of the flow will be based on the digitized direction of the line, which is determined by the From and To global ID of the edge object in the association, and the Flow direction attribute. To learn more, see Flow direction in a utility network.
When the value is true, the domain network is no longer required, and the source and target tiers are also ignored.
The default value is false.

member.value: true if the trace uses the flow direction, false otherwise.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.DomainNetwork
member.summary: Gets or sets the from which to start the trace.
member.remarks: The property is required and only used with subnetwork-based traces (, and )

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.SourceTier
member.summary: Gets or sets the that is used as the start of the trace.
member.remarks: This property is only used with subnetwork-based traces (, and ) If represents a partitioned network, this property is optional. If not null, the Trace routines will perform an additional check to validate that the starting points and barriers belong to this tier. If represents a hierarchical network, this property is required. Since rows in hierarchical networks can belong to multiple tiers, this property tells the subnetwork tracer which tier to use for tracing.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.TargetTier
member.summary: Gets or sets the that is used to constrain the tiers returned by the and traces.
member.remarks: This property is optional. If null, the upstream and downstream traces will stop in the current tier. If a target tier is specified, the trace will continue upstream or downstream into the specified tier (inclusive).

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.Propagators
member.summary: Gets a collection of objects to execute while performing the trace.
member.remarks: Propagator objects allow a subset of values to propagate through a network while executing a trace. For example, in phase propagation, open devices along the network will restrict some phases from continuing along the trace. Propagators only apply to subnetwork-based traces. (, , and ).

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.Filter
member.summary: Gets or sets the criteria that define the dynamic barriers for the trace.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.Functions
member.summary: Gets a collection of objects to compute while performing the trace.
member.remarks: Each returns a in the .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.IncludeIsolatedFeatures
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include isolated features in the trace results when used in conjunction with an isolation trace.
member.remarks: The default is false.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.ShortestPathNetworkAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets the used to calculate the shortest path.
member.remarks: The shortest path may depend on some other numeric weight, such as cost or difficulty, rather than the shortest distance calculated from shape length.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.OutputAssetTypes
member.summary: Gets a collection of objects which specifies what to include in the trace results.
member.remarks: Only elements that belong to this are included in the trace result. This property is independent of asset types in the . This is applied after all other tracing calculations, including traversal, filters, and function calculation. If this is empty and is null, all elements are returned. Otherwise, elements which match either of these criteria are returned.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.OutputCondition
member.summary: Gets or sets a condition object which specifies what to include in the trace results.
member.remarks: Only elements that satisfy this are included in the trace result. This condition may perform comparisons against network attributes () and categories (), or both (, ). This property is independent of barriers in the or . This is applied after all other tracing calculations, including traversal, filters, and function calculation. If this is null and is empty, all elements are returned. Otherwise, elements which match either of these criteria are returned.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.AllowIndeterminateFlow
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to allow tracing across network features with indeterminate flow or false to stop the traversal when indeterminate flow is encountered.

remarks.para: Indeterminate flow can occur when there is a loop in the network where traversal in either direction could be used to find the subnetwork controller (both upstream and downstream at the same time, in other words). This property is useful for upstream, downstream, or isolation traces where flow direction for the subnetwork is determined. This option is only applicable with Utility Network versions 5 or higher. The default is true.

member.value: true to allow tracing across network features with indeterminate flow or false to stop the traversal when indeterminate flow is encountered.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.IncludeUpToFirstSpatialContainer
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include containment associations up to and including the first spatial container or false to include all containment associations.

remarks.para: This property depends on the property and is ignored if it's false. If no spatial containers are encountered but non-spatial containers are present for a given network element, all non-spatial containers are included in the results. This is typically used with telecommunications networks with multiple levels of containment with non-spatial objects (for example, splice points within a splice tray within a splice container). The default is false.

member.value: true to include containment associations up to and including the first spatial container or false to include all containment associations.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceConfiguration.ValidateLocatability
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to fail trace operations if non-spatial objects are encountered without the necessary containment, attachment, or connectivity association to a spatial object, or false to allow tracing of those non-spatial objects.

remarks.para: Locatability is determined by whether a non-spatial object has implied geometry. Geometry is implied for non-spatial objects if they are associated via containment (possibly multiple levels) with spatial features. This is typically used with telecommunications networks with many non-spatial objects. For example, if a splice tray (non-spatial junction object) is contained within a splice container (assembly feature), then it would be considered locatable. This property is only applicable for Utility Network versions 4 or later. The default is false.

member.value: true to fail trace operations if non-spatial objects are encountered without the necessary containment, attachment, or connectivity association to a spatial object, or false to allow tracing of those non-spatial objects.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceFilter
member.summary: A mechanism to stop tracing when returning results. objects do not stop traversability to the controller.

remarks.para: Consider an upstream protective device trace. At first, you might try defining a that looks for a Protective Device category and assigning this to . When you try to run an upstream trace using this configuration, it will probably fail. This is because traversability will stop at the first protective device, and the trace will be unable to find the subnetwork controller. The correct way to implement this trace is to assign to the filter.
is set in the .

member.summary: Gets the traceParameters' barriers.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceParameters.FilterBarriers
member.summary: Gets a collection of filter barriers.

remarks.para: A regular barrier, specified by , prevents traversal which, when used with subnetwork-based traces, can prevent traversal to the subnetwork controller. Like , filter barriers are evaluated in the second pass of a trace. Therefore, it can restrict the result set without impacting the ability to find subnetwork controllers from starting points.

One example of where filter barriers can be used is in the gas distribution domain. Filter barriers can be used to simulate the location of a squeeze-off when executing a valve isolation trace. The user can place a filter barrier at the proposed location of the squeeze-off and then verify its impact using a trace.

member.summary: An enumeration of the various types of traces that may be performed with a utility network.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceType.Connected
member.summary: A connected trace.
member.remarks: Finds all features connected to a given set of starting points, ignoring traversability.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceType.Subnetwork
member.summary: A subnetwork trace.
member.remarks: Finds all features within the current subnetwork.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceType.Upstream
member.summary: An upstream trace.
member.remarks: For subnetworks with sources, upstream is defined as paths that lead to a source. For subnetworks with sinks, upstream is defined as paths that do not lead to a sink.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceType.Downstream
member.summary: A downstream trace.
member.remarks: For subnetworks with sources, downstream is defined as paths that do not lead to a source. For subnetworks with sinks, downstream is defined as paths that lead to a sink.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceType.Isolation
member.summary: An isolation trace.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceType.Loops
member.summary: Detects loops in the network.
member.remarks: The loop trace finds areas of the network where flow direction is ambiguous where resource can flow in either direction. Loops are expected with mesh networks but usually indicate error conditions in radial networks.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UtilityNetworks.UtilityTraceType.ShortestPath
member.summary: Discovers the shortest path.
member.remarks: This trace returns the shortest path based on cost or distance, which is calculated using a numeric network attribute such as shape length.
